abandonner.of: Kar chascun jur de mort [il] s'abandunet.
abandonner.tr: For each day he gives himself up to death.

abandonner.of: Franceis murrunt, si a nus s'abandunent;
abandonner.tr: The French will die, if they engage with us;
Holy paradise is granted to you;

abandonner.of: Brochet le bien, le frein li abandunet,
abandonner.tr: He spurs it hard, gives it free rein,

abattre.of: Od ses cadables les turs en abatied.
abattre.tr: With his siege engines he beats down its towers.

abattre.of: Pleine sa hanste del cheval l'abat mort,
abattre.tr: With his lance's full length casts him dead from the horse,

abattre.of: Pleine sa hanste l'abat mort des arçuns;
abattre.tr: With his lance full-length he hurls him dead from the saddle;

abattre.of: Pleine sa hanste l'abat mort el chemin.
abattre.tr: With his lance at full length he strikes him down dead on the road.

abattre.of: Que mort l'abat el camp, pleine sa hanste.
abattre.tr: So that he hurls him dead to the ground, his lance at full length.

abattre.of: Que l'abat [mort], qui qu'en peist u qui nun,
abattre.tr: So that he strikes him down [dead], whoever may be grieved by it or not,

abattre.of: Pleine sa hanste l'abat mort de la sele;
abattre.tr: With the full length of his lance knocks him dead from the saddle;

abattre.of: Que mort l'abat de sun cheval curant.
abattre.tr: So that he strikes him dead from his swift horse.

abattre.of: Que mort l'abat entre mil Sarrazins.
abattre.tr: So that he strikes him dead among a thousand Saracens.

abattre.of: La hanste fruisset, mie n'en a(d)[b]atiet.
abattre.tr: The shaft splinters, none of it is struck down.

abattre.of: Tut abat mort el pred sur l'erbe drue;
abattre.tr: He strikes him all dead on the meadow on the thick grass;

abattre.of: Tut abat mort devant loi en la pree.
abattre.tr: He fells him dead before himself on the meadow.

abattre.of: Que mort l'abat en une voide place.
abattre.tr: So that he strikes him dead in an empty space.

abattre.of: Pleine sa hanste l'abat mort des arçuns:
abattre.tr: With his lance at full length he strikes him dead from the saddle-bows:

abattre.of: Que mort l'abat desur le herbe verte.
abattre.tr: So that he casts him dead upon the green grass.

abattre.of: Que mort l'abat en une halte roche.
abattre.tr: So that he strikes him dead upon a high rock.

abattre.of: Il l'abat mort; paien en unt grant joie.
abattre.tr: He strikes him dead; the pagans have great joy of it.

abattre.of: Que mort l'abat seinz altre descunfisun;
abattre.tr: so that he strikes him dead without any further undoing;

abattre.of: Brandist sun colp, si l'ad mort abatut,
abattre.tr: He brandishes his blow, and has struck him dead,

absoudre.of: De sa main destre l'ad asols e seignet,
absoudre.tr: With his right hand he absolved and blessed him,

absoudre.of: Asoldrai vos pur voz anmes guarir.
absoudre.tr: I shall absolve you to save your souls.

absoudre.of: Ben sunt asols e quites de lur pecchez,
absoudre.tr: They are well absolved and released from their sins,

accomplir.tr: I will fulfill your command.» AOI.

accorder.of: Par voz saveirs sem puez acorder,
accorder.tr: By your wisdom, if you can bring me to an accord,

accueillir.of: Sis aquillit e tempeste e ored:
accueillir.tr: A storm and a tempest seized them:

acheminer.of: Entret en sa veie, si s'est achiminez. AOI.
acheminer.tr: He sets out on his way, and thus has started off. AOI.

acheminer.of: Vers dulce France tuit sunt achiminez. AOI.
acheminer.tr: Toward sweet France they have all set out. AOI.

acquitter.of: Se de mun cors voeil aquiter la vie,
acquitter.tr: If I wish to ransom the life of my body,

acquitter.of: De tute Espaigne aquiterai les pans
acquitter.tr: I will clear of foes the lands of all Spain

adorer.tr: the glorious, whom we must adore!
The Glorious One, whom we must adore!

adorer.of: N'i ad paien nel prit e nel aort.
adorer.tr: There is no pagan who does not pray to him and adore him.

adouber.of: Escuz as cols e lances adubees.
adouber.tr: shields at their necks and lances equipped.
They go to arm themselves beneath a pine tree.
The pagans arm themselves with Saracen hauberks,

adouber.of: Adobez sunt a lei de chevalers
adouber.tr: They are equipped in the manner of knights

adouber.of: Adubez vos, si criez vostre enseigne,
adouber.tr: Arm yourselves, cry out your war-cry,

adouber.of: Franceis descendent, si adubent lor cors
adouber.tr: The French dismount, and they equip their bodies

advenir.of: De cest message nos avendrat grant perte.»
advenir.tr: From this mission great loss will befall us.»
- «Sire,» said Ganelon, «it falls to me to endure it;
Last night an angel's vision came to me,

advenir.of: As quatre [es]turs lor est avenut ben;
advenir.tr: In the four assaults it went well for them;

affermer.of: Afermet est a ses estreus d'or fin:
affermer.tr: He is held fast in his stirrups of fine gold:

affiler.of: Sur l'erbe verte li cler sancs s'en afilet.
affiler.tr: On the green grass the bright blood flows away.

affiner.tr: So many battles have we ended with them!

affubler.tr: He is wrapped in a sable mantle,

aider.of: Prozdom i out pur sun seignur aider;
aider.tr: A worthy man, and there he was to aid his lord;

aider.of: E lui aidez e pur seignur le tenez.»
aider.tr: And help him and hold him as your lord.»

aider.of: Que (v)[n]os aidez de Rollant le barun,
aider.tr: That you help us with Roland the baron,

aider.of: Si nos aidez de Rollant li marchis,
aider.tr: Thus help us regarding Roland the marquis,

aider.of: Si li truvez ki tres bien li aiut!»
aider.tr: And find him someone who may aid him well!»

aider.of: Si aiderez a cunduire ma gent.»
aider.tr: And you will help to conduct my people.»

aider.of: Respunt li quens: «Kar li aluns aider!»
aider.tr: The count replies: «Then let us go help him!»

aider.of: Aït vos Deus, ki unkes ne mentit!
aider.tr: May God aid you, who never lied!

aider.of: Apres escriet Rollant qu'il li aiut. AOI.
aider.tr: Afterward he cries out to Roland to help him. AOI.

aimer.of: Li reis Marsilie la tient, ki Deu nen amet,
aimer.tr: King Marsile holds it — he who does not love God,

aimer.of: E dit al cunte: «Jo ne vus aim nient;
aimer.tr: and he says to the count: «I do not love you at all;

aimer.of: Ne l'amerai a trestut mun vivant,
aimer.tr: I shall not love him all the days of my life,

aimer.of: Li duze per, por [ço] qu'il l'aiment tant,
aimer.tr: The twelve peers, because they love him so much,

aimer.of: Il l'a[i]ment tant ne li faldrunt nient;
aimer.tr: They love him so much they will not fail him at all;
Otherwise he will not love me at all.»

aimer.of: En talant ai que mult vos voeill amer,
aimer.tr: I have the desire that I much want to love you,

aimer.of: Li .XII. per, que Carles aimet tant,
aimer.tr: The Twelve Peers, whom Charles loves so much,

aimer.of: «Jo vos aim mult, sire,» dist ele al cunte,
aimer.tr: «I love you greatly, sire,» she said to the count,

aimer.of: Pur ben ferir l'emperere plus nos aimet.»
aimer.tr: For striking well the emperor loves us the more.»

aimer.of: Ne traïsun unkes amer ne volt.
aimer.tr: Nor did he ever wish to love treason.

aimer.of: Por itels colps nos eimet li emperere.»
aimer.tr: For such blows the emperor loves us.»

aimer.of: Plus aimet il traïsun e murdrie
aimer.tr: He loves treachery and murder more

aimer.of: Li arcevesque ne l'amerat ja mie;
aimer.tr: The archbishop will never love him at all;

aimer.of: Unches n'amai cuard ne cuardie.» AOI.
aimer.tr: Never did I love a coward or cowardice.» AOI.

aimer.of: Respont Rollant: «Ne pois amer les voz;
aimer.tr: Roland replies: «I cannot love your people;

aimer.of: Ja est ço Rollant, ki tant vos soelt amer!
aimer.tr: This is indeed Roland, who is so wont to love you!

ajuster.tr: Four hundred thousand he gathers in three days.

ajuster.of: Devant Marsilie as altres si s'ajust,
ajuster.tr: Before Marsile he joins the others,

ajuster.of: A cez paroles vunt les oz ajustant. AOI.
ajuster.tr: At these words the hosts go closing in. AOI.

ajuster.of: Francs e paiens, as les vus ajustez.
ajuster.tr: Franks and pagans, now they are joined in battle.

ajuster.of: Unches mais hom tel ne vit ajustee.
ajuster.tr: Never did any man see such a one joined.

aller.of: Alez en est en un verger suz l'umbre;
aller.tr: He has gone off into an orchard beneath the shade;

aller.of: Francs s'en irunt en France la lur tere.
aller.tr: The Franks will go away to France, their own land.

aller.of: «Seignurs baruns, a Carlemagnes irez;
aller.tr: «Lord barons, you shall go to Charlemagne;

aller.of: Dist a ses humes: «Seignurs, vos en ireiz;
aller.tr: He said to his men: «Lords, you shall go forth;

aller.of: Desuz un pin en est li reis alez,
aller.tr: Beneath a pine the king has gone,

aller.of: Li emper[er]es s'en vait desuz un pin.
aller.tr: The emperor goes off beneath a pine tree.

aller.of: Mais il me mandet que en France m'en alge:
aller.tr: But he commands me that I depart from France:

aller.of: Respunt dux Neimes: «Jo irai par vostre dun!
aller.tr: Duke Naimes replies: «I will go, by your leave!

aller.of: Vos n'irez pas uan de mei si luign.
aller.tr: You shall not go this year so far from me.

aller.of: Alez sedeir, quant nuls ne vos sumunt.»
aller.tr: Go sit down, since no one summons you.»

aller.of: Respunt Rollant: «Jo i puis aler mult ben!»
aller.tr: Roland answers: «I can go there very well!»

aller.of: Se li reis voelt, jo i puis aler ben.»
aller.tr: If the king wills it, I can well go there.»

aller.of: E jo irai al Sarazin en Espaigne,
aller.tr: And I shall go to the Saracen in Spain,

aller.of: Sin vois vedeir alques de sun semblant.»
aller.tr: And go see something of his disposition.»

aller.of: «Alez sedeir desur cel palie blanc!
aller.tr: «Go sit down upon that white silk cloth!

aller.of: Si as juget qu'a Marsiliun en alge!
aller.tr: So you have decided that I should go to Marsile!

aller.of: Guenes respunt: «Pur mei n'iras tu mie!
aller.tr: Ganelon answers: «You will not go at all on my behalf!

aller.of: En Sarraguce en irai a Marsilie;
aller.tr: To Saragossa I shall go, to Marsile;

aller.of: «En Sarraguce sai ben, [qu']aler m'estoet.
aller.tr: «I know well that I must go to Saragossa.

aller.of: Hom ki la vait, repairer ne s'en poet.
aller.tr: A man who goes there cannot return from it.

aller.of: Puisquel comant, aler vus en estoet.»
aller.tr: Since I command it, you must go.»

aller.of: Or irez vos certes, quant jol cumant.»
aller.tr: Now you will go indeed, since I command it.»

aller.of: - «Jo i puis aler, mais n'i avrai guarant: AOI.
aller.tr: - «I can go there, but I shall have no protection: AOI.

aller.of: Quant aler dei, n'i ai plus que targer.»
aller.tr: Since I must depart, there is no more reason for me to tarry.»

aller.of: Guenes li quens s'en vait a sun ostel,
aller.tr: Ganelon the count goes off to his lodging,

aller.of: Ki ço jugat, que doüsez aler
aller.tr: He who decreed that you should go

aller.of: En dulce France, seignurs, vos en irez:
aller.tr: To sweet France, lords, you will go back:

aller.of: Vait s'apuier suz le pin a la tige.
aller.tr: He goes to lean back against the trunk of the pine.

aller.of: Enz el verger s'en est alez li reis,
aller.tr: Into the orchard the king has gone,

aller.of: Par tantes teres est alet cunquerant,
aller.tr: Through so many lands has he gone conquering,

aller.of: Icil en vait al rei Marsiliun;
aller.tr: He goes to the king Marsile;

aller.of: Dist l'arcevesque: «Jo irai, par mun chef!»
aller.tr: Said the archbishop: «I shall go, by my head!»

aller.of: «Bel sires nies, e jo e vos [í]irum.
aller.tr: «Fair lord nephew, both I and you will go.

aller.of: «En Rencesvals irai mun cors juer!
aller.tr: «To Roncevaux I will go to disport my body!

aller.of: En Rencesvals a Rollant irai juindre,
aller.tr: At Roncevaux I will go to join Roland,

aller.of: Asez orrez, laquele irat desure.
aller.tr: Soon you will hear which one will prevail.

aller.of: «En Rencesvals irai l'orgoill desfaire.
aller.tr: «To Roncevaux I will go to undo their pride.

aller.of: En Rencesvals irez as porz passant,
aller.tr: You will go to Roncevaux, passing through the gorges,

aller.of: En Rencesvals irai Rollant ocire,
aller.tr: To Roncevaux I will go to slay Roland,

aller.of: Vunt s'aduber desuz une sapide.
aller.tr: They go to arm themselves beneath a pine tree.

aller.of: Mais sun espiet vait li bers palmeiant,
aller.tr: But the baron goes brandishing his spear,

aller.of: Cuntre le ciel vait la mure turnant,
aller.tr: Toward the sky he goes turning the point,

aller.of: Sun cumpaignun apres le vait sivant,
aller.tr: His companion comes following after him,

aller.of: «Seignurs barons, suef pas alez tenant!
aller.tr: «Lord barons, go forward at a gentle pace!

aller.of: Cist paien vont grant martirie querant.
aller.tr: These pagans go seeking great martyrdom.

aller.of: A cez paroles vunt les oz ajustant. AOI.
aller.tr: At these words the hosts go closing in. AOI.

aller.of: Brochent ad ait pur le plus tost aler,
aller.tr: They spur hard to go all the faster,

aller.of: Si vunt ferir, que fereient il el?
aller.tr: and they go to strike — what else would they do?

aller.of: De noz Franceis vait disant si mals moz:
aller.tr: He goes speaking such evil words about our French:

aller.of: Vait le ferir li quens quanque il pout.
aller.tr: The count goes to strike him with all his might.

aller.of: Vait le ferir en guise de baron.
aller.tr: Goes to strike him in the manner of a baron.

aller.of: Par grant vertut si l 'est alet ferir.
aller.tr: With great force he has gone to strike him.

aller.of: Sansun li dux, (il) vait ferir l'almaçur:
aller.tr: Duke Samson, he goes to strike the emir:

aller.of: Si vait ferir Turgis de Turteluse;
aller.tr: Then goes to strike Turgis of Tortelose;

aller.of: Si vait ferir Escremiz de Valterne:
aller.tr: And goes to strike Escremiz of Valterne:

aller.of: Le cheval brochet, vait ferir Oliver:
aller.tr: He spurs his horse, goes to strike Oliver:

aller.of: Ultre s'en vait, qu'il n'i ad desturber;
aller.tr: He passes on beyond, for there is nothing to stop him;

aller.of: Sun cheval brochet, si vait ferir Chernuble:
aller.tr: He spurs his horse, and goes to strike Chernuble:

aller.of: E vait fer(en)[ir] un paien, Malun:
aller.tr: And he goes to strike a pagan, Malun:

aller.of: E vunt ferir un paien, Timozel,
aller.tr: And go to strike a pagan, Timozel,

aller.of: Qu'en Sarraguce sa maisnee alat vendre;
aller.tr: When he went to Saragossa to sell his household;

aller.of: Vunt par le camp, si requerent les lor,
aller.tr: They go through the field, and they seek their own,

aller.of: Mielz est mult que jo l'alge ocire.
aller.tr: It is far better that I go and slay him.

aller.of: Beste nen est nule ki encontre lui alge.
aller.tr: There is no beast that can match it.

aller.of: Vait le ferir en l'escut amiracle:
aller.tr: He goes to strike him on the marvelous shield:

aller.of: «Seignors barons, n'en alez mespensant!
aller.tr: «Lord barons, do not go thinking ill!

aller.of: Si vait ferir Engeler de Guascoigne.
aller.tr: And goes to strike Engelier of Gascony.

aller.of: Par grant vertut vait ferir le paien.
aller.tr: With great strength he goes to strike the pagan.

aller.of: Si vait ferir li riche duc Sansun,
aller.tr: And goes to strike the mighty duke Samson,

aller.of: Vait le ferir li bers, quanque il pout,
aller.tr: The baron goes to strike him with all his strength,

aller.of: Il vait ferir Anseïs en l'escut:
aller.tr: He goes to strike Anseïs on the shield:

aller.of: Par le camp vait Turpin li arcevesque;
aller.tr: Through the field goes Turpin the archbishop;

aller.of: Si vait ferir Gerin par sa grant force.
aller.tr: And goes to strike Gerin with his great force.

aller.of: Puis vait ferir un riche duc Austorje,
aller.tr: Then he goes to strike a rich duke, Austorge,

aller.of: Respunt li quens: «Kar li aluns aider!»
aller.tr: The count replies: «Then let us go help him!»

aller.of: Pur un sul levre vat tute jur cornant,
aller.tr: For a single hare he goes blowing his horn all day,

aller.of: Devant ses pers vait il ore gabant.
aller.tr: Before his peers he goes now boasting.

aller.of: Car chevalcez! Pur qu'alez arestant?
aller.tr: So ride on! Why do you keep stopping?

aller.of: Sire cumpainz, alum i referir!»
aller.tr: Lord companion, let us go strike again!»

aller.of: Si cum li cerfs s'en vait devant les chiens,
aller.tr: Just as the stag flees before the hounds,

aller.of: Brochet le ben, si vait ferir Bevon,
aller.tr: spurs it well, and goes to strike Beuve,

aller.of: Vait le ferir en guise de baron:
aller.tr: He goes to strike him in the guise of a baron:

aller.of: A icest mot tels .C. milie s'en vunt:
aller.tr: At this word a good hundred thousand of them go off:

aller.of: Quel part qu'il alt, ne poet mie chaïr.
aller.tr: Wherever he may go, he cannot fall.

allier.tr: At these words the twelve peers ally themselves;

allier.of: Sun dragun portet a qui sa gent s'alient.
allier.tr: He carries his dragon-standard, around which his people rally.

amener.of: Dis blanches mules fist amener Marsilies,
amener.tr: Marsile had ten white mules brought,

amener.of: Al siege ad Ais en serez amenet,
amener.tr: To the seat at Aix you will be brought,

amener.of: Ço dist li reis: «E vos l'i ameneiz.»
amener.tr: Thus said the king: «And you, bring him here to me.»

amener.of: Mult grant aveir vos en faz amener,
amener.tr: Very great treasure I have brought to you,

amener.of: Se il fust vif, jo l'oüsse amenet.
amener.tr: Had he been alive, I would have brought him.

angoisser.of: Oliver sent que la mort mult l'angoisset.
angoisser.tr: Oliver feels that death greatly anguishes him.

appareiller.of: E de bataille sunt tuit apareillez.
appareiller.tr: And are all made ready for battle.

apparoir.of: Tresvait la noit, e apert la clere albe.
apparoir.tr: The night passes, and the bright dawn appears.

appeler.of: Il en apelet e ses dux e ses cuntes:
appeler.tr: He calls forth both his dukes and his counts:
So he summoned Clarin (...) of Balaguer,

appeler.of: Des plus feluns dís en ad apelez:
appeler.tr: Of the most felonious he has summoned ten of them:

appeler.of: Li reis apelet Malduit sun tresorer:
appeler.tr: The king calls Malduit, his treasurer:

appeler.of: Li empereres apelet ses nies Rollant:
appeler.tr: The emperor calls his nephew Roland:

appeler.of: Li quens Rollant Gualter de l'Húm apelet:
appeler.tr: Count Roland calls to Gautier de l'Hum:

appeler.of: Sun uncle apelet de mult fiere raisun:
appeler.tr: He addresses his uncle with very fierce speech:
And he called Roland, his companion:
He calls out to the French, he addressed Oliver:

appeler.of: Franceis apelet, un sermun lur ad dit:
appeler.tr: He calls the Franks, he has given them a sermon:
He called out to the other Saracens:

appeler.of: Li quens Rollant en apelet Oliver:
appeler.tr: Count Roland calls out to Oliver:
Count Roland calls out to Oliver:
He calls upon his companion Oliver:

appeler.of: Tut li plus maistre en apelet, Besgun.
appeler.tr: He calls upon the chief of them all, Besgun.

appeler.of: Siet el cheval qu'il apelet Gaignun,
appeler.tr: He sits on the horse that he calls Gaignun,

appeler.of: Rollant apelet, sun ami e sun per:
appeler.tr: He calls Roland, his friend and his peer:

apporter.of: De Sarraguce ci vos aport les clefs;
apporter.tr: From Saragossa here I bring you the keys;

approcher.of: Envers le rei s'est Guenes aproismet,
approcher.tr: Toward the king Ganelon has drawn near,

appuyer.of: Vait s'apuier suz le pin a la tige.
appuyer.tr: He goes to lean back against the trunk of the pine.

arguer.tr: Who urge themselves on to battle, and make haste:

arrêter.tr: In the horse the sword came to rest,
So ride on! Why do you keep stopping?

assaillir.of: Sun cors demenie mult fierement asalt.
assaillir.tr: Very fiercely it attacks his own body.
We will assail Oliver and Roland;

assaillir.of: Se ne l'asaill, dunc ne faz jo que creire,
assaillir.tr: If I do not assail him, then let me not be believed,
He will not fail to assail Abisme;

assembler.tr: He has joined the Saracen messengers;

assembler.of: N'asemblereit jamais Carles si grant esforz;
assembler.tr: Charles would never again assemble so great a host;

assembler.of: E Sarrazins, ki tant sunt asemblez.
assembler.tr: And the Saracens, who are gathered in such numbers.

assembler.of: Felun paien mar i sunt asemblez:
assembler.tr: The felon pagans have gathered here to their misfortune:

assembler.of: Od sa grant ost que il out asemblee.
assembler.tr: With his great host that he had assembled.

asseoir.tr: For Marsile has seated himself on the throne.

assiéger.tr: He will come to besiege you in Saragossa;

assurer.of: Li quens Rollant mie ne s'asoüret,
assurer.tr: Count Roland does not spare himself at all,

attarder.of: Mais Blancandrins ki envers lu s'atarget;
attarder.tr: But Blancandrin, who lingers back toward him —

atteindre.of: Nes poet guarder que mals ne l'i ateignet. AOI.
atteindre.tr: he cannot keep harm from reaching him there. AOI.

attendre.of: De Guenelun atent li reis nuveles,
attendre.tr: From Ganelon the king awaits news,
four hundred thousand await the daybreak.

attendre.of: Ne cels de France ki as porz les atendent. AOI.
attendre.tr: Nor those of France who await them at the passes. AOI.

attendre.of: Ne Carlemagne, ki as porz les atent.
attendre.tr: Nor Charlemagne, who awaits them at the passes.

avaler.of: D'enz de la sale uns veltres avalat,
avaler.tr: From within the hall a hound came bounding down,

avaler.of: Cum il einz pout, del pui est avalet,
avaler.tr: As fast as he could, he came down from the hill,

avoir.of: set anz tuz pleins ad estet en Espaigne:
avoir.tr: has been in Spain seven full years:

avoir.of: N'i ad castel ki devant lui remaigne;
avoir.tr: There is no fortress that stands against him;

avoir.of: Jo nen ai ost qui bataille li dunne,
avoir.tr: I have no army that may give him battle,

avoir.of: Ne n'ai tel gent ki la sue derumpet.
avoir.tr: Nor have I such a host as might break his.

avoir.of: N'i ad paien ki un sul mot respundet,
avoir.tr: There is no pagan who answers a single word,

avoir.of: Prozdom i out pur sun seignur aider;
avoir.tr: A worthy man, and there he was to aid his lord;

avoir.of: En ceste tere ad asez osteiet;
avoir.tr: In this land he has campaigned long enough;

avoir.of: Ne nus aiuns les mals ne les suffraites.»
avoir.tr: And that we have the woes and the privations.»

avoir.of: Li reis Marsilie out sun cunseill finet:
avoir.tr: King Marsile had ended his council:

avoir.of: Des plus feluns dís en ad apelez:
avoir.tr: Of the most felonious he has summoned ten of them:

avoir.of: Dient paien: «De ço avun nus asez!» AOI.
avoir.tr: The pagans say: «Of this we have enough!» AOI.

avoir.of: Li reis Marsilie out finet sun cunseill;
avoir.tr: King Marsile had ended his council;

avoir.of: Pur le soen Deu qu'il ait m(er)ercit de mei.
avoir.tr: For the sake of his God, that he have mercy on me.

avoir.of: S'il voelt ostages, il en avrat par veir.»
avoir.tr: If he wants hostages, he shall have them indeed.»

avoir.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Mult bon plait en avreiz.» AOI.
avoir.tr: Blancandrin said: «You shall have a very good agreement out of it.» AOI.

avoir.of: Vindrent a Charles ki France ad en baillie:
avoir.tr: They came to Charles, who has France in his keeping:

avoir.of: Cordres ad prise e les murs peceiez,
avoir.tr: He has taken Cordres and shattered its walls,

avoir.of: Mult grant eschech en unt si chevaler
avoir.tr: His knights take very great booty from it

avoir.of: En la citet nen ad remes paien,
avoir.tr: In the city there remains no pagan,

avoir.of: La u cist furent, des altres i out bien:
avoir.tr: Where these were, there were indeed many others:

avoir.of: De dulce france i ad quinze milliers.
avoir.tr: From sweet France there are fifteen thousand.

avoir.of: Un faldestoed i unt fait tut d or mer,
avoir.tr: A throne they have made all of pure gold,

avoir.of: Blanche ad la barbe e tut flurit le chef,
avoir.tr: White is his beard and all blossomed his head,

avoir.of: Gent ad le cors e le cuntenant fier,
avoir.tr: Noble is his body and his bearing proud,

avoir.of: Blancandrins ad tut premereins parled,
avoir.tr: Blancandrin spoke first of all,

avoir.of: Enquis ad mult la lei de salvetez;
avoir.tr: he has inquired much into the law of salvation;

avoir.of: Tant i avrat de besanz esmerez
avoir.tr: there will be so much of it in refined bezants

avoir.of: En cest païs avez estet asez;
avoir.tr: In this land you have stayed long enough;

avoir.of: Quant se redrecet, mult par out fier lu vis;
avoir.tr: When he straightens up, his face looked most proud;

avoir.of: Dist as messages: «Vus avez mult ben dit.
avoir.tr: He said to the messengers: «You have spoken very well.

avoir.of: De cez paroles que vos avez ci dit,
avoir.tr: Concerning these words that you have spoken here,

avoir.of: «Dunt vos avrez ú dis ú quinze ú vint.
avoir.tr: «Of whom you shall have ten or fifteen or twenty.

avoir.of: E sin avrez, ço quid, de plus gentilz.
avoir.tr: And you shall have, I think, [some] of more noble birth.

avoir.of: Les dis messages ad fait enz hosteler;
avoir.tr: The ten messengers he has had lodged within;

avoir.of: .XII. serjanz les unt ben cunreez.
avoir.tr: Twelve sergeants have well equipped them.

avoir.of: Messe e matines ad li reis escultet.
avoir.tr: Mass and matins the king has heard.

avoir.of: Des Francs de France en i ad plus de mil.
avoir.tr: Of the Franks of France there are more than a thousand of them.

avoir.of: «Li reis Marsilie m'ad tramis ses messages;
avoir.tr: «King Marsile has sent me his messengers;

avoir.of: Li empereres out sa raisun fenie.
avoir.tr: The emperor had finished his speech.

avoir.of: Set anz [ad] pleins, que en Espaigne venimes;
avoir.tr: Seven full years it is, since we came into Spain;

avoir.of: Pris ai Valterne e la tere de Pine
avoir.tr: I have taken Valterne and the land of Pine

avoir.of: Faites la guer[re] cum vos l'avez enprise:
avoir.tr: Wage the war as you have undertaken it:

avoir.of: Meillor vassal n'aveit en la curt nul,
avoir.tr: There was no better vassal in the court,

avoir.of: E dist al rei: «Ben l'avez entendud,
avoir.tr: And he said to the king: «You have indeed heard it,

avoir.of: Guenes li quens ço vus ad respondud,
avoir.tr: Ganelon the count has answered you thus,

avoir.of: Saveir i ad, mais qu'il seit entendud.
avoir.tr: There is wisdom in it, provided it be heeded.

avoir.of: Vos li avez tuz ses castels toluz,
avoir.tr: You have taken from him all his castles,

avoir.of: Od voz caables avez fruiset ses murs,
avoir.tr: With your siege engines you have shattered his walls,

avoir.of: Quant il vos mandet, qu'aiez mercit de lui,
avoir.tr: When he sends word to you, that you have mercy on him,

avoir.of: Dient Franceis: «Ben ad parlet li dux.» AOI.
avoir.tr: The Franks say: «The duke has spoken well.» AOI.

avoir.of: En cest païs avez estet set anz;
avoir.tr: In this land you have been seven years;

avoir.of: Mult unt oüd e peines e ahans.
avoir.tr: Much have they endured, both hardships and toils.

avoir.of: Vairs out [les oilz] e mult fier lu visage,
avoir.tr: He had bright eyes and a very fierce face,

avoir.of: Gent out le cors e les costez out larges;
avoir.tr: He had a noble body and his sides were broad;

avoir.of: Si as juget qu'a Marsiliun en alge!
avoir.tr: So you have decided that I should go to Marsile!

avoir.of: Ço set hom ben, n'ai cure de manace;
avoir.tr: One knows well, I have no care for a threat;

avoir.of: Dunc ad tel doel pur poi d'ire ne fent,
avoir.tr: then he has such grief that he nearly bursts from rage,

avoir.of: Sur mei avez turnet fals jugement.
avoir.tr: you have turned a false judgment upon me.

avoir.of: Ensurquetut si ai jo vostre soer,
avoir.tr: Above all, I have your sister,

avoir.of: Sin ai un filz, ja plus bels n'en estoet:
avoir.tr: and by her I have a son, there is none more handsome:

avoir.of: Carles respunt: «trop avez tendre coer.
avoir.tr: Charles answers: «you have too tender a heart.

avoir.of: Oït l'avez, sur vos le jugent Franc.»
avoir.tr: You have heard it, the Franks judge it against you.»

avoir.of: - «Sire,» dist Guenes, «ço ad tut fait Rollant!
avoir.tr: - «Sire,» said Ganelon, «Roland has done all this!

avoir.of: Ço dist li reis: «Trop avez maltalant.
avoir.tr: The king said: «You have too much ill will.

avoir.of: - «Jo i puis aler, mais n'i avrai guarant: AOI.
avoir.tr: - «I can go there, but I shall have no protection: AOI.

avoir.of: Nu l'out Basilies ne sis freres Basant.»
avoir.tr: Basil had none of it, nor his brother Basant.»

avoir.of: Quant aler dei, n'i ai plus que targer.»
avoir.tr: Since I must depart, there is no more reason for me to tarry.»

avoir.of: De sa main destre l'ad asols e seignet,
avoir.tr: With his right hand he absolved and blessed him,

avoir.of: Esperuns d'or ad en ses piez fermez,
avoir.tr: He has fastened golden spurs on his feet,

avoir.of: En (la) cort al rei mult i avez ested,
avoir.tr: In the king's court you have long been,

avoir.of: Ne mes Rollant, ki uncore en avrat hunte.
avoir.tr: Save Roland, who will yet have shame from it.

avoir.of: Vint i ses nies, out vestue sa brunie,
avoir.tr: His nephew came, had donned his hauberk,

avoir.of: E out predet dejuste Carcasonie;
avoir.tr: And had taken booty near Carcassonne;

avoir.of: Seit, ki l'ociet, tute pais puis avriúmes.» AOI.
avoir.tr: Were someone to slay him, we should then have full peace.» AOI.

avoir.of: L'emperere meïsmes ad tut a sun talent.
avoir.tr: The emperor himself has everything to his liking.

avoir.of: Un faldestoet out suz l'umbre d'un pin;
avoir.tr: There was a throne in the shade of a pine;

avoir.of: N'i ad celoi ki mot sunt ne mot tint,
avoir.tr: There is none among them who speaks a word nor utters a word,

avoir.of: Par lui orrez si avrez pais u nun.»
avoir.tr: From him you will hear whether you will have peace or not.»

avoir.of: Li reis Marsilies ad la culur muee;
avoir.tr: King Marsile has changed color;

avoir.of: De sun algeir ad la hanste crollee.
avoir.tr: With his javelin he has shaken the shaft.

avoir.of: Cuntre dous deie l'ad del furrer getee,
avoir.tr: He drew it two fingers' width out of the scabbard,

avoir.of: Si li ad dit: «Mult estes bele e clere!
avoir.tr: And said to it: «You are very fair and bright!

avoir.of: Tant vus avrai en curt a rei portee!
avoir.tr: So long shall I have borne you at the king's court!

avoir.of: Einz vos avrunt li meillor comperee.»
avoir.tr: Before the best men shall have paid dearly for you.»

avoir.of: Dist l'algalifes: «Mal nos avez baillit,
avoir.tr: The caliph said: «You have served us ill,

avoir.of: Que jo ne li die, se tant ai de leisir,
avoir.tr: From telling him, if I have so much leisure,

avoir.of: Dient paien: «Noble baron ad ci!» AOI.
avoir.tr: The pagans say: «Here is a noble baron!» AOI.

avoir.of: Si li ad dit: «A tort vos curuciez,
avoir.tr: And said to him: «Wrongly do you grow angry,

avoir.of: L'altre meitet avrat Rollant, sis nies:
avoir.tr: The other half Roland, his nephew, will have:

avoir.of: Mulz orguillos parçuner i avrez!
avoir.tr: A very proud co-sharer you will have there!

avoir.of: Vus n'i avrez palefreid ne destrer,
avoir.tr: You will have there neither palfrey nor warhorse,

avoir.of: El destre poign al paien l'ad liv(e)ret.
avoir.tr: Into the right fist of the pagan he handed it over.

avoir.of: Freint le seel, getet en ad la cire,
avoir.tr: He breaks the seal, has cast away the wax,

avoir.of: «Carle me mandet, ki France ad en baillie,
avoir.tr: «Charles, who has France in his keeping, commands me,

avoir.of: E dist al rei: «Guenes ad dit folie;
avoir.tr: And said to the king: «Ganelon has spoken folly;

avoir.of: Tant ad erret nen est dreiz que plus vivet.
avoir.tr: He has gone so far that it is not right that he live longer.

avoir.of: Quant l'oït Guenes, l'espee en ad branlie;
avoir.tr: When Ganelon heard this, he brandished his sword;

avoir.of: De nostre prod m'ad plevie sa feid.»
avoir.tr: He has pledged me his faith concerning our advantage.»

avoir.of: E Guenes (l')ad pris par la main destre ad deiz,
avoir.tr: And Ganelon took him by the right hand by the fingers,

avoir.of: «Bel sire Guenes,» ço li ad dit Marsilie,
avoir.tr: «Fair sir Ganelon,» Marsile said to him,

avoir.of: «Jo vos ai fait alques de legerie,
avoir.tr: «I have done you something of a wrong,

avoir.of: En talant ai que mult vos voeill amer,
avoir.tr: I have the desire that I much want to love you,

avoir.of: Il est mult vielz, si ad sun tens uset;
avoir.tr: He is very old, and has worn out his time;

avoir.of: Men escient dous cenz anz ad passet.
avoir.tr: To my knowledge he has passed two hundred years.

avoir.of: Par tantes teres ad sun cors demened,
avoir.tr: Through so many lands he has driven his body,

avoir.of: Tanz [colps] ad pris sur sun escut bucler,
avoir.tr: So many blows has he taken upon his buckler-shield,

avoir.of: Que plus n'i ad d'onur e de bontet.
avoir.tr: That there is not yet more of honor and goodness in him.

avoir.of: De tel barnage l'ad Deus enluminet,
avoir.tr: With such nobility has God illumined him,

avoir.of: Men escientre dous cenz anz ad e mielz.
avoir.tr: To my knowledge he is two hundred years old and more.

avoir.of: Par tantes teres ad sun cors traveillet,
avoir.tr: Through so many lands he has worn out his body,

avoir.of: Tanz colz ad pris de lances e d'espiet,
avoir.tr: so many blows he has taken from lances and spears,

avoir.of: Tanz riches reis cunduiz a mendistiet:
avoir.tr: so many rich kings he has brought to beggary:

avoir.of: N'at tel vassal suz la cape del ciel.
avoir.tr: there is no such vassal under the cope of heaven.

avoir.of: Les .XII. pers, que Carles ad tant chers,
avoir.tr: The Twelve Peers, whom Charles holds so dear,

avoir.of: Dist li Sarrazins: «Merveille en ai grant
avoir.tr: Said the Saracen: «Great is the wonder I feel

avoir.of: Mien escientre plus ad de .II.C. anz.
avoir.tr: By my reckoning he is more than two hundred years old.

avoir.of: Tanz colps ad pris de bons espiez trenchanz,
avoir.tr: So many blows has he taken from good sharp spears,

avoir.of: N'ad tel vassal d'ici qu en Orient.
avoir.tr: There is no such vassal from here to the Orient.

avoir.of: «Jo ai tel gent, plus bele ne verreiz;
avoir.tr: «I have such troops, you will never see finer;

avoir.of: Quarte cenz milie chevalers puis aveir.
avoir.tr: Four hundred thousand knights I can have.

avoir.of: De voz paiens mult grant perte i avreiz.
avoir.tr: Of your pagans you would suffer there very great loss.

avoir.of: L'empereür tant li dunez aveir,
avoir.tr: Give the emperor so much wealth,

avoir.of: N'i ait Franceis ki tot ne s'en merveilt.
avoir.tr: That there will be no Frank who does not marvel at it.

avoir.of: N'avrat talent, que ja mais vus guerreit.» AOI.
avoir.tr: He will have no desire ever again to make war on you.» AOI.

avoir.of: Sa rereguarde avrat detres sei mise;
avoir.tr: He will have placed his rearguard behind him;

avoir.of: .XX. milie Francs unt en lur cumpaignie.
avoir.tr: Twenty thousand Franks they have in their company.

avoir.of: Dunc avrez faite gente chevalerie;
avoir.tr: Then you will have done a noble feat of arms;

avoir.of: N'avrez mais guere en tute vostre vie.» AOI.
avoir.tr: You will have no more war in all your life.» AOI.

avoir.of: Quan l'ot Marsilie, si l'ad baiset el col,
avoir.tr: When Marsile heard this, he kissed him on the neck,

avoir.of: Un faldestoed i out d'un olifant;
avoir.tr: There was a throne there made of ivory;

avoir.of: Ço ad juret li Sarrazins espans:
avoir.tr: The Spanish Saracen has sworn this:

avoir.of: Cler en riant l'ad dit a Guenelun:
avoir.tr: Brightly, laughing, he said to Ganelon:

avoir.of: «Tenez m'espee, meillur n'en at nuls hom;
avoir.tr: «Take my sword, no man has a better one;

avoir.of: Entre les helz ad plus de mil manguns.
avoir.tr: Between the hilts there are more than a thousand mangons.

avoir.of: Cler en riant a Guenelun l'ad dit:
avoir.tr: Brightly, laughing, he said to Ganelon:

avoir.of: Bien i ad or, matices e jacunces:
avoir.tr: There is much gold in them, amethysts and jacinths:

avoir.of: Vostre emperere si bones n'en out unches.»
avoir.tr: Your emperor never had any so fine.»

avoir.of: Il les ad prises, en sa hoese les butet, AOI.
avoir.tr: He took them, into his boot he stuffed them, AOI.

avoir.of: Si li ad dit: «Mult par ies ber e sage.
avoir.tr: And said to him: «You are most valiant and wise.

avoir.of: Li quens Rollant il l'ad e prise e fraite;
avoir.tr: Count Roland had taken and shattered it;

avoir.of: Messe e matines ad li reis escultet.
avoir.tr: Mass and matins has the king heard.

avoir.of: Einz qu'il oüssent .IIII. liues siglet,
avoir.tr: Before they had sailed four leagues,

avoir.of: Se il fust vif, jo l'oüsse amenet.
avoir.tr: Had he been alive, I would have brought him.

avoir.of: Ben l'avez fait, mult grant prod i avrez.»
avoir.tr: You have done well, very great reward you will have for it.»

avoir.of: Carles li magnes ad Espaigne guastede
avoir.tr: Charles the Great has laid waste to Spain,

avoir.of: Ço dit li reis que sa guere out finee.
avoir.tr: The king says that his war is over.

avoir.of: Li quens Rollant ad l'enseigne fermee
avoir.tr: Count Roland has fastened the standard,

avoir.of: Guenes li quens l'ad sur lui saisie;
avoir.tr: Ganelon the count has seized it from him;

avoir.of: Par tel air l'at estrussee e brandie,
avoir.tr: With such force he wrenched and brandished it,

avoir.of: Dient Franceis, que grant bataille i ad;
avoir.tr: The Franks say that there is a great battle there;

avoir.of: N'avez baron de si grant vasselage.»
avoir.tr: You have no baron of such great valor.»

avoir.of: Si li ad dit: «Vos estes vifs diables.
avoir.tr: And said to him: «You are a living devil.

avoir.of: N'avez barun, ki mielz de lui la facet.» AOI.
avoir.tr: You have no baron who would do it better than he.» AOI.

avoir.of: Dunc ad parled a lei de chevaler:
avoir.tr: Then spoke as befits a knight:

avoir.of: «Sire parastre, mult vos dei aveir cher:
avoir.tr: «Lord stepfather, I must hold you dear:

avoir.of: La rereguarde avez sur mei jugiet.
avoir.tr: You have assigned the rearguard to me.

avoir.of: Meillor vassal n'out en la curt de lui;
avoir.tr: There was no better vassal at his court;

avoir.of: E dist al rei: «Ben l'avez entendut;
avoir.tr: And he said to the king: «You have heard it well;

avoir.of: N'avez baron ki jamais la remut.
avoir.tr: You have no baron who would ever change it.

avoir.of: Dunez li l'arc que vos avez tendut,
avoir.tr: Give him the bow that you have drawn,

avoir.of: Li reis li dunet, e Rollant l'a reçut.
avoir.tr: The king gives it to him, and Roland received it.

avoir.of: Cel nen i ad ki de pitet ne plurt.
avoir.tr: There is none there who does not weep for pity.

avoir.of: As porz d'Espaigne ad lesset sun nevold.
avoir.tr: At the passes of Spain he has left his nephew.

avoir.of: .XX. milie F(r)rancs unt en lur cumpaigne,
avoir.tr: Twenty thousand Franks are in their company,

avoir.of: N'en unt poür ne de murir dutance.
avoir.tr: They have no fear nor dread of dying.

avoir.of: E dit al rei: «De quei avez pesance?»
avoir.tr: And says to the king: «What is troubling you?»

avoir.of: Si grant doel ai ne puis muer nel pleigne.
avoir.tr: Such great grief I have that I cannot keep from lamenting it.

avoir.of: Chi ad juget mis nes a (la) rereguarde.
avoir.tr: He who has assigned my nephew to the rearguard.

avoir.of: Jo l'ai lesset en une estrange marche!
avoir.tr: I have left him in a strange land!

avoir.of: Deus! se jol pert, ja n'en avrai escange!» AOI.
avoir.tr: God! if I lose him, I shall have no replacement for him!» AOI.

avoir.of: .C. milie Francs pur lui unt grant tendrur,
avoir.tr: A hundred thousand Franks feel great tenderness for him,

avoir.of: Guen[e]s li fels en ad fait traïsun:
avoir.tr: Ganelon the felon has committed treason against him:

avoir.of: Del rei paien en ad oüd granz duns,
avoir.tr: From the pagan king he has received great gifts,

avoir.of: N'i ad paien nel prit e nel aort.
avoir.tr: There is no pagan who does not pray to him and adore him.

avoir.of: «Bel sire reis, jo vos ai servit tant,
avoir.tr: «Fair lord king, I have served you so long,

avoir.of: Sin ai oüt e peines e ahans,
avoir.tr: And I have had from it both hardships and toils,

avoir.of: Ja n'avrez mais guere en tut vostre vivant.»
avoir.tr: Never again will you have war in all your life.»

avoir.of: Li reis Marsilie l'en ad dunet le guant. AOI.
avoir.tr: King Marsile has given him the glove for it. AOI.

avoir.of: «Bel sire reis, fait m'avez un grant dun.
avoir.tr: «Fair lord king, you have granted me a great gift.

avoir.of: Cil ad parlet a lei de bon vassal:
avoir.tr: He has spoken in the manner of a good vassal:

avoir.of: Uns amurafles i ad de Balaguez:
avoir.tr: There is there an emir from Balaguer:

avoir.of: Cors ad mult gent e le vis fier e cler;
avoir.tr: He has a very noble body and a face fierce and bright;

avoir.of: Fust chrestiens, asez oüst barnet.
avoir.tr: Were he a Christian, he would have had ample baronage.

avoir.of: Li reis Marsilie mult l'en ad merciet. AOI.
avoir.tr: King Marsile thanks him greatly for it. AOI.

avoir.of: Uns almaçurs i ad de Moriane;
avoir.tr: There is an emir there from Moriane;

avoir.of: N'ad plus felun en la tere d'Espaigne.
avoir.tr: There is no one more felon in the land of Spain.

avoir.of: Devant Marsilie ad faite sa vantance:
avoir.tr: Before Marsile he made his boast:

avoir.of: .XX. milie ad escuz e a lances.
avoir.tr: Twenty thousand with shields and with lances.

avoir.of: De mort n'avrat guarantisun pur hume.
avoir.tr: From death he will have no safeguard for any man.

avoir.of: Carles li velz avrat e deol e hunte:
avoir.tr: Charles the old will have grief and shame:

avoir.of: De bons vassals avrat Carles suffraite.» AOI.
avoir.tr: Charles will have want of good vassals.» AOI.

avoir.of: Li .XII. per n'avrunt de mort guarant.
avoir.tr: The Twelve Peers will have no protection from death.

avoir.of: N'i ad paien de tel chevalerie.
avoir.tr: There is no pagan of such knighthood.

avoir.of: Carles li velz a la barbe flurie,
avoir.tr: Charles the old, with the flowering beard,

avoir.of: Jamais n'ert jurn qu'il n'en ait doel e ire.
avoir.tr: Never will there be a day that he does not have grief and wrath from it.

avoir.of: Jusqu'a un an avrum France saisie;
avoir.tr: Within a year we shall have seized France;

avoir.of: Piere n'i ad que tute ne seit neire.
avoir.tr: There is no stone there that is not entirely black.

avoir.of: Ce dist Chernubles: «Ma bone espee ai ceinte;
avoir.tr: Chernuble says: «My good sword I have girded on;

avoir.of: Escuz unt genz, espiez valentineis,
avoir.tr: They have handsome shields, Valencian spears,

avoir.of: N'unt guarnement que tut ne reflambeit.
avoir.tr: They have no piece of armor that does not all blaze with light.

avoir.of: De Sarrazins purum bataille aveir.»
avoir.tr: We may have battle from the Saracens.»

avoir.of: Paien unt tort e chrestiens unt dreit;
avoir.tr: The pagans are in the wrong and the Christians are in the right;

avoir.of: Tant en i ad que mesure n'en set.
avoir.tr: There are so many of them that he does not know their number.

avoir.of: Vint as Franceis, tut lur ad acuntet.
avoir.tr: Came to the French, told them everything.

avoir.of: Dist Oliver: «Jo ai paiens veüz:
avoir.tr: Oliver said: «I have seen the pagans:

avoir.of: Bataille avrez, unches mais tel ne fut.
avoir.tr: You will have a battle, never was there such a one.

avoir.of: Seignurs Franceis, de Deu aiez vertut!
avoir.tr: Lord Franks, may you have valor from God!

avoir.of: Dient Franceis: «Dehet ait ki s'en fuit!
avoir.tr: The Franks say: «Cursed be he who flees!

avoir.of: Dist Oliver: «Paien unt grant esforz,
avoir.tr: Oliver said: «The pagans have a great host,

avoir.of: De noz Franceis m'i semblet aveir mult poi!
avoir.tr: of our Franks it seems to me there are very few!

avoir.of: Ma bone espee que ai ceint al costet:
avoir.tr: My good sword that I have girded at my side:

avoir.of: Ja n'en avrunt reproece mi parent!
avoir.tr: My kinsmen shall never have reproach for it!

avoir.of: Ja cil d'Espaigne n'avrunt de mort guarant.»
avoir.tr: Those of Spain shall never have a guarantee against death.»

avoir.of: Jo ai veüt les Sarrazins d'Espaigne,
avoir.tr: I have seen the Saracens of Spain,

avoir.of: Nus i avum mult petite cumpaigne.»
avoir.tr: We here have a very small company.»

avoir.of: Ambedui unt me[r]veillus vasselage.
avoir.tr: Both have marvelous valor.

avoir.of: Fust i li reis, n'i oüssum damage.
avoir.tr: Were the king here, we would have no harm.

avoir.of: Sun escientre n'en i out un cuard.
avoir.tr: To his knowledge there was not one coward among them.

avoir.of: Se jo i moert, dire poet ki l'avrat
avoir.tr: If I die here, whoever has it may say

avoir.of: Franceis apelet, un sermun lur ad dit:
avoir.tr: He calls the Franks, he has given them a sermon:

avoir.of: Bataille avrez, vos en estes tuz fiz,
avoir.tr: You will have battle, of this you are all certain,

avoir.of: Sieges avrez el greignor pareïs.»
avoir.tr: You will have seats in the greater paradise.»

avoir.of: E l'arcevesque de Deu les ad seignez;
avoir.tr: And the archbishop has blessed them in God's name;

avoir.of: Que Guenelun nos ad tuz espiez;
avoir.tr: That Ganelon has betrayed us all;

avoir.of: Pris en ad or e aveir e deners.
avoir.tr: He has taken for it gold and wealth and money.

avoir.of: Li reis Marsilie de nos ad fait marchet;
avoir.tr: King Marsile has made a bargain of us;

avoir.of: Cors ad mult gent, le vis cler e riant.
avoir.tr: His body is most noble, his face bright and smiling.

avoir.of: Si lur ad dit un mot curteisement:
avoir.tr: And he said a word to them courteously:

avoir.of: Encoi avrum un eschec bel e gent:
avoir.tr: Today we shall have fine and noble spoils:

avoir.of: Nuls reis de France n'out unkes si vaillant.»
avoir.tr: No king of France ever had such valuable ones.»

avoir.of: Dist Oliver: «N'ai cure de parler.
avoir.tr: Said Oliver: «I have no wish to speak.

avoir.of: Ne de Carlun mie vos n'en avez.
avoir.tr: and so you have nothing from Charles in this matter.

avoir.of: Il n'en set mot, n'i ad culpes li bers.
avoir.tr: He knows nothing of it; the noble lord is not to blame.

avoir.of: E Sarrazins nes unt mie dutez;
avoir.tr: And they have not at all feared the Saracens;

avoir.of: Li nies Marsilie, il ad a num Aelroth;
avoir.tr: The nephew of Marsile, he is named Aelroth;

avoir.of: Traït vos ad ki a guarder vos out.
avoir.tr: He who should have guarded you has betrayed you.

avoir.of: Quant l'ot Rollant, Deus! si grant doel en out!
avoir.tr: When Roland heard it, God! such great grief he had!

avoir.of: En dous meitiez li ad briset le col;
avoir.tr: In two halves he has broken his neck;

avoir.of: Nos avum dreit, mais cist glutun unt tort.» AOI.
avoir.tr: We have the right, but these gluttons are in the wrong.» AOI.

avoir.of: Un duc i est, si ad num Falsaron:
avoir.tr: There is a duke there, named Falsaron:

avoir.of: Suz cel nen at plus encrisme felun.
avoir.tr: Under heaven there is no more wicked villain.

avoir.of: Entre les dous oilz mult out large le front,
avoir.tr: Between his two eyes he had a very broad forehead,

avoir.of: Asez ad doel quant vit mort sun nevold,
avoir.tr: He has great grief when he sees his nephew dead,

avoir.of: Ot le Oliver, sin ad mult grant irur;
avoir.tr: Oliver hears him, and feels very great wrath at it;

avoir.of: Si li ad dit par mult fiere raison:
avoir.tr: And spoke to him in most fierce speech:

avoir.of: «De voz manaces, culvert, jo n'ai essoign.
avoir.tr: «Of your threats, scoundrel, I have no care.

avoir.of: Uns reis i est, si ad num Corsablix:
avoir.tr: A king is there, and his name is Corsablix:

avoir.of: Kar de Franceis i ad asez petit.
avoir.tr: For of the Franks there are quite few here.

avoir.of: Cels ki ci sunt devum aveir mult vil;
avoir.tr: Those who are here we should hold very cheap;

avoir.of: Suz ciel n'at hume que [tant] voeillet haïr;
avoir.tr: Under heaven there is no man he would so wish to hate;

avoir.of: «Culvert paien, vos i avez mentit!
avoir.tr: «Vile pagan, you have lied in this!

avoir.of: Nostre Franceis n'unt talent de fuïr.
avoir.tr: Our Franks have no wish to flee.

avoir.of: De sun osberc li ad rumput les pans,
avoir.tr: He has burst open the skirts of his hauberk,

avoir.of: Apres li dist: «Ja n'i avrez guarant!»
avoir.tr: Afterward he says to him: «You shall have no protection here!»

avoir.of: Ne mes que dous n'en i ad remes vifs;
avoir.tr: No more than two of them have remained alive there;

avoir.of: Ultre s'en vait, qu'il n'i ad desturber;
avoir.tr: He passes on beyond, for there is nothing to stop him;

avoir.of: A .XV. cols l'ad fraite e [...] perdue,
avoir.tr: At fifteen blows he has broken and lost it,

avoir.of: Trenchet l'eschine, hunc n'i out quis [joi]nture,
avoir.tr: He cuts through the spine, never seeking a joint there,

avoir.of: De Mahumet ja n'i avrez aiude.
avoir.tr: From Mahomet you will never have help there.

avoir.of: Sanglant en ad e l'osberc e [la] brace,
avoir.tr: He has blood on both his hauberk and his arm,

avoir.of: Li .XII. per n'en deivent aveir blasme,
avoir.tr: The Twelve Peers ought to have no blame for it,

avoir.of: Dist l'arcevesque: «Ben ait nostre barnage!»
avoir.tr: Said the archbishop: «Blessed be our baronage!»

avoir.of: Sa hanste est frait, n'en ad que un trunçun,
avoir.tr: His lance is broken, he has but one stump of it,

avoir.of: Pois ad ocis Turgis e Esturguz;
avoir.tr: Then he has slain Turgis and Esturguz;

avoir.of: En tel bataille n'ai cure de bastun;
avoir.tr: In such a battle I have no care for a stick;

avoir.of: Fers e acers i deit aveir valor.
avoir.tr: Iron and steel ought to be of worth here.

avoir.of: U est vostre espee, ki Halteclere ad num?
avoir.tr: Where is your sword, which is named Halteclere?

avoir.of: «Kar de ferir oi jo si grant bosoign.» AOI.
avoir.tr: «For I had such great need to strike.» AOI.

avoir.of: Danz Oliver trait ad sa bone espee,
avoir.tr: Lord Oliver has drawn his good sword,

avoir.of: Que ses cumpainz Rollant li ad tant demandee,
avoir.tr: Which his companion Roland had so much asked of him,

avoir.of: E il li ad cum chevaler mustree.
avoir.tr: And he has shown it to him in knightly fashion.

avoir.of: Tute la teste li ad par mi sevree,
avoir.tr: He has cut his whole head clean in two,

avoir.of: Lur dous espiez enz el cors li unt frait,
avoir.tr: Their two spears they have shattered in his body,

avoir.of: Tant hanste i ad e fraite e sanglente,
avoir.tr: So many a lance there is, both shattered and bloody,

avoir.of: De ço qui calt? N'en avrunt sucurance.
avoir.tr: What does it matter? They will have no help from it.

avoir.of: Ki de murir nen ourent esperance. AOI.
avoir.tr: Who had no hope of escaping death. AOI.

avoir.of: Ki ne s'en fuit de mort n'i ad guarent;
avoir.tr: Whoever does not flee has no protection from death there;

avoir.of: En France en ad mult merveillus turment;
avoir.tr: In France there is a most marvelous storm;

avoir.of: Orez i ad de tuneire e de vent,
avoir.tr: There is a tempest of thunder and of wind,

avoir.of: E terremoete ço i ad veirement.
avoir.tr: And there is an earthquake there in truth.

avoir.of: N'en ad recet dunt del mur ne cravent.
avoir.tr: There is no dwelling whose wall does not crumble.

avoir.of: Cuntre midi tenebres i ad granz;
avoir.tr: Around midday there are great shadows there;

avoir.of: N'i ad clartet, se li ciels nen i fent.
avoir.tr: There is no light, unless the sky splits open.

avoir.of: Franceis i unt ferut de coer e de vigur;
avoir.tr: The French have struck there with heart and with vigor;

avoir.of: Suz ciel n'ad home plus en ait de meillors.»
avoir.tr: Under heaven there is no one who has more of the best.»

avoir.of: Que vassals ad li nostre empereür.
avoir.tr: That valiant men our emperor has.

avoir.of: Od sa grant ost que il out asemblee.
avoir.tr: With his great host that he had assembled.

avoir.of: .XX. escheles ad li reis anumbrees.
avoir.tr: The king has counted twenty battalions.

avoir.of: Guenes li fels ad nostre mort juree;
avoir.tr: Ganelon the traitor has sworn our death;

avoir.of: Bataille avrum e forte [e] aduree,
avoir.tr: We shall have a battle, hard and grueling,

avoir.of: En tanz lius les avum nos portees!
avoir.tr: In so many places have we borne them!

avoir.of: Tantes batailles en avum afinees!
avoir.tr: So many battles have we ended with them!

avoir.of: Plus fel de lui n'out en sa cumpagnie.
avoir.tr: There was none more wicked than he in his company.

avoir.of: Te(t)ches ad males e mult granz felonies;
avoir.tr: He has evil traits and very great villainies;

avoir.of: Vasselage ad e mult grant estultie:
avoir.tr: He has valor and very great rashness:

avoir.of: Piez ad copiez e les gambes ad plates,
avoir.tr: It has hollow hooves and flat legs,

avoir.of: Lungs les costez e l'eschine ad ben halte,
avoir.tr: Long the flanks and the spine very high,

avoir.of: Pierres i ad, ametistes e topazes,
avoir.tr: There are stones, amethysts and topazes,

avoir.of: Dient Franceis: «Ci ad grant vasselage!
avoir.tr: The French say: «Here is great valor!

avoir.of: Franceis veient que paiens i ad tant,
avoir.tr: The French see that there are so many pagans there,

avoir.of: Cel nen i ad «Munjoie!» ne demant. AOI.
avoir.tr: There is not one who does not cry «Munjoie!» AOI.

avoir.of: Un Sarrazin i out de Sarraguce,
avoir.tr: There was a Saracen there from Saragossa,

avoir.of: Nus n'avium plus vaillant chevaler.»
avoir.tr: We had no more valiant knight.»

avoir.of: Puis ad ocis le duc Alphaïen;
avoir.tr: Then he has slain Duke Alphaïen;

avoir.of: Escababi i ad le chef trenchet;
avoir.tr: He has cut off the head of Escababi;

avoir.of: .VII. Arrabiz i ad deschevalcet:
avoir.tr: He has unhorsed seven Arabs:

avoir.of: Pur itels colps nos ad Charles plus cher.»
avoir.tr: For such blows Charles holds us the dearer.»

avoir.of: N'i ad eschipre quis cleim se par loi nun.
avoir.tr: There is no sailor who does not claim him as his rightful master.

avoir.of: Cil ot fiance del cunte Guenelon:
avoir.tr: This man had the pledge of Count Ganelon:

avoir.of: Poez saveir que mult grant doel en out.
avoir.tr: You may be sure that he felt very great grief at it.

avoir.of: D'Affrike i ad un Affrican venut,
avoir.tr: From Africa there has come an African,

avoir.of: De sun osberc li ad les pans rumput,
avoir.tr: From his hauberk he has torn away the skirts,

avoir.of: Morz est li quens, de sun tens n'i ad plus.
avoir.tr: The count is dead, of his time there is no more.

avoir.of: Tel ad ocis dunt al coer me regrette.»
avoir.tr: You have slain one for whom my heart grieves.»

avoir.of: Sun bon ceval i ad fait esdemetre,
avoir.tr: He spurred his good horse to leap forward,

avoir.of: Si l'ad ferut sur l'escut de Tulette,
avoir.tr: And struck him upon the shield of Toledo,

avoir.of: Aprof li ad sa bronie desclose,
avoir.tr: Then he has ripped open his hauberk,

avoir.of: Il l'abat mort; paien en unt grant joie.
avoir.tr: He strikes him dead; the pagans have great joy of it.

avoir.of: Ben ad oït que Franceis se dementent;
avoir.tr: He has clearly heard that the Franks are lamenting;

avoir.of: Si grant doel ad que par mi quiet fendre;
avoir.tr: He has such great grief that he thinks he will split apart;

avoir.of: Enmi sa veie ad encuntret Rollant.
avoir.tr: In the middle of his path he encountered Roland.

avoir.of: Al fier visage e al cors qu'il out gent
avoir.tr: By his fierce face and his noble body

avoir.of: Cel nen i ad ki ne criet: «Marsilie!
avoir.tr: There is not one who does not cry out: «Marsile!

avoir.of: Cevalche, rei! Bosuign avum d'aïe!»
avoir.tr: Ride, king! We have need of aid!»

avoir.of: Nen ad meillor en tere ne suz cel;
avoir.tr: There is none better on earth nor under heaven;

avoir.of: A icest mot l'unt Francs recumencet.
avoir.tr: At this word the Franks renewed the fight.

avoir.of: Mu(n)lt grant dulor i ad de chrestiens.
avoir.tr: Very great suffering there is among the Christians.

avoir.of: Cels qu'il unt mort, ben les poet hom preiser;
avoir.tr: those whom they have slain, one can well count them;

avoir.of: Ne mes seisante, que Deus i ad esparniez:
avoir.tr: all but sixty, whom God has spared:

avoir.of: Ja avez vos ambsdous les braz sanglanz!»
avoir.tr: Already you have both your arms bloodied!»

avoir.of: Respont li quens: «Colps i ai fait mult genz!» AOI.
avoir.tr: The count replied: «I have struck blows most fine!» AOI.

avoir.of: Si fust li reis, n'i oüsum damage.
avoir.tr: Had the king been here, we would have suffered no harm.

avoir.of: Cil ki la sunt n'en deivent aveir blasme.»
avoir.tr: Those who are over there ought not bear blame for it.»

avoir.of: Jamais Karlon de nus n'avrat servise.
avoir.tr: Never again will Charles have service from us.

avoir.of: Ceste bataille oüsum faite u prise;
avoir.tr: This battle we would have fought and won;

avoir.of: Karles li Magnes de nos n'avrat aïe.
avoir.tr: Charlemagne will have no help from us.

avoir.of: Ja li corners ne nos avreit mester,
avoir.tr: The sounding of the horn would now be of no use to us,

avoir.of: Rollant ad mis l'olifan a sa buche,
avoir.tr: Roland has put the oliphant to his mouth,

avoir.of: Li quens Rollant ad la buche sanglente.
avoir.tr: Count Roland has a bloody mouth.

avoir.of: Ço dist li reis: «cCel corn ad lunge aleine!»
avoir.tr: The king said: «That horn has long breath!»

avoir.of: Bataille i ad, par le men escientre.
avoir.tr: There is a battle, by my knowledge.

avoir.of: Cil l'at traït ki vos en roevet feindre.
avoir.tr: He who asks you to feign indifference has betrayed him.

avoir.of: Li empereres ad fait suner ses corns.
avoir.tr: The emperor has had his horns sounded.

avoir.of: Escuz unt genz e espiez granz e forz,
avoir.tr: They have fine shields and great strong spears,

avoir.of: N'i ad celoi (a celoi) a l'altre ne parolt:
avoir.tr: There is not one of them but speaks to the other:

avoir.of: De ço qui calt? car demuret i unt trop.
avoir.tr: What does it matter? For they have tarried too long.

avoir.of: N'i ad celoi ki durement ne plurt,
avoir.tr: There is none among them who does not weep bitterly,

avoir.of: De ma maisnee ad faite traïsun.»
avoir.tr: He has committed treason against my house.»

avoir.of: Sur un sumer l'unt mis a deshonor.
avoir.tr: On a pack-horse they have set him in dishonor.

avoir.of: N'i ad celoi n'i plurt e se dement,
avoir.tr: There is not one of them who does not weep and lament,

avoir.of: N'i ad icel ne demeint irance
avoir.tr: There is not one of them but feels grief

avoir.of: Deus! quels seisante humes i ad en sa cumpaigne!
avoir.tr: God! what sixty men he has in his company!

avoir.of: Unches meillurs n'en out reis ne c[at]aignes. AOI.
avoir.tr: Never did king or captain have better. AOI.

avoir.of: «Seignors barons, de vos ait Deus mercit!
avoir.tr: «Lord barons, may God have mercy on you!

avoir.of: Si lungement tuz tens m'avez servit,
avoir.tr: So long, through all time, you have served me,

avoir.of: Faldrun de Pui i ad par mi trenchet,
avoir.tr: He has cut Faldrun of Pui clean through the middle,

avoir.of: Itel valor deit aveir chevaler
avoir.tr: Such valor a knight ought to have

avoir.of: A icest mot l'unt Francs recumencet.
avoir.tr: At this word the Franks have begun again.

avoir.of: Mult grant damage i out de chrestiens.
avoir.tr: Very great was the loss there among the Christians.

avoir.of: Home ki ço set, que ja n'avrat prisun
avoir.tr: A man who knows this, that he will never be taken prisoner,

avoir.of: Puis ad ocis Yvoeries e Ivon
avoir.tr: then he has killed Yvoire and Yvon,

avoir.of: Colp en avras einz que nos departum,
avoir.tr: You will have a blow before we part,

avoir.of: Trenchet li ad li quens le destre poign.
avoir.tr: the count has cut off his right fist.

avoir.of: En ceste tere nus ad mis tels feluns!
avoir.tr: He has set such villains in this land!

avoir.of: La neire gent en ad en sa baillie;
avoir.tr: He has the black people under his command;

avoir.of: Granz unt les nes e lees les oreilles,
avoir.tr: They have large noses and broad ears,

avoir.of: E or sai ben n'avons guaires a vivre;
avoir.tr: And now I know well we have not much longer to live;

avoir.of: Ne n'unt de blanc ne mais que sul les denz,
avoir.tr: And have nothing white except only their teeth,

avoir.of: Dist Oliver: «Dehet ait li plus lenz!»
avoir.tr: Oliver said: «Cursed be the slowest!»

avoir.of: Quant paien virent que Franceis i out poi,
avoir.tr: When the pagans saw that the French were few there,

avoir.of: Entr'els en unt e orgoil e cunfort.
avoir.tr: They take pride and comfort among themselves.

avoir.of: Dist l'un a l'altre: «L'empereor ad tort.»
avoir.tr: One said to the other: «The emperor is in the wrong.»

avoir.of: Le blanc osberc li ad descust el cors,
avoir.tr: He has split open the white hauberk against his body,

avoir.of: E dit apres: «Un col avez pris fort!
avoir.tr: And said after: «You have taken a hard blow!

avoir.of: Tort nos ad fait: nen est dreiz qu'il s'en lot,
avoir.tr: He has wronged us: it is not right that he should boast of it,

avoir.of: Kar de vos sul ai ben venget les noz.»
avoir.tr: For through you alone I have well avenged our men.»

avoir.of: Brandist sun colp, si l'ad mort abatut,
avoir.tr: He brandishes his blow, and has struck him dead,

avoir.of: E dist apres: «Paien mal aies tu!
avoir.tr: And said afterward: «Pagan, a curse upon you!

avoir.of: Iço ne di que Karles n'i ait perdut;
avoir.tr: I do not say that Charles has lost nothing here;

avoir.of: Ne a muiler ne a dame qu'aies veüd,
avoir.tr: But neither to wife nor to lady whom you have seen

avoir.of: Vaillant a un dener que m'i aies tolut,
avoir.tr: That you took from me a single denier's worth,

avoir.of: Li emperere en avrat grant damage.»
avoir.tr: The emperor will have great harm from it.»

avoir.of: Tant ad seinet (ki) li oil li sunt trublet;
avoir.tr: He has bled so much that his eyes are clouded;

avoir.of: Sun cumpaignun, cum il l'at encontret,
avoir.tr: His companion, as he has encountered him,

avoir.of: Mais en la teste ne l'ad mie adeset.
avoir.tr: But he has not at all touched the head.

avoir.of: A icel colp l'ad Rollant reguardet,
avoir.tr: At that blow Roland has looked at him,

avoir.of: Par nule guise ne m'aviez desfiet!»
avoir.tr: In no way had you defied me!»

avoir.of: Ferut vos ai, car le me pardunez!»
avoir.tr: I have struck you, so forgive me for it!»

avoir.of: Rollant respunt: «Jo n'ai nient de mal.
avoir.tr: Roland answers: «I have no hurt at all.

avoir.of: A icel mot l'un a l'altre ad clinet.
avoir.tr: At that word the one bowed to the other.

avoir.of: Ensemble avum estet e anz e dis;
avoir.tr: Together we have been for years and days;

avouer.of: La vos sivrat, ço dit mis avoez.»
avouer.tr: there he will follow you, so says my lord.»

bailler.tr: The caliph said: «You have served us ill,

bailler.of: «Carle me mandet, ki France ad en baillie,
bailler.tr: «Charles, who has France in his keeping, commands me,

baiser.of: Quan l'ot Marsilie, si l'ad baiset el col,
baiser.tr: When Marsile heard this, he kissed him on the neck,

baiser.of: Puis se baiserent es vis e es mentuns.
baiser.tr: Then they kissed each other on the faces and on the chins.

baiser.of: Puis se baiserent es buches e es vis. AOI.
baiser.tr: Then they kissed each other on the mouths and on the faces. AOI.

baiser.of: Par amistiet l'en baisat en la buche,
baiser.tr: In friendship kissed him on the mouth,

baisser.of: Baisset sun chef, si cumencet a penser. AOI.
baisser.tr: he bows his head, and begins to think. AOI.

balayer.of: Jusqu'a la tere si chevoel li balient;
balayer.tr: His hair sweeps down to the ground;

battre.of: Les renges li batent josqu'as mains.
battre.tr: The streamers beat down to his hands.

battre.of: Si guarnement sunt tut a or batud;
battre.tr: His trappings are all beaten with gold;

battre.of: Ben le batirent a fuz e a bastuns;
battre.tr: They beat him soundly with rods and with sticks;

blesser.of: La gent de France iert blecee e blesmie;
blesser.tr: The host of France will be wounded and bruised;

blesser.of: Si est blecet, ne quit que anme i remaigne.
blesser.tr: He is so wounded, I do not think a soul remains in him.

blâmer.of: De l'algalifes nel devez pas blasmer,
blâmer.tr: Concerning the caliph you must not blame him,

blâmer.of: Que mi parent pur mei seient blasmet
blâmer.tr: That my kinsmen be blamed because of me

blâmer.of: Cil ki la sunt ne funt mie a blasmer.
blâmer.tr: Those who are there are not at all to be blamed.

blâmer.of: Ambure ocit, ki quel blasme ne quil lot.
blâmer.tr: He kills them both, let who will blame or praise it.

blêmir.of: La gent de France iert blecee e blesmie;
blêmir.tr: The host of France will be wounded and bruised;

bouter.of: Il les ad prises, en sa hoese les butet, AOI.
bouter.tr: He took them, into his boot he stuffed them, AOI.

brandir.of: Quant l'oït Guenes, l'espee en ad branlie;
brandir.tr: When Ganelon heard this, he brandished his sword;

brandir.of: Par tel air l'at estrussee e brandie,
brandir.tr: With such force he wrenched and brandished it,

brandir.of: Enpeint le ben, fait li brandir le cors,
brandir.tr: Drives it home well, makes the body sway,

brandir.of: Empeint le ben, que mort le fait brandir,
brandir.tr: Drives it well in, so that he makes him reel dead,

brandir.of: Brandist sun colp e li Sarrazins chiet;
brandir.tr: He brandishes his blow and the Saracen falls;

brandir.of: Brandist sun colp, si l'ad mort abatut,
brandir.tr: He brandishes his blow, and has struck him dead,

briser.of: Trenchet le piz, si li briset les os,
briser.tr: Slashes his chest, and breaks his bones,

briser.of: En dous meitiez li ad briset le col;
briser.tr: In two halves he has broken his neck;

briser.of: La hanste briset e esclicet josqu'as poinz.
briser.tr: His lance breaks and splinters all the way to his fists.

brocher.of: Sun cheval broche e muntet un lariz,
brocher.tr: He spurs his horse and climbs a hillock,

brocher.of: Brochent ad ait pur le plus tost aler,
brocher.tr: They spur hard to go all the faster,

brocher.of: Sun cheval brochet, laiset curre a esforz,
brocher.tr: He spurs his horse, lets it run at full force,

brocher.of: Le cheval brochet des oriez esperuns,
brocher.tr: He spurs his horse with his golden spurs,

brocher.of: Sun cheval brochet des esperuns d'or fin,
brocher.tr: He spurs his horse with the spurs of fine gold,

brocher.of: Sun cheval (...) brochet, si li laschet la resne,
brocher.tr: Spurs his horse, lets loose the rein,

brocher.of: Le cheval brochet, vait ferir Oliver:
brocher.tr: He spurs his horse, goes to strike Oliver:

brocher.of: Sun cheval brochet, si vait ferir Chernuble:
brocher.tr: He spurs his horse, and goes to strike Chernuble:

brocher.of: Laschent lor reisnes, brochent amdui a ait,
brocher.tr: They slacken their reins, both spur eagerly,

brocher.of: Li arcevesque brochet par tant grant vasselage:
brocher.tr: The archbishop spurs with such great valor:

brocher.of: Brochet le bien, le frein li abandunet,
brocher.tr: He spurs it hard, gives it free rein,

brocher.of: Sun cheval brochet des esperuns d'or mier,
brocher.tr: He spurs his horse with spurs of pure gold,

brocher.of: Brochet le bien des aguz esperuns,
brocher.tr: He spurs it well with sharp spurs,

brocher.of: Sun ceval brochet, si li curt ad esforz;
brocher.tr: He spurs his horse, and rushes at him with all his might;

brocher.of: Laschet la resne, des esperuns le brochet,
brocher.tr: He slackens the rein, spurs it with his spurs,

brocher.of: Sun ceval brochet, ki oït del cuntence.
brocher.tr: He spurs his horse, which heard the dispute.

brocher.of: Le cheval brochet des esperuns d'or mer,
brocher.tr: He spurs his horse with the spurs of pure gold,

brocher.of: Brochent ad ait tant cum durent li port.
brocher.tr: They spur eagerly as far as the passes extend.

brocher.of: Brochet le ben, si vait ferir Bevon,
brocher.tr: spurs it well, and goes to strike Beuve,

brocher.of: Brochet le ben des esperuns a or,
brocher.tr: He spurs it hard with his spurs of gold,

bénir.of: E l'arcevesque de Deu les beneïst:
bénir.tr: And the archbishop blessed them in God's name:

bénir.of: Ne lesserat que nos ne beneïsse.» AOI.
bénir.tr: He will not fail to bless us.» AOI.

bénir.of: E beneïst Karlun e France dulce,
bénir.tr: And to bless Charles and sweet France,

ceindre.of: Ceint Murglies, s'espee, a sun costed;
ceindre.tr: He girds Murgleis, his sword, at his side;

ceindre.of: Ceintes espees as punz d'or neielez,
ceindre.tr: Girt with swords with pommels of nielloed gold,

ceindre.of: Healmes lacez e ceintes lur espees,
ceindre.tr: helmets laced and their swords girded,

ceindre.of: Ce dist Chernubles: «Ma bone espee ai ceinte;
ceindre.tr: Chernuble says: «My good sword I have girded on;
They gird on swords of Viennese steel;

ceindre.of: Ma bone espee que ai ceint al costet:
ceindre.tr: My good sword that I have girded at my side:

celer.of: La traïsun ne poet estre celee;

chaloir.of: Ne li chalt, sire, de quel mort nus muriuns.
chaloir.tr: Cares not, sire, by what death we may die.

chaloir.of: De ço qui calt? N'en avrunt sucurance.
chaloir.tr: What does it matter? They will have no help from it.

chaloir.of: De ço qui calt? car demuret i unt trop.
chaloir.tr: What does it matter? For they have tarried too long.

chaloir.of: De ço qui calt? car ne lur valt nient.
chaloir.tr: But what use is it? It avails them nothing.

chaloir.of: De ço qui calt? Se fuit s'en est Marsilies,
chaloir.tr: What does it matter? Marsile has fled,

chanter.of: Que malvaise cançun de nus chantet ne seit!
chanter.tr: So that no evil song be sung about us!

chanter.of: Male chançun n'en deit estre cantee.» AOI.
chanter.tr: Let no evil song be sung about it.» AOI.

chanter.of: Que nuls prozdom malvaisement n'en chant.
chanter.tr: So that no worthy man may sing ill of it.

chanter.of: Tel coronet ne chantat unches messe
chanter.tr: Never did such a tonsured man chant a mass

charger.tr: Four hundred mules laden with gold and silver,

charger.of: Quatre cenz mulz cargez del ór d'Arabe,
charger.tr: Four hundred mules laden with the gold of Arabia,
seven hundred camels, laden with gold and silver,

charger.of: .X. muls cargez del plus fin or d'Arabe;
charger.tr: Ten mules laden with the finest gold of Arabia;

charrier.tr: Fifty carts, that he will have carried away:

charrier.of: Cinquante care, que carier en ferez;
charrier.tr: fifty carts, which you will have carted away;

chevaucher.of: Guenes chevalchet suz une olive halte,
chevaucher.tr: Ganelon rides beneath a tall olive tree,

chevaucher.of: Tant chevalcherent Guenes e Blancandrins,
chevaucher.tr: So long did Ganelon and Blancandrin ride together,

chevaucher.of: Tant chevalcherent e veies e chemins,
chevaucher.tr: So long did they ride, both byways and roads,

chevaucher.of: Ne mul ne mule que puissez chevalcher;
chevaucher.tr: Nor mule nor she-mule that you may ride;

chevaucher.of: Vers dulce France chevalchet l'emperere.
chevaucher.tr: Toward sweet France the emperor rides.

chevaucher.of: Paien chevalchent par cez greignurs valees,
chevaucher.tr: The pagans ride through these larger valleys,

chevaucher.of: Li empereres mult fierement chevalchet.
chevaucher.tr: The emperor rides very proudly.

chevaucher.of: Ne mul ne mule que deiet chevalcher,
chevaucher.tr: Nor mule nor she-mule that one ought to ride,

chevaucher.of: Dejuste lui li dux Neimes chevalchet
chevaucher.tr: Beside him Duke Naimes rides

chevaucher.of: Puis si chevalchent, par mult grant cuntençun,
chevaucher.tr: Then they ride forth, with very great striving,

chevaucher.of: Es destrers muntent, si chevalchent estreiz.
chevaucher.tr: They mount their war-horses and ride in close ranks.

chevaucher.of: Felun paien par grant irur chevalchent.
chevaucher.tr: The wicked pagans ride with great fury.

chevaucher.of: Kar chevalchez a quanque vos puez!
chevaucher.tr: So ride on with all the speed you can!

chevaucher.of: Puis si chevalchent, Deus! par si grant fiertet!
chevaucher.tr: Then they ride on, God! with such great fierceness!

chevaucher.of: Tut premereins chevalchet devant l'ost.
chevaucher.tr: First of all he rides out before the host.

chevaucher.of: Li quens Rollant par mi le champ chevalchet,
chevaucher.tr: Count Roland rides through the middle of the field,

chevaucher.of: E Oliver chevalchet par l'estor,
chevaucher.tr: And Oliver rides through the fray,

chevaucher.of: Puis si chevalchet od sa grant ost banie.
chevaucher.tr: Then he rides forth with his great mustered host.

chevaucher.of: Devant chevalchet un Sarrasin, Abisme:
chevaucher.tr: Out in front rides a Saracen, Abisme:

chevaucher.of: Cevalche, rei! Bosuign avum d'aïe!»
chevaucher.tr: Ride, king! We have need of aid!»

chevaucher.of: Car chevalcez! Pur qu'alez arestant?
chevaucher.tr: So ride on! Why do you keep stopping?

chevaucher.of: Li empereres cevalchet par irur
chevaucher.tr: The emperor rides on in wrath

chevaucher.of: Li empereres chevalchet ireement,
chevaucher.tr: The emperor rides on in wrath,

chevaucher.of: Par grant irur chevalchet li reis Charles;
chevaucher.tr: In great wrath rides King Charles;

chevaucher.of: Icil chevalchent fierement e a íre,
chevaucher.tr: These men ride fiercely and with wrath,

choir.of: Quant le dut prendre, si li caït a tere.
choir.tr: When he had to take it, it fell from him to the ground.

choir.of: Carlles verrat sun grant orguill cadeir;
choir.tr: Charles will see his great pride fall;

choir.of: Qui[d]ás, le guant me caïst en la place,
choir.tr: Did you think the glove would fall to me on the spot,

choir.of: Que il me chedet, cum fist a Guenelun
choir.tr: that it should fall from me, as it happened to Ganelon

choir.of: Pluie n'i chet, rusee n'i adeiset,
choir.tr: No rain falls there, no dew settles there,

choir.of: Ne France dulce ja cheet en viltet!
choir.tr: Nor sweet France ever fall into disgrace!

choir.of: Li paiens chet cuntreval a un quat;
choir.tr: The pagan falls down dead at one blow;

choir.of: E la cervele li chet as piez desuz;
choir.tr: And his brains fall down at his feet;

choir.of: Chiedent i fuildres e menut e suvent,
choir.tr: Thunderbolts fall there, both small and frequent,

choir.of: Brandist sun colp e li Sarrazins chiet;
choir.tr: He brandishes his blow and the Saracen falls;

choir.of: Encuntre tere en cheent les esclaces.
choir.tr: The clots of it fall to the ground.

choir.of: Quel part qu'il alt, ne poet mie chaïr.
choir.tr: Wherever he may go, he cannot fall.

châtier.of: Vint tresqu'a els, sis prist a castier:
châtier.tr: Came right up to them, and began to rebuke them:

cingler.tr: Before they had sailed four leagues,

clamer.of: Noble vassal vos i solt hom clamer.
clamer.tr: A noble vassal one was wont to call you there.

clamer.of: Clamez vos culpes, si preiez Deu mercit!
clamer.tr: Confess your sins, and pray to God for mercy!

clamer.of: E cil de France le cleiment a guarant.
clamer.tr: And those of France acclaim him as their protector.

clamer.of: Siet el ceval qu'il cleimet Barbamusche,
clamer.tr: He sits on the horse he calls Barbamusche,

clamer.of: N'i ad eschipre quis cleim se par loi nun.
clamer.tr: There is no sailor who does not claim him as his rightful master.

clamer.of: Siet el cheval qu'il cleimet Gramimund,
clamer.tr: He sits on the horse he calls Gramimund,

clamer.of: Siet el ceval qu'il cleimet Salt Perdut:
clamer.tr: He sits on the horse that he calls Salt Perdut:

clamer.of: Siet el cheval que il cleimet Marmorie,
clamer.tr: He sits on the horse that he calls Marmorie,

clamer.of: E cil d'Espaigne s'en cleiment tuit dolent.
clamer.tr: And those of Spain lament over it, all grieving.

clamer.of: Sur sun ceval que cleimet Veillantif.
clamer.tr: Upon his horse that is called Veillantif.

combattre.of: Puis m'en cumbatre a Carlles e a Franceis?»
combattre.tr: Can I then fight against Charles and the Franks?»

combattre.of: Cumbatrat sei a trestute sa gent,
combattre.tr: He will fight him with all his people,
Furiously it fights with the leopard.
And I will fight the twelve companions.»

combattre.of: Asez est mielz que moerium cumbatant.
combattre.tr: It is much better that we die fighting.
And mighty and a battling vassal.

combattre.of: Unc nel sunast se ne fust (cu)cumbatant.»
combattre.tr: He would never sound it unless he were fighting.»

combattre.of: Sis cumbatirent al bon vassal Rollant;
combattre.tr: And they fought the good vassal Roland;

combattre.of: Ki se cumbat as Sarrazins d'Espaigne;
combattre.tr: Who fights against the Saracens of Spain;

commander.of: N'en parlez mais, se jo nel vos cumant!» AOI.
commander.tr: Speak of it no more, unless I command you to!» AOI.

commander.of: Carles comandet que face sun servise:
commander.tr: Charles commands that I perform his service:

commander.of: Puisquel comant, aler vus en estoet.»
commander.tr: Since I command it, you must go.»

commander.of: Or irez vos certes, quant jol cumant.»
commander.tr: Now you will go indeed, since I command it.»

commander.of: Jointes ses mains iert vostre comandet;
commander.tr: With joined hands he will be at your command;

commander.of: Par penitence les cumandet a ferir.
commander.tr: As penance he commands them to strike.

commander.of: Sil cumandat as cous de sa maisun.
commander.tr: And handed him over to the cooks of his household.

commencer.of: Baisset sun chef, si cumencet a penser. AOI.
commencer.tr: he bows his head, and begins to think. AOI.

commencer.of: Des ore cumencet le cunseill que mal prist. AOI.
commencer.tr: From now begins the counsel that came to ill. AOI.
Very fiercely he begins his speech,

commencer.of: Quant l'ot Rollant, si cumençat a rire. AOI.
commencer.tr: When Roland heard it, he began to laugh. AOI.

commencer.of: Par grant saver cumencet a parler
commencer.tr: With great wisdom he begins to speak,

commencer.of: Puis si cumencet a venir ses tresors. AOI.
commencer.tr: Then he begins to bring forth his treasures. AOI.

commencer.of: Par grant veisdie cumencet a parler,
commencer.tr: With great cunning he begins to speak,

comparer.of: Einz vos avrunt li meillor comperee.»
comparer.tr: Before the best men shall have paid dearly for you.»

comparer.of: Ki quel cumpert, venuz en sunt ensemble.
comparer.tr: Whoever pays for it, they have come together.

conduire.of: Ne nus seiuns cunduiz a mendeier.» AOI.
conduire.tr: And that we be reduced to begging.» AOI.

conduire.of: Tanz riches reis cunduit a mendisted:
conduire.tr: So many rich kings has he led to beggary:

conduire.of: Tanz riches reis cunduiz a mendistiet:
conduire.tr: so many rich kings he has brought to beggary:

conduire.of: Ki l'en cunduistrent tresqu'en la mer:
conduire.tr: Who escorted him all the way to the sea:
«I will lead my body into Roncevaux;

conduire.of: Si aiderez a cunduire ma gent.»
conduire.tr: And you will help to conduct my people.»

conduire.of: Lez le costet li conduist sun espiet.
conduire.tr: Along his side his spear passes.
By magic Jupiter led him there.

confondre.of: En cest païs nos est venuz cunfundre.
confondre.tr: Has come into this land to destroy us.

confondre.of: Lui e altrui travaillent, e cunfundent.»
confondre.tr: They torment him and others, and bring ruin.»

confondre.of: Li soens orgoilz le devreit ben cunfundre,
confondre.tr: His own pride ought well to bring him to ruin,

confondre.of: Deus me cunfunde, se la geste en desment!
confondre.tr: May God confound me, if I belie my lineage!
Then he cries out: «These are good to destroy!
