connaître.of: N'est hom kil veit e conuistre le set
connaître.tr: There is no man who sees him and comes to know him

conquérir.of: Tresqu'en la mer cunquist la tere altaigne.
conquérir.tr: he conquered the high land all the way to the sea.

conquérir.of: Jo vos cunquis e Noples e Commibles,
conquérir.tr: I conquered for you both Noples and Commibles,

conquérir.of: Ki cunquist Puille e trestute Calabre;
conquérir.tr: Who conquered Apulia and all of Calabria;

conquérir.of: Ad oes seint Perre en cunquist le chevage:
conquérir.tr: For Saint Peter's use he won from it the tribute:

conquérir.of: Cunquerrat li les teres d'ici qu'en Orient.» AOI.
conquérir.tr: He will conquer lands for them from here to the Orient.» AOI.

conquérir.of: Par tantes teres est alet cunquerant,
conquérir.tr: Through so many lands has he gone conquering,

conquérir.of: Si cunquerrai Durendal od la meie.
conquérir.tr: And I shall conquer Durendal with my own blade.

conquérir.of: A oes Carlon si granz païs cunquis!
conquérir.tr: and for Charles's use conquered such great lands!

conseiller.of: Cunseilez mei cume mi savie hume,
conseiller.tr: Counsel me, as my wise men,

consentir.of: Dist al paien: «Deus tut mal te consente!
consentir.tr: He said to the pagan: «May God grant you every evil!

conter.of: E Blancandrins, por la raisun cunter.
conter.tr: And Blancandrin, to set forth the matter.

contrarier.of: Pur Deu vos pri, ne vos cuntraliez!
contrarier.tr: For God's sake I pray you, do not quarrel!

contredire.of: En piez se drecet, si li vint cuntredire.
contredire.tr: Rises to his feet, and came to contradict him.

convenir.of: Dient Franceis: «Il nus i cuvent guarde!» AOI.
convenir.tr: The Franks say: «We must be on our guard in this!» AOI.

corner.of: Ne pur paien, que ja seie cornant!
corner.tr: Nor for any pagan, that I should ever sound the horn!
Roland said: «I will sound the olifant,

corner.of: Se vos cornez, n'ert mie hardement.
corner.tr: If you sound the horn, it will be no act of valor.

corner.of: Jo cornerai, si l'orrat li reis Karles.»
corner.tr: I will sound the horn, and King Charles will hear it.»

corner.of: Pur un sul levre vat tute jur cornant,
corner.tr: For a single hare he goes blowing his horn all day,

corroyer.tr: Twelve sergeants have well equipped them.

corroyer.of: De guarnemenz se prent a cunreer,
corroyer.tr: He sets about equipping himself with gear,

coucher.of: Sur un perrun de marbre bloi se culchet,
coucher.tr: Upon a block of blue-grey marble he lies down,

coucher.of: Descent a piet, a l[a] tere se culchet,
coucher.tr: He dismounts on foot, lies down on the ground,

courir.of: Plus curt a piet que ne fait un cheval.
courir.tr: He runs faster on foot than a horse does.

courir.of: Curant i vint Margariz de Sibilie;
courir.tr: Margariz of Seville came running there;

courir.of: Sur Veillantif, sun bun cheval curant.
courir.tr: On Veillantif, his good swift horse.

courir.of: Sun cheval brochet, laiset curre a esforz,
courir.tr: He spurs his horse, lets it run at full force,

courir.of: E Anseïs laiset le cheval curre,
courir.tr: And Anseïs lets his horse run,

courir.of: Sun ceval brochet, si li curt ad esforz;
courir.tr: He spurs his horse, and rushes at him with all his might;

courir.of: Beste nen est ki poisset curre a lui.
courir.tr: There is no beast that can run as fast as it.

courir.of: Li val parfunt e les ewes curant.
courir.tr: The valleys deep and the rivers swift-running.

courroucer.of: Si li ad dit: «A tort vos curuciez,
courroucer.tr: And said to him: «Wrongly do you grow angry,

couvrir.of: Ki fut cuvert d'une palie alexandrin.
couvrir.tr: Which was covered with an Alexandrian silk.

couvrir.of: Cuverz en sunt li val e les muntaignes
couvrir.tr: The valleys and the mountains are covered with them,

couvrir.of: De tutes parz en sunt cuvert li camp;
couvrir.tr: On all sides the fields are covered with them;

craindre.of: Jo me crendreie, que vos vos meslisez.
craindre.tr: I would fear that you would come to blows.

craindre.of: Soürs est Carles, que nuls home ne crent.» AOI.
craindre.tr: Charles is secure, for he fears no man.» AOI.

craindre.of: Soürs est Carlles, ne (cre) crent hume vivant.» AOI.
craindre.tr: Charles is secure, he fears no living man.» AOI.

craindre.of: Ja mar crendrez nul hume a mun vivant!» AOI.
craindre.tr: Never, while I live, need you fear any man!» AOI.

crier.of: Cel nen i ad ki ne criet: «Marsilie!
crier.tr: There is not one who does not cry out: «Marsile!

crier.of: Adubez vos, si criez vostre enseigne,
crier.tr: Arm yourselves, cry out your war-cry,

croire.of: Il dist al rei: «Ja mar crerez Marsilie.
croire.tr: He said to the king: «It would be folly to trust Marsile.

croire.of: E dist al rei: «Ja mar crerez bricun,
croire.tr: And said to the king: «Never to your harm will you believe a knave,

croire.of: Iert i sis nies, li quens Rollant, (...) ço crei,
croire.tr: There will be his nephew, Count Roland, (...) so I believe,

croire.of: Mort sunt li cunte, se est ki mei en creit.
croire.tr: The counts are dead, if there is anyone who believes me in this.

croire.of: Del rei paien, sire, par veir creez,
croire.tr: Concerning the pagan king, lord, believe in truth,

croire.of: Ne creit en Deu, le filz sainte Marie;
croire.tr: He does not believe in God, the son of holy Mary;

croire.of: Sem(e) creïsez, venuz i fust mi sire;
croire.tr: Had you trusted me, my lord would have come here;

crouler.of: De sun algeir ad la hanste crollee.
crouler.tr: With his javelin he has shaken the shaft.

croître.of: Soleill n'i luist, ne blet n'i poet pas creistre,
croître.tr: The sun does not shine there, nor can grain grow there,

daigner.of: Vostre olifan, suner vos nel deignastes;
daigner.tr: Your olifant, you did not deign to sound it;
You did not deign to sound your oliphant,

daigner.of: Quant jel vos dis, cumpainz, vos ne deignastes.
daigner.tr: When I told you of it, comrade, you did not deign to.

demander.of: S'est, kil demandet, ne l estoet enseigner.
demander.tr: He is so, whoever seeks him, that there is no need to point him out.

demander.of: Carles respunt: «Tort fait kil me demandet!
demander.tr: Charles replies: «He who asks me does wrong!
Whoever then had heard them call «Munjoie»

demander.of: Que ses cumpainz Rollant li ad tant demandee,
demander.tr: Which his companion Roland had so much asked of him,

demander.of: Cel nen i ad «Munjoie!» ne demant. AOI.
demander.tr: There is not one who does not cry «Munjoie!» AOI.

demander.of: Si li demandet dulcement e suef:
demander.tr: And asks him gently and softly:

demeurer.of: La noit demurent tresque vint al jur cler.
demeurer.tr: The night they remain until the bright day came.

demeurer.of: De ço qui calt? car demuret i unt trop.
demeurer.tr: What does it matter? For they have tarried too long.

demeurer.of: Demurent trop, n'i poedent estre a tens. AOI.
demeurer.tr: They tarry too long, they cannot be there in time. AOI.

demeurer.of: Morz est li quens, que plus ne se demuret.
demeurer.tr: The count is dead, he lingers no longer.

descendre.of: E li message descendirent a pied,
descendre.tr: And the messengers dismounted on foot,

descendre.of: Que en Sarraguce descendent suz un if.
descendre.tr: That in Saragossa they dismount beneath a yew.
He will not come down from them for any bad news,

descendre.of: Franceis de[s]cendent, a tere se sunt mis,
descendre.tr: The Franks dismount, they prostrated themselves on the ground,

descendre.of: Nostre Franceis i descendrunt a pied,
descendre.tr: Our Frenchmen will dismount here on foot,

descendre.of: Franceis descendent, si adubent lor cors
descendre.tr: The French dismount, and they equip their bodies

descendre.of: Descent a piet, a l[a] tere se culchet,
descendre.tr: He dismounts on foot, lies down on the ground,

devenir.of: Ne seit ocis, u devient chrestien.
devenir.tr: Who is not slain, or becomes a Christian.
There he will wish to become a Christian.»

devenir.of: Qu'il devendrat jointes ses mains tis hom,
devenir.tr: That he will become, his hands joined, your vassal,

devoir.of: En France, ad Ais, s'en deit ben repairer.
devoir.tr: To France, to Aix, he ought well to return.

devoir.of: Le glorius, que de[v]u[n]s aürer!
devoir.tr: the glorious, whom we must adore!

devoir.of: En France, ad Ais, devez bien repairer;
devoir.tr: to France, to Aix, you ought rightly to return;

devoir.of: Ceste grant guerre ne deit munter a plus.»
devoir.tr: This great war ought not to mount up to more.»

devoir.of: Mai[s] saives hom, il deit faire message:
devoir.tr: But a wise man, he ought to carry a message:

devoir.of: Quant le dut prendre, si li caït a tere.
devoir.tr: When he had to take it, it fell from him to the ground.

devoir.of: Quant aler dei, n'i ai plus que targer.»
devoir.tr: Since I must depart, there is no more reason for me to tarry.»

devoir.of: Ki ço jugat, que doüsez aler
devoir.tr: He who decreed that you should go

devoir.of: Li quens Rollant nel se doüst penser,
devoir.tr: Count Roland ought not to have thought of it,

devoir.of: Li soens orgoilz le devreit ben cunfundre,
devoir.tr: His own pride ought well to bring him to ruin,

devoir.of: Li Glorius, qui devum aürer!
devoir.tr: The Glorious One, whom we must adore!

devoir.of: Vos le doüssez esculter e oïr.»
devoir.tr: You ought to have listened to him and heard him out.»

devoir.of: De l'algalifes nel devez pas blasmer,
devoir.tr: Concerning the caliph you must not blame him,

devoir.of: «Sire parastre, mult vos dei aveir cher:
devoir.tr: «Lord stepfather, I must hold you dear:

devoir.of: Ne mul ne mule que deiet chevalcher,
devoir.tr: Nor mule nor she-mule that one ought to ride,

devoir.of: «Hom sui Rollant, jo ne li dei faillir.»
devoir.tr: «I am Roland's man, I must not fail him.»

devoir.of: Respunt gualter: «Pur vos le dei ben faire.»
devoir.tr: Gautier replies: «For you I must do it well.»

devoir.of: Ben devuns ci estre pur nostre rei:
devoir.tr: We must indeed stand here for our king:

devoir.of: Pur sun seignor deit hom susfrir destreiz
devoir.tr: For his lord a man should suffer hardships

devoir.of: Sin deit hom perdre e del quir e del peil.
devoir.tr: And a man should lose both hide and hair for it.

devoir.of: Pur sun seignur deit hom susfrir granz mals
devoir.tr: For his lord a man must suffer great hardships

devoir.of: Sin deit hom perdre del sanc e de la char.
devoir.tr: And a man must lose of his blood and of his flesh.

devoir.of: Pur nostre rei devum nus ben murir.
devoir.tr: For our king we must surely die.

devoir.of: Li emperere nos devreit ben venger.
devoir.tr: The emperor ought well to avenge us.

devoir.of: L'enseigne Carle n'i devum ublier.»
devoir.tr: We must not forget Charles's war-cry here.»

devoir.of: Cels ki ci sunt devum aveir mult vil;
devoir.tr: Those who are here we should hold very cheap;

devoir.of: Li .XII. per n'en deivent aveir blasme,
devoir.tr: The Twelve Peers ought to have no blame for it,

devoir.of: Fers e acers i deit aveir valor.
devoir.tr: Iron and steel ought to be of worth here.

devoir.of: Male chançun n'en deit estre cantee.» AOI.
devoir.tr: Let no evil song be sung about it.» AOI.

devoir.of: Cil ki la sunt n'en deivent aveir blasme.»
devoir.tr: Those who are over there ought not bear blame for it.»

devoir.of: Ja cil d'Espaigne ne s'en deivent turner liez.
devoir.tr: Those of Spain shall not return from here rejoicing.

devoir.of: Oliver, frere, vos ne dei jo faillir.
devoir.tr: Oliver, brother, I must not fail you.

devoir.of: Itel valor deit aveir chevaler
devoir.tr: Such valor a knight ought to have

devoir.of: En bataille deit estre forz e fiers,
devoir.tr: In battle he ought to be strong and fierce,

devoir.of: Einz deit monie estre en un de cez mustiers,
devoir.tr: Rather he ought to be a monk in one of these minsters,

dire.of: E dist al rei: «Ore ne vus esmaiez!
dire.tr: And he said to the king: «Now do not be dismayed!

dire.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Pa[r] ceste meie destre
dire.tr: Blancandrin said: «By this my right hand

dire.of: Dient paien: «Issi poet il ben estre!»
dire.tr: The pagans say: «It may well be so!»

dire.of: Dient paien: «De ço avun nus asez!» AOI.
dire.tr: The pagans say: «Of this we have enough!» AOI.

dire.of: Dist a ses humes: «Seignurs, vos en ireiz;
dire.tr: He said to his men: «Lords, you shall go forth;

dire.of: Si me direz a Carlemagne, le rei,
dire.tr: And you shall say for me to Charlemagne, the king,

dire.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Mult bon plait en avreiz.» AOI.
dire.tr: Blancandrin said: «You shall have a very good agreement out of it.» AOI.

dire.of: E dist al rei: «Salvez seiez de Deu
dire.tr: and said to the king: «Be saved by God

dire.of: La vos sivrat, ço dit mis avoez.»
dire.tr: there he will follow you, so says my lord.»

dire.of: Dist as messages: «Vus avez mult ben dit.
dire.tr: He said to the messengers: «You have spoken very well.

dire.of: De cez paroles que vos avez ci dit,
dire.tr: Concerning these words that you have spoken here,

dire.of: - «Voet par hostages,» ço dist li Sarrazins,
dire.tr: - «By hostages,» said the Saracen,

dire.of: Mis avoez la vos sivrat, ço dit;
dire.tr: My lord will follow you there, so he says;

dire.of: «Seignurs barons,» dist li emperere Carles,
dire.tr: «My lord barons,» said the emperor Charles,

dire.of: Dient Franceis: «Il nus i cuvent guarde!» AOI.
dire.tr: The Franks say: «We must be on our guard in this!» AOI.

dire.of: Il dist al rei: «Ja mar crerez Marsilie.
dire.tr: He said to the king: «It would be folly to trust Marsile.

dire.of: E dist al rei: «Ja mar crerez bricun,
dire.tr: And said to the king: «Never to your harm will you believe a knave,

dire.of: E dist al rei: «Ben l'avez entendud,
dire.tr: And he said to the king: «You have indeed heard it,

dire.of: Dient Franceis: «Ben ad parlet li dux.» AOI.
dire.tr: The Franks say: «The duke has spoken well.» AOI.

dire.of: - «Nu ferez certes!» dist li quens Oliver;
dire.tr: - «You shall certainly not!» said Count Oliver;

dire.of: E dist al rei: «Laisez ester voz Francs!
dire.tr: And said to the king: «Let your Franks be!

dire.of: - «Francs chevalers,» dist li emperere Carles,
dire.tr: - «Frankish knights,» said the emperor Charles,

dire.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Ço ert Guenes, mis parastre.»
dire.tr: Rollant said this: «That will be Guenes, my stepfather.»

dire.of: Dient Franceis: «Car il le poet ben faire;
dire.tr: The Franks say: «For he can do it well;

dire.of: Dist a Rollant: «Tut fol, pur quei t'esrages?
dire.tr: He said to Rollant: «Utter fool, why do you rage?

dire.of: E dit al cunte: «Jo ne vus aim nient;
dire.tr: and he says to the count: «I do not love you at all;

dire.of: Ço est Baldewin,» ço dit, «ki ert prozdoem.
dire.tr: it is Baldwin,» he says, «who will be a worthy man.

dire.of: Ço dist li reis: «Guenes venez avant. AOI.
dire.tr: The king said: «Ganelon, come forward. AOI.

dire.of: - «Sire,» dist Guenes, «ço ad tut fait Rollant!
dire.tr: - «Sire,» said Ganelon, «Roland has done all this!

dire.of: Ço dist li reis: «Trop avez maltalant.
dire.tr: The king said: «You have too much ill will.

dire.of: Dient Franceis: «Deus! que purrat ço estre?
dire.tr: The Franks say: «God! what can this be?

dire.of: - «Seignurs» dist Guenes, «vos en orrez noveles!»
dire.tr: - «Lords,» said Ganelon, «you will hear news of it!»

dire.of: - «Sire,» dist Guenes, «dunez mei le cungied;
dire.tr: - «Lord,» said Ganelon, «give me leave to go;

dire.of: Ço dist li reis: «Al Jhesu e al mien!»
dire.tr: The king said this: «In Jesus' keeping and in mine!»

dire.of: Ki tuit li dient «Tant mare fustes, ber!
dire.tr: Who all say to him: «What an evil hour was yours, baron!

dire.of: Enpres li dient: «Sire, car nos menez!»
dire.tr: Then they say to him: «Lord, do lead us along!»

dire.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Merveilus hom est Charles,
dire.tr: Blancandrin said: «Charles is a marvelous man,

dire.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Francs sunt mult gentilz home;
dire.tr: Blancandrin said: «The Franks are very noble men;

dire.of: «Tenez bel sire,» dist Rollant a sun uncle,
dire.tr: «Take it, fair lord,» said Roland to his uncle,

dire.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Mult est pesmes Rollant,
dire.tr: Blancandrin said: «Roland is most fearsome,

dire.of: E dist al rei: «Salvez seiez de Mahun
dire.tr: And said to the king: «Be saved by Mahomet

dire.of: Respunt Marsilie: «Or diet, nus l'orrum!» AOI.
dire.tr: Marsile replies: «Let him speak now, we shall hear him!» AOI.

dire.of: E dist al rei: «Salvez seiez de Deu
dire.tr: And said to the king: «May you be saved by God,

dire.of: Si li ad dit: «Mult estes bele e clere!
dire.tr: And said to it: «You are very fair and bright!

dire.of: Ja nel dirat de France li emperere,
dire.tr: Never shall the emperor of France say

dire.of: Dient paien: «Desfaimes la mellee!»
dire.tr: The pagans say: «Let us break off the quarrel!»

dire.of: Dist l'algalifes: «Mal nos avez baillit,
dire.tr: The caliph said: «You have served us ill,

dire.of: - «Sire,» dist Guenes, «mei l'avent a suffrir;
dire.tr: - «Sire,» said Ganelon, «it falls to me to endure it;

dire.of: Que jo ne li die, se tant ai de leisir,
dire.tr: From telling him, if I have so much leisure,

dire.of: Dient paien: «Noble baron ad ci!» AOI.
dire.tr: The pagans say: «Here is a noble baron!» AOI.

dire.of: Si li ad dit: «A tort vos curuciez,
dire.tr: And said to him: «Wrongly do you grow angry,

dire.of: E dist al rei: «Guenes ad dit folie;
dire.tr: And said to the king: «Ganelon has spoken folly;

dire.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Apelez le Franceis,
dire.tr: Said Blancandrin: «Call the Frenchman,

dire.of: Ço dist li reis: «E vos l'i ameneiz.»
dire.tr: Thus said the king: «And you, bring him here to me.»

dire.of: «Bel sire Guenes,» ço li ad dit Marsilie,
dire.tr: «Fair sir Ganelon,» Marsile said to him,

dire.of: Ço dist Marsilies: «Guenes par veir sacez,
dire.tr: Marsile said this: «Ganelon, know in truth,

dire.of: Que ço ne diet que l'emperere est ber.
dire.tr: Who would not say that the emperor is noble.

dire.of: Dist li paiens: «Mult me puis merveiller
dire.tr: The pagan said: «Greatly may I marvel

dire.of: - «Ço n'iert,» dist Guenes: «tant cum vivet sis niés:
dire.tr: - «That will not be,» said Ganelon, «as long as his nephew lives:

dire.of: Dist li Sarrazins: «Merveille en ai grant
dire.tr: Said the Saracen: «Great is the wonder I feel

dire.of: - «Ço n'iert,» dist Guenes, «tant cum vivet Rollant:
dire.tr: - «That will not be,» said Ganelon, «so long as Roland lives:

dire.of: - «Bel sire Guenes» dist marsilies li reis,
dire.tr: - «Fair lord Ganelon,» said Marsile the king,

dire.of: - «Bel sire Guenes,[» ço dist li reis Marsilies,]
dire.tr: - «Fair sir Ganelon,» said King Marsile to him,

dire.of: Guenes respont: «Ço vos sai jo ben dire.
dire.tr: Ganelon answers: «That I can well tell you.

dire.of: Nel di por ço, des voz iert la martirie.
dire.tr: I do not say it for that, the slaughter will be of yours.

dire.of: Ço dist Marsilies: «Qu'en parlereient il plus?
dire.tr: Marsile said: «Why should they speak further about it?

dire.of: Cler en riant l'ad dit a Guenelun:
dire.tr: Brightly, laughing, he said to Ganelon:

dire.of: Cler en riant a Guenelun l'ad dit:
dire.tr: Brightly, laughing, he said to Ganelon:

dire.of: «Jo vos aim mult, sire,» dist ele al cunte,
dire.tr: «I love you greatly, sire,» she said to the count,

dire.of: Si li ad dit: «Mult par ies ber e sage.
dire.tr: And said to him: «You are most valiant and wise.

dire.of: E dist al rei: «Salvez seiez de Deu!
dire.tr: And said to the king: «May you be saved by God!

dire.of: Ço dist li reis: «Graciet en seit Deus!
dire.tr: Thus said the king: «May God be thanked for it!

dire.of: Ço dit li reis que sa guere out finee.
dire.tr: The king says that his war is over.

dire.of: Dient Franceis, que grant bataille i ad;
dire.tr: The Franks say that there is a great battle there;

dire.of: «Seignurs barons,» dist li emperere Carles,
dire.tr: «Lord barons,» said the emperor Charles,

dire.of: Si li ad dit: «Vos estes vifs diables.
dire.tr: And said to him: «You are a living devil.

dire.of: Guenes respunt: «Veir dites, jol sai bien.» AOI.
dire.tr: Ganelon answers: «You speak truly, I know it well.» AOI.

dire.of: - «Dreiz emperere,» dist Rollant le barun,
dire.tr: - «Rightful emperor,» said Roland the baron,

dire.of: E dist al rei: «Ben l'avez entendut;
dire.tr: And he said to the king: «You have heard it well;

dire.of: Ço dit li quens: «Jo n'en ferai nient;
dire.tr: Says the count: «I will do nothing of the sort;

dire.of: Dist l'arcevesque: «Jo irai, par mun chef!»
dire.tr: Said the archbishop: «I shall go, by my head!»

dire.of: - «E jo od vos,» ço dist li quens Gualters;
dire.tr: - «And I with you,» so said the count Gualter;

dire.of: E dit al rei: «De quei avez pesance?»
dire.tr: And says to the king: «What is troubling you?»

dire.of: Dist a sun uncle belement en riant:
dire.tr: He said to his uncle, smiling pleasantly:

dire.of: Ço dist al rei: «Ne vos esmaiez unches!
dire.tr: He said to the king: «Never be dismayed!

dire.of: Ço dist Marsilie: «Seignurs, venez avant!
dire.tr: Marsile said this: «Lords, come forward!

dire.of: E dist al rei: «Ne vos esmaiez mie!
dire.tr: And said to the king: «Do not be dismayed at all!

dire.of: Icele tere, ço dit, dun il esteit,
dire.tr: That land, he says, from which he came,

dire.of: Dient alquanz que diables i meignent.
dire.tr: Some say that devils dwell there.

dire.of: Ce dist Chernubles: «Ma bone espee ai ceinte;
dire.tr: Chernuble says: «My good sword I have girded on;

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «Sire cumpainz, ce crei,
dire.tr: Oliver said: «Sir comrade, I believe

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «Jo ai paiens veüz:
dire.tr: Oliver said: «I have seen the pagans:

dire.of: Dient Franceis: «Dehet ait ki s'en fuit!
dire.tr: The Franks say: «Cursed be he who flees!

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «Paien unt grant esforz,
dire.tr: Oliver said: «The pagans have a great host,

dire.of: «Que ço seit dit de nul hume vivant,
dire.tr: «That it be said by any living man,

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «D'iço ne sai jo blasme?
dire.tr: Said Oliver: «Of this I do not know how to make complaint.

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «Rollant, veez en alques!
dire.tr: Said Oliver: «Roland, look over there a bit!

dire.of: Respunt Rollant: «Ne dites tel ultrage!
dire.tr: Roland answers: «Do not speak such outrage!

dire.of: «Sire cumpainz, amis, nel dire ja!
dire.tr: «Sir companion, friend, do not say so!

dire.of: Se jo i moert, dire poet ki l'avrat
dire.tr: If I die here, whoever has it may say

dire.of: (E purrunt dire) que ele fut a noble vassal.»
dire.tr: (And they will be able to say) that it belonged to a noble vassal.»

dire.of: Franceis apelet, un sermun lur ad dit:
dire.tr: He calls the Franks, he has given them a sermon:

dire.of: Si lur ad dit un mot curteisement:
dire.tr: And he said a word to them courteously:

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «N'ai cure de parler.
dire.tr: Said Oliver: «I have no wish to speak.

dire.of: De noz Franceis vait disant si mals moz:
dire.tr: He goes speaking such evil words about our French:

dire.of: Ne leserat, ço dit, que n'i parolt:
dire.tr: He will not refrain, he says, from speaking there:

dire.of: Si li ad dit par mult fiere raison:
dire.tr: And spoke to him in most fierce speech:

dire.of: Ne laisserat que n'i parolt, ço dit:
dire.tr: He will not refrain from speaking to him, he says this:

dire.of: Nuveles vos di: mort vos estoet susfrir.
dire.tr: News I tell you: you must suffer death.

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «Gente est nostre bataille!»
dire.tr: Oliver said: «Noble is our battle!»

dire.of: Dist l'arcevesque: «Cist colp est de baron!»
dire.tr: Said the archbishop: «This blow is a baron's!»

dire.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Cist colp est de produme!»
dire.tr: Roland says this: «This blow is a worthy man's!»

dire.of: Apres li dist: «Turnet estes a perdre!» AOI.
dire.tr: Afterward he says to him: «You are doomed to perish!» AOI.

dire.of: Apres li dist: «Ja n'i avrez guarant!»
dire.tr: Afterward he says to him: «You shall have no protection here!»

dire.of: Apres li dist: «Culvert, mar i moüstes!
dire.tr: Afterward he says to him: «Coward, in an evil hour you set forth!

dire.of: Dist l'arcevesque: «Ben ait nostre barnage!»
dire.tr: Said the archbishop: «Blessed be our baronage!»

dire.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Cumpainz, que faites vos?
dire.tr: Roland says this: «Comrade, what are you doing?

dire.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Vos receif jo, frere!
dire.tr: Roland said: «I accept you, brother!

dire.of: Ne l'oï dire ne jo mie nel sai
dire.tr: I never heard it told, nor do I know at all

dire.of: Ço dist Turpin: «Icist nos ert forsfait.»
dire.tr: Turpin says: «This one has done us wrong.»

dire.of: Dient plusor: «Ço est li definement,
dire.tr: Many say: «This is the ending,

dire.of: Il nel sevent, ne dient veir nient:
dire.tr: They do not know it, they speak no truth at all:

dire.of: Rollant dist: «Nostre hume sunt mult proz:
dire.tr: Roland said: «Our men are very valiant:

dire.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Oliver, compaign, frere,
dire.tr: Roland said this: «Oliver, companion, brother,

dire.of: Mult quiement le dit a sei meïsme:
dire.tr: Very quietly he says to himself:

dire.of: Dient Franceis: «Ci ad grant vasselage!
dire.tr: The French say: «Here is great valor!

dire.of: E l'arcevesque lur dist de sun semblant:
dire.tr: And the archbishop spoke to them in his fashion:

dire.of: Tere Major ço dit, metrat a hunte,
dire.tr: The Great Land, he says, he will put to shame,

dire.of: Dient Franceis: «Deus quel doel de prodome!» AOI.
dire.tr: The Franks say: «God, what grief for so brave a man!» AOI.

dire.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Mis cumpainz est irez!
dire.tr: Roland says: «My companion is enraged!

dire.of: Dient Franceis: «Deus quel doel de baron.!» AOI.
dire.tr: The Franks say: «God, what grief for such a baron!» AOI.

dire.of: Dient paien(t) [...]: «Cist colp nus est mult fort!»
dire.tr: The pagans say: «This blow is very hard on us!»

dire.of: Dient Franceis: «Barun, tant mare fus!»
dire.tr: The Franks say: «Baron, how ill-fated you were!»

dire.of: Dist al paien: «Deus tut mal te tramette!
dire.tr: He said to the pagan: «May God send you every evil!

dire.of: Dient Franceis: «Mult decheent li nostre!»
dire.tr: The Franks say: «Greatly do our men decline!»

dire.of: Dist al paien: «Deus tut mal te consente!
dire.tr: He said to the pagan: «May God grant you every evil!

dire.of: Dient Franceis: «Ben fiert nostre guarent!»
dire.tr: The Franks say: «Well does our protector strike!»

dire.of: Ço dit la Geste, plus de .IIII. milliers.
dire.tr: So says the Geste, more than four thousand.

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «Jo nel sai cument quere.
dire.tr: Said Oliver: «I know not how to seek it.

dire.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Cornerai l'olifant,
dire.tr: Roland said: «I will sound the olifant,

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «Vergoigne sereit grant
dire.tr: Oliver said: «It would be a great shame

dire.of: Quant jel vos dis, n'en feïstes nient;
dire.tr: When I told you this, you did nothing about it;

dire.of: Ço dit Rollant: «Forz est nostre bataille;
dire.tr: Roland says: «Our battle is fierce;

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «Ne sereit vasselage!
dire.tr: Oliver said: «That would be no act of valor!

dire.of: Quant jel vos dis, cumpainz, vos ne deignastes.
dire.tr: When I told you of it, comrade, you did not deign to.

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «Par ceste meie barbe,
dire.tr: Oliver said: «By this beard of mine,

dire.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Por quei me portez ire?»
dire.tr: Roland said this: «Why do you bear anger toward me?»

dire.of: Respunt Rollant: «Sire, mult dites bien.» AOI.
dire.tr: Roland answers: «Lord, you speak very well.» AOI.

dire.of: Ço dit li reis: «Bataille funt nostre hume!»
dire.tr: The king says: «Our men are giving battle!»

dire.of: «S'altre le desist, ja semblast grant mençunge!» AOI.
dire.tr: «Had another said it, it would seem a great lie!» AOI.

dire.of: Ce dist li reis: «Jo oi le corn Rollant!
dire.tr: The king says: «I hear Roland's horn!

dire.of: Ço dist li reis: «cCel corn ad lunge aleine!»
dire.tr: The king said: «That horn has long breath!»

dire.of: Dist l'arcevesque: «Asez le faites ben!
dire.tr: The archbishop said: «You do this very well indeed!

dire.of: Dist al paien: «Damnesdeus mal te duinst!
dire.tr: he said to the pagan: «May the Lord God give you ill!

dire.of: Dist l'un a l'altre: «E! car nos en fuiums!»
dire.tr: One said to the other: «Eh! let us flee from here!»

dire.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Ci recevrums ma[r]tyrie,
dire.tr: Roland said: «Here we shall receive our martyrdom,

dire.of: Ço dist li quens: «Or sai jo veirement
dire.tr: The count said this: «Now I know truly

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «Dehet ait li plus lenz!»
dire.tr: Oliver said: «Cursed be the slowest!»

dire.of: Dist l'un a l'altre: «L'empereor ad tort.»
dire.tr: One said to the other: «The emperor is in the wrong.»

dire.of: E dit apres: «Un col avez pris fort!
dire.tr: And said after: «You have taken a hard blow!

dire.of: E dist apres: «Paien mal aies tu!
dire.tr: And said afterward: «Pagan, a curse upon you!

dire.of: Iço ne di que Karles n'i ait perdut;
dire.tr: I do not say that Charles has lost nothing here;

dire.of: «Deus!» dist li quens, «or ne sai jo que face.
dire.tr: «God!» said the count, «now I know not what to do.

dire.of: Dist Oliver: «Or vos oi jo parler;
dire.tr: Oliver says: «Now I hear you speak;

donner.of: Jo nen ai ost qui bataille li dunne,
donner.tr: I have no army that may give him battle,

donner.of: Vos li durrez urs e leons e chens,
donner.tr: You will give him bears and lions and dogs,

donner.of: Jo vos durrai or e argent asez,
donner.tr: I shall give you gold and silver in plenty,

donner.of: De sun aveir vos voelt asez duner,
donner.tr: of his wealth he wishes to give you plenty,

donner.of: De sun aveir me voelt duner grant masse,
donner.tr: From his wealth he wishes to give me a great quantity,

donner.of: Dunez m'en, sire, le bastun e le guant,
donner.tr: Give me, sire, the staff and the glove,

donner.of: Se Deus ço dunet que jo de la repaire,
donner.tr: If God grants that I return from there,

donner.of: - «Sire,» dist Guenes, «dunez mei le cungied;
donner.tr: - «Lord,» said Ganelon, «give me leave to go;

donner.of: A lur seignur, ki tel cunseill li dunent:
donner.tr: To their lord, who give him such counsel:

donner.of: Demi Espaigne vos voelt en fiu duner.
donner.tr: He wishes to give you half of Spain in fief.

donner.of: Demi Espaigne vus durat il en fiet.
donner.tr: Half of Spain he will give you in fief.

donner.of: L'empereür tant li dunez aveir,
donner.tr: Give the emperor so much wealth,

donner.of: Par amistiez, bel sire, la vos duins,
donner.tr: In friendship, fair lord, I give it to you,

donner.of: De mun aveir vos voeill dunner grant masse:
donner.tr: Of my wealth I will give you a great quantity:

donner.of: «Dunez mei l'arc, que vos tenez el poign.
donner.tr: «Give me the bow that you hold in your fist.

donner.of: Dunez li l'arc que vos avez tendut,
donner.tr: Give him the bow that you have drawn,

donner.of: Li reis li dunet, e Rollant l'a reçut.
donner.tr: The king gives it to him, and Roland received it.

donner.of: Ne lesserat bataille ne lur dunt.
donner.tr: Will not fail to give them battle.

donner.of: Dunez m'un feu, ço est le colp de Rollant;
donner.tr: Grant me a fief: it is the blow against Roland;

donner.of: Li reis Marsilie l'en ad dunet le guant. AOI.
donner.tr: King Marsile has given him the glove for it. AOI.

donner.of: Se trois Rollant, de mort li duins fiance.
donner.tr: If I find Roland, I pledge him to death.

donner.of: Ma bone espee, que li reis me dunat.
donner.tr: My good sword, which the king gave me.

donner.of: De colps ferir, de receivre e (de) duner!
donner.tr: to strike blows, to receive and (to) give them!

donner.of: En Val Metas li dunat uns diables,
donner.tr: In Val Metas a devil gave it to him,

donner.of: Si l'en dunat s'espee e s'escarbuncle.
donner.tr: And gave him his sword and his carbuncle.

donner.of: Respont li quens: «Deus le me doinst venger!»
donner.tr: The count replies: «May God grant me to avenge him!»

donner.of: Il li dunat s'espee e mil manguns.
donner.tr: He gave him his sword and a thousand mangons.

donner.of: Ensembl'od lui i durriums granz colps.»
donner.tr: Together with him we would deal great blows.»

donner.of: Dist al paien: «Damnesdeus mal te duinst!
donner.tr: he said to the pagan: «May the Lord God give you ill!

donner.of: Si priet Deu que pareïs li dunget
donner.tr: And he prays to God to grant him paradise

dormir.of: Carles se dort, li empereres riches.
Carles se dort, qu'il ne s'esveillet mie.
dormir.tr: Charles sleeps, for he does not wake at all.

dormir.of: Carles se dort, mie ne s'esveillat. AOI.
dormir.tr: Charles sleeps on, he did not wake at all. AOI.

douter.of: E Sarrazins nes unt mie dutez;
douter.tr: And they have not at all feared the Saracens;

dresser.of: En piez se drecet, si li vint cuntredire.
dresser.tr: Rises to his feet, and came to contradict him.

dresser.of: En piez se drecet, si vint devant Carlun,
dresser.tr: He rises to his feet, and came before Charles,

dresser.of: Franceis se drecent, si se metent sur piez.
dresser.tr: The French rise up and get to their feet.

durer.of: Ki durerat a trestut tun edage.»
durer.tr: That it will last all your lifetime.»

durer.of: Fiert de l'espiet tant cume hanste li duret;
durer.tr: He strikes with his spear as long as the shaft lasts him;

durer.of: Iceste hunte dureit al lur vivant!
durer.tr: This disgrace would last all their lives!

durer.of: Brochent ad ait tant cum durent li port.
durer.tr: They spur eagerly as far as the passes extend.

déchoir.tr: The Franks say: «Greatly do our men decline!»

déclore.of: L'escut li freint e l'osberc li desclot,
déclore.tr: He shatters his shield and breaks open his hauberk,

déclore.of: Aprof li ad sa bronie desclose,
déclore.tr: Then he has ripped open his hauberk,

découdre.of: Le blanc osberc li ad descust el cors,
découdre.tr: He has split open the white hauberk against his body,

défaillir.of: Oi nus defalt la leial cumpaignie:
défaillir.tr: Today the loyal fellowship fails us:

défaire.of: L'ost des Franceis verrez sempres desfere.
défaire.tr: You will soon see the host of the Franks disband.
The pagans say: «Let us break off the quarrel!»
«To Roncevaux I will go to undo their pride.

défendre.of: Fierent li un, li altre se defendent.
défendre.tr: The one side strikes, the others defend themselves.

défier.of: Desfi les ci, sire, vostre veiant.»
défier.tr: I defy them here, sire, in your sight.»

défier.of: Par nule guise ne m'aviez desfiet!»
défier.tr: In no way had you defied me!»

déguerpir.of: Mais de s'espee ne volt mie guerpir;
déguerpir.tr: But he would by no means give up his sword;

déguerpir.of: Meilz voelt murir que guerpir sun barnet.»
déguerpir.tr: He would rather die than abandon his noble company.»

déguerpir.of: Voelent u nun, si guerpissent le camp.
déguerpir.tr: Willing or not, they abandon the field.

déguerpir.of: Ja pur murir le camp ne guerpirunt.»
déguerpir.tr: Even on pain of death they will not abandon the field.»

déjeter.of: Ki ço vos lodet que cest plait degetuns,
déjeter.tr: Whoever advises you that we reject this agreement,

démailler.of: L'escut li freint e l'osberc li desmailet,
démailler.tr: He shatters his shield and breaks apart the mail of his hauberk,

démembrer.tr: Whoever had seen him dismember the Saracens,

démener.of: Par tantes teres ad sun cors demened,
démener.tr: Through so many lands he has driven his body,

démener.of: N'i ad icel ne demeint irance
démener.tr: There is not one of them but feels grief

démentir.of: Deus me cunfunde, se la geste en desment!
démentir.tr: May God confound me, if I belie my lineage!

démontrer.of: Quant por ferir vus demustrai grant ire.
démontrer.tr: when I showed you great anger by making to strike you.

départir.of: Colp en avras einz que nos departum,
départir.tr: You will have a blow before we part,

dépecer.of: Cordres ad prise e les murs peceiez,
dépecer.tr: He has taken Cordres and shattered its walls,

dépecer.of: Que entre mes puinz me depeçout ma hanste,
dépecer.tr: That between my hands my lance was shattered,

déranger.of: Gualter desrenget les destreiz e les tertres,
déranger.tr: Gautier ranges over the passes and the hills,

déserter.of: Oi desertet a tant rubostl exill.
déserter.tr: today laid waste by such cruel ruin.

désirer.of: Cum il le vit, a ferir le desiret.
désirer.tr: As soon as he saw him, he longed to strike him.

détordre.of: Si duist sa barbe, e detoerst sun gernun;
détordre.tr: He smoothed his beard, and twisted his moustache;

détourner.of: Ferir l'en volt, se n'en fust desturnet. AOI.
détourner.tr: He wished to strike him with it, had he not been prevented. AOI.

détruire.tr: Through Ganelon France will be destroyed,

effrayer.of: Li reis Marsilies en fut mult esfreed.
effrayer.tr: King Marsile was much frightened by this.

embattre.of: Sun bon espiet enz el cors li enbat.
embattre.tr: He drives his good spear into the body.

embroncher.of: Falt li le coer, le helme li embrunchet,
embroncher.tr: His heart fails him, his helmet droops down,

emmener.of: Ses meillors humes enmeinet ensembl'od sei:
emmener.tr: He brings his best men along with him:

empenner.of: Un algier tint, ki d'or fut enpenet,
empenner.tr: He held a javelin, which was feathered with gold,

employer.of: Or guart chascuns que granz colps (l')[i] empleit,
employer.tr: Now let each man take care to strike great blows there,

enchaîner.of: Urs e leuns e veltres enchaignez,
enchaîner.tr: bears and lions and leashed hunting-dogs,

enchaîner.of: Si l'encaeinent altresi cum un urs;
enchaîner.tr: And chain him up just like a bear;

encombrer.tr: «Hear, my lords, what calamity weighs upon us:

endurer.of: E endurer e granz chalz e granz freiz,
endurer.tr: And endure both great heat and great cold,

endurer.of: E endurer e forz freiz e granz chalz,
endurer.tr: And endure both fierce cold and great heat,

enfouir.tr: They will bury us in the churchyards of minsters;

enfreindre.of: Mur ne citet n'i est remés a fraindre
enfreindre.tr: no wall or city is left to be breached

enfreindre.of: Freint le seel, getet en ad la cire,
enfreindre.tr: He breaks the seal, has cast away the wax,

enfreindre.of: Li quens Rollant il l'ad e prise e fraite;
enfreindre.tr: Count Roland had taken and shattered it;

enfreindre.of: L'escut li freint e l'osberc li desclot,
enfreindre.tr: He shatters his shield and breaks open his hauberk,

enfreindre.of: L'escut li freint e l'osberc li derumpt,
enfreindre.tr: He shatters his shield and tears apart his hauberk,

enfreindre.of: L'escut li freinst, l'osberc li descumfist,
enfreindre.tr: He shattered his shield, he broke apart his hauberk,

enfreindre.of: Tute li freint la bucle de cristal,
enfreindre.tr: He shatters the boss of crystal all over it,

enfreindre.of: L'escut li freint e l'osberc li desmailet,
enfreindre.tr: He shatters his shield and breaks apart the mail of his hauberk,

enfreindre.of: L'escut li freinst, ki est a flurs e ad ór,
enfreindre.tr: He shattered his shield, which is wrought with flowers and gold,

enfreindre.of: L'escut li freint desuz l'oree bucle,
enfreindre.tr: He shatters his shield beneath the gilded boss,

enfreindre.of: L'escut del col li freint e escantelet,
enfreindre.tr: He shatters and smashes to pieces the shield at his neck,

enfreindre.of: L'escut li freinst, l'osberc li descumfist,
enfreindre.tr: He shattered his shield, he tore apart his hauberk,

enfreindre.of: L'escut li freint suz la bucle d'or mer,
enfreindre.tr: He shatters his shield beneath the boss of pure gold,

enfreindre.of: A .XV. cols l'ad fraite e [...] perdue,
enfreindre.tr: At fifteen blows he has broken and lost it,

enfreindre.of: L'elme li freint u li carbuncle luisent,
enfreindre.tr: He breaks his helm where the carbuncles gleam,

enfreindre.of: L'escut li freint, ki est ad or e a flur,
enfreindre.tr: He shatters his shield, which is adorned with gold and flowers,

enfreindre.of: Lur dous espiez enz el cors li unt frait,
enfreindre.tr: Their two spears they have shattered in his body,

enfreindre.of: Tant hanste i ad e fraite e sanglente,
enfreindre.tr: So many a lance there is, both shattered and bloody,

enfreindre.of: L'escut li freint e l'osberc li derumpt,
enfreindre.tr: He shatters his shield and tears apart his hauberk,

enfreindre.of: L'escut vermeill li freint, de col li portet;
enfreindre.tr: He shatters his vermilion shield, tears it from his neck;

enfreindre.of: L'escut li freint e l'osberc li derumpt,
enfreindre.tr: He breaks his shield and tears apart his hauberk,

enfuir.of: De Marcilie s'en fuient por la chrestientet,
enfuir.tr: From Marsile they flee on account of Christianity,

enfuir.of: Dient Franceis: «Dehet ait ki s'en fuit!
enfuir.tr: The Franks say: «Cursed be he who flees!

enfuir.of: Ki ne s'en fuit de mort n'i ad guarent;
enfuir.tr: Whoever does not flee has no protection from death there;

enfuir.of: Devant Rollant si s'en fuient paiens.
enfuir.tr: So the pagans flee before Roland.

enfuir.of: Dist l'un a l'altre: «E! car nos en fuiums!»
enfuir.tr: One said to the other: «Eh! let us flee from here!»

enfuir.of: De ço qui calt? Se fuit s'en est Marsilies,
enfuir.tr: What does it matter? Marsile has fled,

enluminer.of: De tel barnage l'ad Deus enluminet,
enluminer.tr: With such nobility has God illumined him,

enquérir.of: Enquis ad mult la lei de salvetez;
enquérir.tr: he has inquired much into the law of salvation;

enrager.of: Dist a Rollant: «Tut fol, pur quei t'esrages?
enrager.tr: He said to Rollant: «Utter fool, why do you rage?

enseigner.of: S'est, kil demandet, ne l estoet enseigner.
enseigner.tr: He is so, whoever seeks him, that there is no need to point him out.

entendre.of: E dist al rei: «Ben l'avez entendud,
entendre.tr: And he said to the king: «You have indeed heard it,

entendre.of: Saveir i ad, mais qu'il seit entendud.
entendre.tr: There is wisdom in it, provided it be heeded.

entendre.of: E dist al rei: «Ben l'avez entendut;
entendre.tr: And he said to the king: «You have heard it well;

entendre.of: Karles l'oït e ses Franceis l'entendent,
entendre.tr: Charles hears it and his Franks hear it,

entreprendre.of: Faites la guer[re] cum vos l'avez enprise:
entreprendre.tr: Wage the war as you have undertaken it:

entrer.of: Entret en sa veie, si s'est achiminez. AOI.
entrer.tr: He sets out on his way, and thus has started off. AOI.

entrer.of: Pois est munted, entret en sun veiage. AOI.
entrer.tr: Then he mounted, and set out on his journey. AOI.

entrer.of: El cors vos est entree mortel rage.
entrer.tr: Into your body has entered a deadly rage.

envelopper.tr: It was wrapped in a silk cloth of Alexandria:

envoyer.of: S'en volt ostages, e vos l'en enveiez,
envoyer.tr: If he wants hostages, then send them to him,

envoyer.of: Enveiu[n]s i les filz de noz muillers:
envoyer.tr: Let us send there the sons of our wives:

envoyer.of: Par nun d'ocire i enveierai le men.
envoyer.tr: Even at the risk of death I will send mine.

envoyer.of: De ses pai[ens il vus] enveiat quinze,
envoyer.tr: Of his pagans he sent you fifteen,
«My lord barons, whom shall we send

envoyer.of: Ci vos enveiet un sun noble barun,
envoyer.tr: He sends you here one of his noble barons,

envoyer.of: Nostre emperere vus enveiet cest bref.»
envoyer.tr: Our emperor sends you this letter.»

envoyer.of: Dunc li envei mun uncle, l'algalife;
envoyer.tr: Then let me send him my uncle, the caliph;
By twenty hostages that you will send him

envoyer.of: De voz paiens lur enveiez .C. milie:
envoyer.tr: Of your pagans send them a hundred thousand:

envoyer.of: A vostre femme enveierai dous nusches;
envoyer.tr: To your wife I will send two necklaces;

errer.of: Par cels de France voelt il del tut errer. AOI.
errer.tr: Through those of France he wishes to act in everything. AOI.

errer.of: Tant ad erret nen est dreiz que plus vivet.
errer.tr: He has gone so far that it is not right that he live longer.

escrimer.tr: And the nimble young squires fence.

esquiver.tr: Never, even on pain of death, will they shun battle.

ester.of: E dist al rei: «Laisez ester voz Francs!
ester.tr: And said to the king: «Let your Franks be!

ester.of: Sur l'erbe verte estut devant sun tref.
ester.tr: On the green grass he stood before his tent.

ester.of: El camp estez, que ne seium vencuz!
ester.tr: Hold the field, that we be not vanquished!»

ester.of: Se vos murez, esterez seinz martirs,
ester.tr: If you die, you will be holy martyrs,

exploiter.of: Par quele gent quiet il espleiter tant?»
exploiter.tr: With what people does he think to achieve so much?»

extraire.of: Que estrait estes de mult grant parented.»
extraire.tr: Since you are descended from a very great lineage.»

faillir.of: Il l'a[i]ment tant ne li faldrunt nient;
faillir.tr: They love him so much they will not fail him at all;

faillir.of: «Hom sui Rollant, jo ne li dei faillir.»
faillir.tr: «I am Roland's man, I must not fail him.»

faillir.of: Ja pur murir ne vus en faldrat uns.» AOI.
faillir.tr: Never, even to escape death, will a single one of us fail you.» AOI.

faillir.of: Oliver, frere, vos ne dei jo faillir.
faillir.tr: Oliver, brother, I must not fail you.

faillir.of: Falt li le coer, le helme li embrunchet,
faillir.tr: His heart fails him, his helmet droops down,

faire.of: Cinquante carre, qu'en ferat carier:
faire.tr: Fifty carts, that he will have carried away:

faire.of: De noz ostages ferat tre[n]cher les testes;
faire.tr: He will have the heads of our hostages cut off;

faire.of: Dis blanches mules fist amener Marsilies,
faire.tr: Marsile had ten white mules brought,

faire.of: Cil sunt muntez ki le message firent,
faire.tr: Those who carried the message have mounted,

faire.of: Li empereres se fait e balz e liez,
faire.tr: The emperor makes himself both joyful and glad,

faire.of: Un faldestoed i unt fait tut d or mer,
faire.tr: A throne they have made all of pure gold,

faire.of: Cinquante care, que carier en ferez;
faire.tr: fifty carts, which you will have carted away;

faire.of: Enz en voz bainz que Deus pur vos i fist,
faire.tr: There within your baths that God made for you,

faire.of: Les dis mulez fait Char[l]es establer,
faire.tr: The ten mules Charles has stabled,

faire.of: El grant verger fait li reis tendre un tref,
faire.tr: In the great orchard the king has a tent pitched,

faire.of: Les dis messages ad fait enz hosteler;
faire.tr: The ten messengers he has had lodged within;

faire.of: Guenes i vint, ki la traïsun fist.
faire.tr: Guenes came there, who committed the treason.

faire.of: Li reis Marsilie i fist mult que traïtre:
faire.tr: King Marsile acted there very much like a traitor:

faire.of: Faites la guer[re] cum vos l'avez enprise:
faire.tr: Wage the war as you have undertaken it:

faire.of: Si vengez cels que li fels fist ocire!» AOI.
faire.tr: And avenge those whom the villain had killed!» AOI.

faire.of: Pecchet fereit, ki dunc li fesist plus,
faire.tr: He would commit a sin, whoever should then do more to him,

faire.of: U par ostage vos (en) voelt faire soürs;
faire.tr: Or by hostages he wishes to give you assurance;

faire.of: - «Nu ferez certes!» dist li quens Oliver;
faire.tr: - «You shall certainly not!» said Count Oliver;

faire.of: Dient Franceis: «Car il le poet ben faire;
faire.tr: The Franks say: «For he can do it well;

faire.of: Mai[s] saives hom, il deit faire message:
faire.tr: But a wise man, he ought to carry a message:

faire.of: Si li reis voelt, prez sui por vus le face.» AOI.
faire.tr: If the king wishes, I am ready to do it for you.» AOI.

faire.of: Carles comandet que face sun servise:
faire.tr: Charles commands that I perform his service:

faire.of: Einz i f[e]rai un poi de [le]gerie,
faire.tr: But first I shall do a bit of recklessness,

faire.of: - «Sire,» dist Guenes, «ço ad tut fait Rollant!
faire.tr: - «Sire,» said Ganelon, «Roland has done all this!

faire.of: Mult grant mal funt e [cil] duc e cil cunte
faire.tr: Great harm do these dukes and these counts

faire.of: Ki tute gent voelt faire recreant,
faire.tr: He who wants to make every people cry for mercy,

faire.of: Vostre message fesime[s] a Charlun.
faire.tr: We delivered your message to Charles.

faire.of: Loat sun Deu, ne fist altre respuns.
faire.tr: Praised his God, made no other reply.

faire.of: Cume celui ki ben faire le set,
faire.tr: Like one who well knows how to do it,

faire.of: Jo ne lerreie, por l'or que Deus fist
faire.tr: I would not refrain, for all the gold that God made

faire.of: Livrez le mei, jo en ferai la justise.»
faire.tr: Hand him over to me, I will do justice on him for it.»

faire.of: «Jo vos ai fait alques de legerie,
faire.tr: «I have done you something of a wrong,

faire.of: Melz en valt l'or que ne funt cinc cenz livres:
faire.tr: their gold is worth more than five hundred pounds:

faire.of: Funt les enguardes a .XX. milie chevalers.
faire.tr: form the vanguards with twenty thousand knights.

faire.of: Funt les enguardes a .XX. milie de Francs,
faire.tr: Form the vanguard with twenty thousand Franks,

faire.of: Dunc avrez faite gente chevalerie;
faire.tr: Then you will have done a noble feat of arms;

faire.of: - «Chi purreit faire, que Rollant i fust mort,
faire.tr: - «Whoever could bring it about that Roland be killed there,

faire.of: Marsilies fait porter un livre avant:
faire.tr: Marsile has a book brought forward:

faire.of: - «Ben serat fait,» li quens Guenes respunt.
faire.tr: - «It will be well done,» answers Count Ganelon.

faire.of: - «Ben serat fait,» Guenes respundit.
faire.tr: - «It will be well done,» Ganelon answered.

faire.of: Jamais n'iert an, altretel ne vos face.
faire.tr: Never will a year pass that I do not give you the same.

faire.of: Mult grant aveir vos en faz amener,
faire.tr: Very great treasure I have brought to you,

faire.of: E .XX. hostages; faites les ben guarder!
faire.tr: And twenty hostages; have them well guarded!

faire.of: Ben l'avez fait, mult grant prod i avrez.»
faire.tr: You have done well, very great reward you will have for it.»

faire.of: Par mi cel ost funt mil grailles suner;
faire.tr: Throughout that host they have a thousand trumpets sounded;

faire.of: Franc desherbergent, funt lur sumers trosser:
faire.tr: The Franks break camp, have their pack-horses loaded:

faire.of: N'avez barun, ki mielz de lui la facet.» AOI.
faire.tr: You have no baron who would do it better than he.» AOI.

faire.of: Cume fist a tei le bastun devant Carle?» AOI.
faire.tr: As the staff fell to you before Charles?» AOI.

faire.of: Que il me chedet, cum fist a Guenelun
faire.tr: that it should fall from me, as it happened to Ganelon

faire.of: Ço dit li quens: «Jo n'en ferai nient;
faire.tr: Says the count: «I will do nothing of the sort;

faire.of: Respunt gualter: «Pur vos le dei ben faire.»
faire.tr: Gautier replies: «For you I must do it well.»

faire.of: Suz sun mantel en fait la cuntenance.
faire.tr: Beneath his cloak he keeps his composure.

faire.of: Carles respunt: «Tort fait kil me demandet!
faire.tr: Charles replies: «He who asks me does wrong!

faire.of: Guen[e]s li fels en ad fait traïsun:
faire.tr: Ganelon the felon has committed treason against him:

faire.of: En Sarraguce fait suner ses taburs;
faire.tr: In Saragossa he has his drums sounded;

faire.of: Faites batailles e vencues en champ!
faire.tr: Fought battles and won them in the field!

faire.of: «Bel sire reis, fait m'avez un grant dun.
faire.tr: «Fair lord king, you have granted me a great gift.

faire.of: Ceste bataille veirement la ferum:
faire.tr: This battle, truly, we will fight it:

faire.of: Plus curt a piet que ne fait un cheval.
faire.tr: He runs faster on foot than a horse does.

faire.of: Mult se fait fiers de ses armes porter;
faire.tr: He bears himself fiercely in carrying his arms;

faire.of: Devant Marsilie ad faite sa vantance:
faire.tr: Before Marsile he made his boast:

faire.of: De chrestiens voelt faire male vode.
faire.tr: Of Christians he wishes to make an evil massacre.

faire.of: Que .IIII. mulez ne funt, quant il sumeient.
faire.tr: Than four pack-mules bear when they are laden.

faire.of: Se ne l'asaill, dunc ne faz jo que creire,
faire.tr: If I do not assail him, then let me not be believed,

faire.of: Icist ferunt nos Franceis grant irur.
faire.tr: These will bring great grief upon our Franks.

faire.of: Respunt Rollant: «Jo fereie que fols!
faire.tr: Roland answers: «I would act like a fool!

faire.of: Si l'orrat Carles, ferat l'ost returner,
faire.tr: Charles will hear it, will make the army turn back,

faire.of: Ki ceste fait, jamais n'en ferat altre.»
faire.tr: He who makes this one will never make another.»

faire.of: Plus se fait fiers que leon ne leupart.
faire.tr: He makes himself fiercer than lion or leopard.

faire.of: Li reis Marsilie de nos ad fait marchet;
faire.tr: King Marsile has made a bargain of us;

faire.of: Cil ki la sunt ne funt mie a blasmer.
faire.tr: Those who are there are not at all to be blamed.

faire.of: Si vunt ferir, que fereient il el?
faire.tr: and they go to strike — what else would they do?

faire.of: Enpeint le ben, fait li brandir le cors,
faire.tr: Drives it home well, makes the body sway,

faire.of: Il fist que proz qu'il nus laisad as porz:
faire.tr: He acted as a brave man when he left us at the passes:

faire.of: Empeint le ben, que mort le fait brandir,
faire.tr: Drives it well in, so that he makes him reel dead,

faire.of: Des Sarrazins lur fait mult grant damage.
faire.tr: Does the Saracens very great harm.

faire.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Cumpainz, que faites vos?
faire.tr: Roland says this: «Comrade, what are you doing?

faire.of: Si fait suner ses cors e ses buisines,
faire.tr: So he has his horns and his trumpets sounded,

faire.of: Qu'(e) il ne fesist trestut l'or de Galice;
faire.tr: Than he would for all the gold of Galicia;

faire.of: Encuntre mei fait asez a preiser.
faire.tr: Beside me he does very well, worthy of praise.

faire.of: Ki de sun cors feïst [...] tantes proecces.
faire.tr: Who with his body performed such feats of valor.

faire.of: Sun bon ceval i ad fait esdemetre,
faire.tr: He spurred his good horse to leap forward,

faire.of: Oliver, frere, cumment le purrum nus faire?
faire.tr: Oliver, brother, how shall we manage it?

faire.of: Quant jel vos dis, n'en feïstes nient;
faire.tr: When I told you this, you did nothing about it;

faire.of: Mais nel ferez par le men loement.
faire.tr: But you will not do it with my approval.

faire.of: Respont li quens: «Colps i ai fait mult genz!» AOI.
faire.tr: The count replied: «I have struck blows most fine!» AOI.

faire.of: (E cil) E il respont: «Cumpainz, vos le feïstes,
faire.tr: And he answers: «Companion, you did it,

faire.of: Mielz valt mesure que ne fait estultie.
faire.tr: Moderation is worth more than recklessness.

faire.of: Ceste bataille oüsum faite u prise;
faire.tr: This battle we would have fought and won;

faire.of: Ço dit li reis: «Bataille funt nostre hume!»
faire.tr: The king says: «Our men are giving battle!»

faire.of: Pur cel le fist ne fust a[pa]rissant.
faire.tr: So that it should not be apparent.

faire.of: Respont dux Neimes: «Baron i fait la p[e]ine!
faire.tr: Duke Naimes answers: «A baron takes the trouble of it!

faire.of: Li empereres ad fait suner ses corns.
faire.tr: The emperor has had his horns sounded.

faire.of: Li reis fait prendre le cunte Guenelun,
faire.tr: The king has Count Ganelon seized,

faire.of: De ma maisnee ad faite traïsun.»
faire.tr: He has committed treason against my house.»

faire.of: En seintes flurs il les facet gesir!
faire.tr: May he make them lie among holy flowers!

faire.of: Dist l'arcevesque: «Asez le faites ben!
faire.tr: The archbishop said: «You do this very well indeed!

faire.of: En tel bataill[e] fait grant defension:
faire.tr: puts up a great defense in such a battle:

faire.of: Tort nos ad fait: nen est dreiz qu'il s'en lot,
faire.tr: He has wronged us: it is not right that he should boast of it,

faire.of: Ne fait damage ne de mei ne d'altrui!»
faire.tr: Nor did harm to me or to any other!»

faire.of: «Deus!» dist li quens, «or ne sai jo que face.
faire.tr: «God!» said the count, «now I know not what to do.

faire.of: «Sire cumpain, faites le vos de gred?
faire.tr: «Lord companion, do you do this on purpose?

faire.of: Nem fesis mal ne jo nel te forsfis.
faire.tr: You never did me wrong, nor did I wrong you.

feindre.of: Cil l'at traït ki vos en roevet feindre.
feindre.tr: He who asks you to feign indifference has betrayed him.

fendre.of: Dunc ad tel doel pur poi d'ire ne fent,
fendre.tr: then he has such grief that he nearly bursts from rage,

fendre.of: N'i ad clartet, se li ciels nen i fent.
fendre.tr: There is no light, unless the sky splits open.

fendre.of: Si grant doel ad que par mi quiet fendre;
fendre.tr: He has such great grief that he thinks he will split apart;

fendre.of: Tresqu'al nasel tut le elme li fent,
fendre.tr: That he splits his whole helmet down to the nasal,

fermer.of: Esperuns d'or ad en ses piez fermez,
fermer.tr: He has fastened golden spurs on his feet,

fermer.of: Li quens Rollant ad l'enseigne fermee
fermer.tr: Count Roland has fastened the standard,

fermer.of: Halbercs vestuz e tres bien fermeez
fermer.tr: hauberks donned and very well fastened,

fiancer.of: U dis u vint pur lui afiancer.
fiancer.tr: Either ten or twenty, to give him assurance.

fier.of: E Oliver, en qui il tant se fiet;
fier.tr: And Oliver, in whom he so trusts;

finir.of: Li reis Marsilie out sun cunseill finet:
finir.tr: King Marsile had ended his council:

finir.of: Li reis Marsilie out finet sun cunseill;
finir.tr: King Marsile had ended his council;

finir.of: Ses baruns mandet pur sun cunseill finer:
finir.tr: His barons he summons to settle his counsel:

finir.of: Ses baruns mandet pur sun cunseill fenir:
finir.tr: He summons his barons to settle his counsel:

finir.of: Li empereres out sa raisun fenie.
finir.tr: The emperor had finished his speech.
By judgment you will there be put to death;

finir.of: Ço dit li reis que sa guere out finee.
finir.tr: The king says that his war is over.

finir.of: Se truis Rollant, de mort serat finet,
finir.tr: If I find Roland, he will be finished by death,

flamber.of: N'unt guarnement que tut ne reflambeit.
flamber.tr: They have no piece of armor that does not all blaze with light.

fleurir.of: Blanche ad la barbe e tut flurit le chef,
fleurir.tr: White is his beard and all blossomed his head,

forfaire.of: La traïsun jurat, e si s'en est forsfait. AOI.
forfaire.tr: He swore the betrayal, and thus made himself guilty of it. AOI.

forfaire.of: Ço dist Turpin: «Icist nos ert forsfait.»
forfaire.tr: Turpin says: «This one has done us wrong.»

forfaire.of: Nem fesis mal ne jo nel te forsfis.
forfaire.tr: You never did me wrong, nor did I wrong you.

froisser.of: Od voz caables avez fruiset ses murs,
froisser.tr: With your siege engines you have shattered his walls,

froisser.of: La hanste fruisset, mie n'en a(d)[b]atiet.
froisser.tr: The shaft splinters, none of it is struck down.

fuir.of: Nostre Franceis n'unt talent de fuïr.
fuir.tr: Our Franks have no wish to flee.

fuir.of: Fuïr s'en voel, mais ne li valt nient:
fuir.tr: He wishes to flee from it, but it avails him nothing:

férir.of: Ferir l'en volt, se n'en fust desturnet. AOI.
férir.tr: He wished to strike him with it, had he not been prevented. AOI.

férir.of: Que li Franceis asmastes a ferir;
férir.tr: In that you dared to strike the Frenchman;

férir.of: Quant por ferir vus demustrai grant ire.
férir.tr: when I showed you great anger by making to strike you.

férir.of: Nus les feruns vermeilles de chald sanc.
férir.tr: We will make them crimson with hot blood.

férir.of: Sempres ferrai de Durendal granz colps;
férir.tr: At once I will strike great blows with Durendal;

férir.of: Einz i ferrai de Durendal asez,
férir.tr: Rather I will strike hard with Durendal,

férir.of: E jo ferrai e mil colps e .VII. cenz,
férir.tr: And I shall strike a thousand blows and seven hundred,

férir.of: Franceis sunt bon, si ferrunt vassalment,
férir.tr: The French are good, they will strike valiantly,

férir.of: Pur ben ferir l'emperere plus nos aimet.»
férir.tr: For striking well the emperor loves us the more.»

férir.of: Fier de [ta] lance e jo de Durendal,
férir.tr: Strike with [your] lance and I with Durendal,

férir.of: Par penitence les cumandet a ferir.
férir.tr: As penance he commands them to strike.

férir.of: De colps ferir, de receivre e (de) duner!
férir.tr: to strike blows, to receive and (to) give them!

férir.of: Si vunt ferir, que fereient il el?
férir.tr: and they go to strike — what else would they do?

férir.of: Vait le ferir li quens quanque il pout.
férir.tr: The count goes to strike him with all his might.

férir.of: Ferez i, Francs, nostre est li premers colps!
férir.tr: Strike, Franks, the first blows are ours!

férir.of: Vait le ferir en guise de baron.
férir.tr: Goes to strike him in the manner of a baron.

férir.of: Ferez i, Francs, kar tres ben les veincrum!»
férir.tr: Strike, Franks, for we shall surely defeat them!»

férir.of: Par grant vertut si l 'est alet ferir.
férir.tr: With great force he has gone to strike him.

férir.of: Voz cumpaignuns feruns trestuz restifs;
férir.tr: Your companions we shall make all motionless;

férir.of: Ferez, Franceis! Nul de vus ne s'ublit!
férir.tr: Strike, Franks! Let none of you forget himself!

férir.of: Engelers fiert Malprimis de Brigal;
férir.tr: Engelier strikes Malprimis of Brigal;

férir.of: E sis cumpainz Gerers fiert l'amurafle:
férir.tr: And his companion Gerers strikes the emir:

férir.of: Sansun li dux, (il) vait ferir l'almaçur:
férir.tr: Duke Samson, he goes to strike the emir:

férir.of: Si vait ferir Turgis de Turteluse;
férir.tr: Then goes to strike Turgis of Tortelose;

férir.of: Si vait ferir Escremiz de Valterne:
férir.tr: And goes to strike Escremiz of Valterne:

férir.of: Sil fiert el piz entre les dous furceles,
férir.tr: Strikes him in the chest between the two collarbones,

férir.of: E Gualter fie[r]t un paien, Estorgans,
férir.tr: And Gualter strikes a pagan, Estorgant,

férir.of: E Berenger, il fiert Astramariz:
férir.tr: And Berenger, he strikes Astramariz:

férir.of: Le cheval brochet, vait ferir Oliver:
férir.tr: He spurs his horse, goes to strike Oliver:

férir.of: Fiert de l'espiet tant cume hanste li duret;
férir.tr: He strikes with his spear as long as the shaft lasts him;

férir.of: Sun cheval brochet, si vait ferir Chernuble:
férir.tr: He spurs his horse, and goes to strike Chernuble:

férir.of: E Oliver de ferir ne se target,
férir.tr: And Oliver does not delay in striking,

férir.of: E li Franceis i fierent e si caplent.
férir.tr: And the Franks strike and hew there.

férir.of: E vait fer(en)[ir] un paien, Malun:
férir.tr: And he goes to strike a pagan, Malun:

férir.of: «Kar de ferir oi jo si grant bosoign.» AOI.
férir.tr: «For I had such great need to strike.» AOI.

férir.of: Fiert un paien, Justin de Val Ferree:
férir.tr: He strikes a pagan, Justin of Val Ferrée:

férir.of: E vunt ferir un paien, Timozel,
férir.tr: And go to strike a pagan, Timozel,

férir.of: Fierent li un, li altre se defendent.
férir.tr: The one side strikes, the others defend themselves.

férir.of: Mult ben i fiert Oliver e Rollant,
férir.tr: Oliver and Roland strike well there,

férir.of: E li Franceis i fierent cumunement.
férir.tr: And the French strike there all together.

férir.of: Franceis i unt ferut de coer e de vigur;
férir.tr: The French have struck there with heart and with vigor;

férir.of: Jo i ferrai de Durendal, m'espee,
férir.tr: I shall strike there with Durendal, my sword,

férir.of: E vos, compainz, ferrez de Halteclere.
férir.tr: And you, companion, will strike with Hauteclaire.

férir.of: Cum il le vit, a ferir le desiret.
férir.tr: As soon as he saw him, he longed to strike him.

férir.of: Vait le ferir en l'escut amiracle:
férir.tr: He goes to strike him on the marvelous shield:

férir.of: Turpins i fiert, ki nient ne l'esparignet,
férir.tr: Turpin strikes him there, who spares him not at all,

férir.of: Si vait ferir Engeler de Guascoigne.
férir.tr: And goes to strike Engelier of Gascony.

férir.of: Ferez, paien, pur la presse derumpre!»
férir.tr: Strike, pagans, to break the press!»

férir.of: Par grant vertut vait ferir le paien.
férir.tr: With great strength he goes to strike the pagan.

férir.of: A voiz escriet: «Ferez i, chevaler!» AOI.
férir.tr: In a loud voice he cries out: «Strike here, knights!» AOI.

férir.of: Si vait ferir li riche duc Sansun,
férir.tr: And goes to strike the mighty duke Samson,

férir.of: «Ferez paien, car tres ben les veintrum!»
férir.tr: «Strike, pagans, for we shall vanquish them right well!»

férir.of: Vait le ferir li bers, quanque il pout,
férir.tr: The baron goes to strike him with all his strength,

férir.of: Il vait ferir Anseïs en l'escut:
férir.tr: He goes to strike Anseïs on the shield:

férir.of: Si l'ad ferut sur l'escut de Tulette,
férir.tr: And struck him upon the shield of Toledo,

férir.of: Si vait ferir Gerin par sa grant force.
férir.tr: And goes to strike Gerin with his great force.

férir.of: Puis vait ferir un riche duc Austorje,
férir.tr: Then he goes to strike a rich duke, Austorge,

férir.of: Li quens le fiert tant vertuusement
férir.tr: The count strikes him so mightily

férir.of: Dient Franceis: «Ben fiert nostre guarent!»
férir.tr: The Franks say: «Well does our protector strike!»

férir.of: Franceis i ferent des espiez brunisant.
férir.tr: The French strike there with their burnished spears.

férir.of: Franceis i ferent par vigur e par ire,
férir.tr: The French strike there with vigor and with wrath,

férir.of: Ben set ferir e de lance e d'espiet.»
férir.tr: He well knows how to strike with both lance and spear.»

férir.of: De lur espees e ferir e capler!
férir.tr: striking and hewing with their swords!

férir.of: Li arcevesque i fiert de sun espiet,
férir.tr: The archbishop strikes there with his spear,

férir.of: Cascun le fiert .IIII. colps de sun puign;
férir.tr: Each strikes him four blows with his fist;

férir.of: Ensembl'od lui i ferrunt veirement.
férir.tr: There at his side they will truly strike their blows.

férir.of: Tient Durendal, cume vassal i fiert.
férir.tr: He holds Durendal, and strikes like a brave warrior.

férir.of: Respunt Rollant: «Ferez, nes esparignez!»
férir.tr: Roland answers: «Strike, do not spare them!»

férir.of: Brochet le ben, si vait ferir Bevon,
férir.tr: spurs it well, and goes to strike Beuve,

férir.of: Vait le ferir en guise de baron:
férir.tr: He goes to strike him in the guise of a baron:

férir.of: Ferez, seignurs, des espees furbies,
férir.tr: Strike, lords, with your burnished swords,

férir.of: Ferez Franceis, car jol vos recumenz!»
férir.tr: Strike, Franks, for I begin it again for you!»

férir.of: A icest mot Franceis se fierent enz.
férir.tr: At this word the Franks hurl themselves in.

férir.of: Fiert Oliver derere en mi le dos.
férir.tr: He strikes Oliver from behind, in the middle of the back.

férir.of: Oliver sent que a mort est ferut.
férir.tr: Oliver feels that he is wounded to death.

férir.of: Fiert Marganices sur l'elme a or, agut,
férir.tr: He strikes Marganices on the helmet of gold, pointed,

férir.of: En la grant presse or i fiert cume ber,
férir.tr: In the great throng he now strikes like a baron,

férir.of: Sil fiert amunt sur l'elme a or gemet:
férir.tr: He strikes him up high upon the gem-set golden helm:

férir.of: Ferut vos ai, car le me pardunez!»
férir.tr: I have struck you, so forgive me for it!»

gager.of: Guaz vos en dreit par cez pels sabelines;
gager.tr: I pledge you amends by these sable furs;

garantir.of: Jo ne vos pois tenser ne guarantir.
garantir.tr: I cannot protect you nor save you.

garder.of: Nes poet guarder que mals ne l'i ateignet. AOI.
garder.tr: he cannot keep harm from reaching him there. AOI.

garder.of: Nes poet guarder que alques ne l'engignent. AOI.
garder.tr: He cannot guard against their deceiving him in some way. AOI.

garder.of: Gua[r]dez le ben, ja nel verrai des oilz.»
garder.tr: Guard him well, for I shall never see him again with my eyes.»

garder.of: Guardet al bref, vit la raisun escrite:
garder.tr: He looks at the letter, saw the writing set down there:

garder.of: Guardez de nos ne turnez le curage.
garder.tr: Take care that you do not turn your heart away from us.

garder.of: E .XX. hostages; faites les ben guarder!
garder.tr: And twenty hostages; have them well guarded!

garder.of: Que il ne voelent ne tenir ne guarder.
garder.tr: Which they wish neither to hold nor to keep.

garder.of: Or guart chascuns que granz colps (l')[i] empleit,
garder.tr: Now let each man take care to strike great blows there,

garder.of: Guardet su destre par mi un val herbus,
garder.tr: He looks to his right across a grassy valley,

garder.of: Guardez amunt devers les porz d'Espaigne:
garder.tr: Look up toward the passes of Spain:

garder.of: Traït vos ad ki a guarder vos out.
garder.tr: He who should have guarded you has betrayed you.

garder.of: Guardet a tere, veit gesir le glutun,
garder.tr: He looks to the ground, sees the wretch lying there,

garder.of: Guardet arere, veit le glutun gesir,
garder.tr: He looks behind, sees the wretch lying there,

garder.of: «Ben le me guarde, si cume tel felon!
garder.tr: «Guard him well for me, as befits such a felon!

garder.of: Tant le guardent quel rendent a Charlun.
garder.tr: They guard him until they hand him back to Charles.

gemmer.of: Luisent cil elme, ki ad or sunt gemmez,
gemmer.tr: The helmets gleam, which are gemmed with gold,

gemmer.of: La bone sele, ki a ór est gemmee,
gemmer.tr: The good saddle, which is jeweled with gold,

gracier.of: Ço dist li reis: «Graciet en seit Deus!
gracier.tr: Thus said the king: «May God be thanked for it!

guerroyer.of: N'avrat talent, que ja mais vus guerreit.» AOI.
guerroyer.tr: He will have no desire ever again to make war on you.» AOI.

guerroyer.of: Cil ne sunt proz ja mais pur guerreier.
guerroyer.tr: They will never again be of use for waging war.

guider.tr: «At Roncevaux I will lead my company,

guérir.of: Si m(e) guarisez e de mort et de hunte.»
guérir.tr: And save me from death and from shame.»
Charles answers: «He may yet be saved.» AOI.

guérir.of: Par Charlemagne n'er(cs) guariz ne tensez.
guérir.tr: Will not be protected nor defended by Charlemagne.

guérir.of: Asoldrai vos pur voz anmes guarir.
guérir.tr: I shall absolve you to save your souls.

guérir.of: Ja pur Charles n'i ert un sul guarit:
guérir.tr: Never, for all of Charles, shall a single one be saved:

guérir.of: Deus le guarit, qu'el(l) cors ne l'ad tuchet.
guérir.tr: God protects him, for it did not touch his body.

guérir.of: De cent millers n'en poent guarir dous.
guérir.tr: Of a hundred thousand not two of them can escape.

guérir.of: Nel poet guarir sun escut ne sa bronie:
guérir.tr: Neither his shield nor his hauberk can protect him:

guérir.of: E p[ri]ent Deu qu'il guarisset Rollant
guérir.tr: And they pray to God that He keep Roland safe

gâter.of: Carles li magnes ad Espaigne guastede
gâter.tr: Charles the Great has laid waste to Spain,

gésir.of: Gesir porrum el burc de seint Denise.»
gésir.tr: We shall be able to lie in the town of Saint Denis.»

gésir.of: Guardet a tere, veit gesir le glutun,
gésir.tr: He looks to the ground, sees the wretch lying there,

gésir.of: Guardet arere, veit le glutun gesir,
gésir.tr: He looks behind, sees the wretch lying there,

gésir.of: Li sanc tuz clers gesir par cele place!
gésir.tr: The bright blood lying all over that place!

gésir.of: L'un gist sur l'altre e envers e adenz.
gésir.tr: One lies upon another, both on his back and face down.

gésir.of: Tanz bons vassals veez gesir par tere!
gésir.tr: So many good vassals you see lying upon the ground!

gésir.of: Ne jerrei(e)z ja mais entre sa brace!» AOI.
gésir.tr: You shall never lie in her arms!» AOI.

gésir.of: Desur (...) sa brunie li gist sa blanche barbe.
gésir.tr: Over his hauberk hangs his white beard.

gésir.of: De cels de France i veit tanz morz gesir!
gésir.tr: of those of France he sees so many lying dead!

gésir.of: En seintes flurs il les facet gesir!
gésir.tr: May he make them lie among holy flowers!

gésir.of: Gesir adenz, a la tere sun vis,
gésir.tr: Lying face down, his face to the earth,

haïr.of: Suz ciel n'at hume que [tant] voeillet haïr;
haïr.tr: Under heaven there is no man he would so wish to hate;

honnir.of: Par quel mesure le poüssum hunir.»
honnir.tr: As to by what means we might dishonor him.»

honnir.of: Franceis murrunt e France en ert hunie;
honnir.tr: The French will die and France will be shamed by it;

honnir.of: Que dulce France par nus ne seit hunie!
honnir.tr: Let sweet France not be dishonored through us!

hâter.tr: Who urge themselves on to battle, and make haste:

héberger.of: Franc se herbergent par tute la cuntree.
héberger.tr: The Franks make camp throughout the countryside.

incliner.of: Li reis paiens parfundement l 'enclinet. AOI.
incliner.tr: The pagan king bows deeply to him. AOI.

incliner.of: A icel mot l'un a l'altre ad clinet.
incliner.tr: At that word the one bowed to the other.

issir.of: Ist de la prese, si se met en bandun,
issir.tr: He springs out of the press and flings himself forward,

issir.of: Fors s'en eissirent li Sarrazins dedenz,

jeter.of: De sun col getet ses grandes pels de martre,
jeter.tr: From his neck he throws his great pelts of marten,

jeter.of: Cuntre dous deie l'ad del furrer getee,
jeter.tr: He drew it two fingers' width out of the scabbard,

jeter.of: Getet le a tere, sil receit Blancandrin;
jeter.tr: He throws it to the ground, and Blancandrin catches it;

jeter.of: Getet serez sur un malvais sumer.
jeter.tr: You will be thrown upon a wretched pack-horse.

jeter.of: Freint le seel, getet en ad la cire,
jeter.tr: He breaks the seal, has cast away the wax,

jeter.of: Od sun espiet l'anme li getet fors,
jeter.tr: With his spear casts out his soul,

jeter.of: Ki lui veïst l'un geter mort su[r] l'altre,
jeter.tr: Whoever had seen him throw one dead upon another,

jeter.of: Osbercs e helmes i getent grant flambur,
jeter.tr: Hauberks and helmets cast forth a great blaze of light,
Throw one dead man upon another,

joindre.of: Qu'il devendrat jointes ses mains tis hom,
joindre.tr: That he will become, his hands joined, your vassal,

joindre.of: En Rencesvals a Rollant irai juindre,
joindre.tr: At Roncevaux I will go to join Roland,

joindre.of: Cuntre le ciel ambesdous ses mains juintes,
joindre.tr: Toward heaven both his hands joined,

jouer.of: As tables juent pur els esbaneier
jouer.tr: At backgammon they play to amuse themselves
«To Roncevaux I will go to disport my body!

jouer.of: Unches nuls hom nel vit juer ne rire.
jouer.tr: Never did any man see him play or laugh.

jouter.tr: «Felon French, today you shall joust with ours,

jouter.of: «Sire cumpaign, a mei car vus justez!
jouter.tr: «Lord companion, come now, join me here!

jouter.of: Trestut le cors a la tere li justet.
jouter.tr: His whole body sinks down to the ground.

juger.of: Li duze per mar i serunt jugez!»
juger.tr: The twelve peers would be ill-judged [for it]!»

juger.of: Si as juget qu'a Marsiliun en alge!
juger.tr: So you have decided that I should go to Marsile!

juger.of: Oït l'avez, sur vos le jugent Franc.»
juger.tr: You have heard it, the Franks judge it against you.»

juger.of: Ki ço jugat, que doüsez aler
juger.tr: He who decreed that you should go

juger.of: Pois me jugez Rollant a rereguarde.
juger.tr: Then assign Roland to me for the rearguard.

juger.of: Kar me jugez, ki ert en la rereguarde.»
juger.tr: Now judge for me, who shall be in the rearguard.»

juger.of: Li quens Rollant quant il s'oït juger,
juger.tr: Count Roland, when he hears himself thus assigned,

juger.of: La rereguarde avez sur mei jugiet.
juger.tr: You have assigned the rearguard to me.

juger.of: La rereguarde est jugee (...) sur lui:
juger.tr: The rearguard has been assigned (...) to him:

juger.of: Chi ad juget mis nes a (la) rereguarde.
juger.tr: He who has assigned my nephew to the rearguard.

juger.of: Il est juget que nus les ocirum.» AOI.
juger.tr: It is judged that we shall kill them.» AOI.

juger.of: Li .XII. per tuit sunt jugez a perdre;
juger.tr: The Twelve Peers are all condemned to perish;
Who judged us before the emperor.»

juger.of: Jo vos plevis, tuz sunt jugez a mort.» AOI.
juger.tr: I pledge you, all are condemned to death.» AOI.

juger.of: El plait ad Ais en fut juget a prendre,
juger.tr: At the trial at Aix he was condemned to be seized,

jurer.of: La traïsun me jurrez de Rollant si illi est.»
jurer.tr: You will swear to me the betrayal of Roland, if it is so.»

jurer.of: La traïsun jurat, e si s'en est forsfait. AOI.
jurer.tr: He swore the betrayal, and thus made himself guilty of it. AOI.

jurer.of: Ço ad juret li Sarrazins espans:
jurer.tr: The Spanish Saracen has sworn this:

jurer.of: Guenes li fels ad nostre mort juree;
jurer.tr: Ganelon the traitor has sworn our death;

lacer.of: Healmes lacez e ceintes lur espees,
lacer.tr: helmets laced and their swords girded,

lacer.of: Lacent lor elmes mult bons sarraguzeis,
lacer.tr: They lace on their fine Saragossan helmets,

lacer.of: Helmes laciez e blancs osbercs vestuz
lacer.tr: helmets laced on and white hauberks donned.

lacer.of: Laciet en su un gunfanun tut blanc;
lacer.tr: A pure white gonfalon laced upon it;

lacer.of: Lacent cil'elme as perres d'or gemmees,
lacer.tr: They lace on those helmets gemmed with gold,

laisser.of: Laissun les fols, as sages nus tenuns.» AOI.
laisser.tr: Let us leave the fools, let us hold to the wise.» AOI.

laisser.of: E dist al rei: «Laisez ester voz Francs!
laisser.tr: And said to the king: «Let your Franks be!

laisser.of: Se lui lessez, n'i trametrez plus saive.»
laisser.tr: If you leave him aside, you will not send a wiser one.»

laisser.of: A lui lais jo mes honurs e mes fieus.
laisser.tr: To him I leave my lands and my fiefs.

laisser.of: Jo ne lerreie, por l'or que Deus fist
laisser.tr: I would not refrain, for all the gold that God made

laisser.of: Lessez (la) folie, tenez vos al saveir.
laisser.tr: Leave aside folly, hold to wisdom.

laisser.of: Sa rereguarde lerrat derere sei:
laisser.tr: His rearguard he will leave behind him:

laisser.of: Demi mun host vos lerrai en present.
laisser.tr: Half my host I will leave to you here and now.

laisser.of: As porz d'Espaigne ad lesset sun nevold.
laisser.tr: At the passes of Spain he has left his nephew.

laisser.of: Jo l'ai lesset en une estrange marche!
laisser.tr: I have left him in a strange land!

laisser.of: Ne lesserat bataille ne lur dunt.
laisser.tr: Will not fail to give them battle.

laisser.of: Se truis Rollant, ne lerrai que nel mat!»
laisser.tr: If I find Roland, I will not fail to bring him down!»

laisser.of: Laissent les mulz e tuz les palefreiz,
laisser.tr: They leave behind the mules and all the palfreys,

laisser.of: Li emperere, ki Franceis nos laisat,
laisser.tr: The emperor, who left the French to us,

laisser.of: «Seignurs baruns, Carles nus laissat ci;
laisser.tr: «Lord barons, Charles left us here;

laisser.of: Fols est li reis ki vos laissat as porz.
laisser.tr: Foolish is the king who left you at the passes.

laisser.of: Sun cheval brochet, laiset curre a esforz,
laisser.tr: He spurs his horse, lets it run at full force,

laisser.of: Ne leserat, ço dit, que n'i parolt:
laisser.tr: He will not refrain, he says, from speaking there:

laisser.of: Il fist que proz qu'il nus laisad as porz:
laisser.tr: He acted as a brave man when he left us at the passes:

laisser.of: Ne laisserat que n'i parolt, ço dit:
laisser.tr: He will not refrain from speaking to him, he says this:
