laisser.of: E Anseïs laiset le cheval curre,
laisser.tr: And Anseïs lets his horse run,

laisser.of: Voillet o nun, tut i laisset sun tens.
laisser.tr: Willing or not, each leaves his life there.

laisser.of: Ne laisserat qu'Abisme nen asaillet;
laisser.tr: He will not fail to assail Abisme;

laisser.of: Ne lesserat que nos ne beneïsse.» AOI.
laisser.tr: He will not fail to bless us.» AOI.

laisser.of: Carles li magnes mar vos laissat as porz!
laisser.tr: Charlemagne left you at the passes to your sorrow!

laver.of: Puis od les ewes lavat les prez del sanc,
laver.tr: Then with the waters he washed the meadows of the blood,

lever.of: Li empereres est par matin levet;
lever.tr: The emperor is risen at morning;

lever.of: Turpins de Reins en est levet del renc,
lever.tr: Turpin of Reims has risen from the ranks,

lever.of: Ambes ses mains en levat cuntre munt,
lever.tr: He raised both his hands toward the heavens,

lever.of: Li empereres est par matin levet;
lever.tr: The emperor has risen at morning;

lever.of: En sur un tertre cuntre le ciel levee.
lever.tr: raised it up on a hilltop against the sky.

lever.of: Mahumet levent en la plus halte tur.
lever.tr: They raise up Mohammed on the highest tower.
He stood godfather to King Marsile,

lever.of: Leverunt nos en bieres sur sumers,
lever.tr: They will lift us onto biers on pack-horses,

lier.of: Pris e liez serez par poested;
lier.tr: You will be seized and bound by force,

lier.of: Par poestet serez pris e liez;
lier.tr: By force you will be taken and bound;

livrer.of: Livrez m'en ore le guant e le bastun.»
livrer.tr: Hand me now the glove and the staff.»

livrer.of: Puis li livrat le bastun e le bref.
livrer.tr: Then he handed over to him the staff and the letter.

livrer.of: El destre poign al paien l'ad liv(e)ret.
livrer.tr: Into the right fist of the pagan he handed it over.

livrer.of: Livrez le mei, jo en ferai la justise.»
livrer.tr: Hand him over to me, I will do justice on him for it.»

livrer.of: Altre bataille lur livrez de meïsme:

livrer.tr: I will give him a mortal battle.»

livrer.of: Une bataille lur livrat le jur pesme. AOI.
livrer.tr: Gave them a fierce battle that grievous day. AOI.

livrer.of: Jo vos plevis, tuz sunt a mort livrez.» AOI.
livrer.tr: I pledge you, they are all delivered up to death.» AOI.

louer.of: Ben en purrat luer ses soldeiers.
louer.tr: Well will he be able to pay his soldiers with it.

louer.of: Dunt bien purrez voz soldeiers luer.
louer.tr: that you will well be able to pay your soldiers.

louer.of: Ki ço vos lodet que cest plait degetuns,
louer.tr: Whoever advises you that we reject this agreement,

louer.of: Loat sun Deu, ne fist altre respuns.
louer.tr: Praised his God, made no other reply.

louer.of: Tant nel vos sai ne preiser ne loer
louer.tr: I cannot so praise him to you nor extol him

louer.of: Ambure ocit, ki quel blasme ne quil lot.
louer.tr: He kills them both, let who will blame or praise it.

louer.of: Tort nos ad fait: nen est dreiz qu'il s'en lot,
louer.tr: He has wronged us: it is not right that he should boast of it,

luire.of: Soleill n'i luist, ne blet n'i poet pas creistre,
luire.tr: The sun does not shine there, nor can grain grow there,

luire.of: Luisent cil elme, ki ad or sunt gemmez,
luire.tr: The helmets gleam, which are gemmed with gold,

luire.of: Dreites cez hanstes, luisent cil espiet brun.
luire.tr: Upright are those lances, those burnished spearheads gleam.

luire.of: L'elme li freint u li carbuncle luisent,
luire.tr: He breaks his helm where the carbuncles gleam,

luire.of: Cuntre le ciel sur tuz les altres luist.
luire.tr: Against the sky he shines above all the others.

lâcher.of: Sun cheval (...) brochet, si li laschet la resne,
lâcher.tr: Spurs his horse, lets loose the rein,

lâcher.of: Laschent lor reisnes, brochent amdui a ait,
lâcher.tr: They slacken their reins, both spur eagerly,

lâcher.of: Laschet la resne, des esperuns le brochet,
lâcher.tr: He slackens the rein, spurs it with his spurs,

mander.of: Mandez Carlun a l'orguillus, (e) al fier,
mander.tr: Send word to Charles, to the proud, to the fierce,

mander.of: Iço vus mandet reis Marsilies, li bers:
mander.tr: This is what King Marsile, the baron, sends word to you:

mander.of: Ses baruns mandet pur sun cunseill finer:
mander.tr: His barons he summons to settle his counsel:

mander.of: Ses baruns mandet pur sun cunseill fenir:
mander.tr: He summons his barons to settle his counsel:

mander.of: Mais il me mandet que en France m'en alge:
mander.tr: But he commands me that I depart from France:

mander.of: Quant ço vos mandet li reis Marsiliun,
mander.tr: When the king Marsile sends you word of this,

mander.of: Quant il vos mandet, qu'aiez mercit de lui,
mander.tr: When he sends word to you, that you have mercy on him,

mander.of: Iço vus mandet Carlemagnes, li ber,
mander.tr: This is what Charlemagne the baron commands you:

mander.of: Que Charles li mandet, li reis poesteïfs,
mander.tr: What Charles commands him, the mighty king,

mander.of: Par mei li mandet, sun mortel enemi.»
mander.tr: Commands him through me, his mortal enemy.»

mander.of: Quar ço vos mandet Carles, ki France tient,
mander.tr: For thus Charles, who rules France, commands you:

mander.of: «Carle me mandet, ki France ad en baillie,
mander.tr: «Charles, who has France in his keeping, commands me,

mander.of: E si vos mandet reis Marsilies li ber,
mander.tr: And so King Marsile the brave sends word to you,
Marsile summons the barons of Spain,
In what way shall we send him news?»

manger.of: N'en mangerunt ne lu ne porc ne chen.»
manger.tr: Neither wolf nor pig nor dog shall eat of us.»

mater.of: Se truis Rollant, ne lerrai que nel mat!»
mater.tr: If I find Roland, I will not fail to bring him down!»

mendier.of: Ne nus seiuns cunduiz a mendeier.» AOI.
mendier.tr: And that we be reduced to begging.» AOI.

mener.of: En Sarraguce menez vostre ost banie,
mener.tr: To Saragossa lead your summoned host,

mener.of: Enpres li dient: «Sire, car nos menez!»
mener.tr: Then they say to him: «Lord, do lead us along!»

mener.of: Menet serez . . . [tut] dreit ad Ais le siet:
mener.tr: You will be led straight to the seat of Aix:

mener.of: Enz el verger l'en meinet josqu'al rei.
mener.tr: Into the orchard he leads him as far as the king.

mener.of: Recreanz ert de sa guerre mener,
mener.tr: He will be weary of waging his war,

mener.of: Itels .C. milie Sarrazins od els meinent,
mener.tr: Such a hundred thousand Saracens they lead with them,

mentir.tr: «Vile pagan, you have lied in this!

mentir.of: Aït vos Deus, ki unkes ne mentit!
mentir.tr: May God aid you, who never lied!

mesurer.of: Grant demi pied mesurer i pout hom.
mesurer.tr: A good half-foot one could measure there.

mettre.of: Li frein sunt d'or, les seles d'argent mises.
mettre.tr: The bridles are of gold, the saddles set with silver.

mettre.of: Pa[r] num de ocire i metrai un mien filz,
mettre.tr: At risk of being slain, I will put there a son of mine,

mettre.of: Metez le sege a tute vostre vie,
mettre.tr: Lay the siege for the whole of your life,

mettre.of: E tutes teres met en chalengement!
mettre.tr: And puts all lands into contention!

mettre.of: Or e argent lur met tant en present,
mettre.tr: He puts so much gold and silver before them as gifts,

mettre.of: Quant le vit Guenes, mist la main a l'espee,
mettre.tr: When Ganelon saw it, he put his hand to his sword,

mettre.of: Sa rereguarde avrat detres sei mise;
mettre.tr: He will have placed his rearguard behind him;

mettre.of: A Durendal jo la metrai encuntre;
mettre.tr: Against Durendal I will set it;

mettre.of: Tere Majur vos metrum en present.
mettre.tr: We will deliver the Great Land to you.

mettre.of: L'empereor vos metrum en present.»
mettre.tr: We will hand over the emperor to you.»

mettre.of: Jo vos plevis qu'en vermeill sanc ert mise.
mettre.tr: I pledge to you that it will be set in crimson blood.

mettre.of: Itels .XX. milie en mist a une part
mettre.tr: Set apart such twenty thousand men

mettre.of: Franceis de[s]cendent, a tere se sunt mis,
mettre.tr: The Franks dismount, they prostrated themselves on the ground,

mettre.of: Franceis se drecent, si se metent sur piez.
mettre.tr: The French rise up and get to their feet.

mettre.of: Ist de la prese, si se met en bandun,
mettre.tr: He springs out of the press and flings himself forward,

mettre.of: El cors li met les pans del gunfanun,
mettre.tr: Drives into his body the folds of the gonfanon,

mettre.of: Sun grant espiet par mi le cors li mist,
mettre.tr: He thrust his great spear through the middle of his body,

mettre.of: Sun bon espiet li me(n)t en la curaille,
mettre.tr: He thrusts his good spear into his belly,

mettre.of: Del bon espiet el cors li met la mure,
mettre.tr: Drives the point of his good spear into his body,

mettre.of: Empeinst le ben, tut le fer li mist ultre,
mettre.tr: Thrusts it well, sends the iron clean through him,

mettre.of: El cors li met sun bon espiet tre[n]chant,
mettre.tr: Into his body he drives his good sharp spear,

mettre.of: Sun fort escut par mi le cors li mist,
mettre.tr: His strong spear he thrust through the middle of his body,

mettre.of: Fors de la teste li met les oilz ansdous,
mettre.tr: From his head he drives out both his eyes,

mettre.of: Tere Major ço dit, metrat a hunte,
mettre.tr: The Great Land, he says, he will put to shame,

mettre.of: De sun espiet el cors li met la mure,
mettre.tr: He thrusts the point of his spear into his body,

mettre.of: Empeint le ben, tut le fer li mist ultre,
mettre.tr: Drives it in hard, sends all the iron through,

mettre.of: El cors li met les pans del gunfanun,
mettre.tr: Into his body he drives the folds of the gonfanon,

mettre.of: El cors li met e le fer e le fust;
mettre.tr: He thrusts into his body both the iron and the shaft;

mettre.of: El cors li met tute l'enseingne bloie,
mettre.tr: Drives into his body all the blue ensign,

mettre.of: Rollant ad mis l'olifan a sa buche,
mettre.tr: Roland has put the oliphant to his mouth,

mettre.of: Cil le receit, si met .C. cumpaignons
mettre.tr: The cook receives him, and sets a hundred companions

mettre.of: E si li metent el col un caeignun,
mettre.tr: And they put a chain around his neck,

mettre.of: Sur un sumer l'unt mis a deshonor.
mettre.tr: On a pack-horse they have set him in dishonor.

mettre.of: En ceste tere nus ad mis tels feluns!
mettre.tr: He has set such villains in this land!

mettre.of: Par mi le piz sun espiet li mist fors,
mettre.tr: He thrust his spear out through the middle of his breast,

monter.of: Cil sunt muntez ki le message firent,
monter.tr: Those who carried the message have mounted,

monter.of: Cunseill d orguill n'est dreiz que a plus munt,
monter.tr: Counsel of pride, it is not right that it rise further,

monter.of: Ceste grant guerre ne deit munter a plus.»
monter.tr: This great war ought not to mount up to more.»

monter.of: En Tachebrun, sun destrer est munted;
monter.tr: He has mounted Tachebrun, his warhorse;

monter.of: Pois est munted, entret en sun veiage. AOI.
monter.tr: Then he mounted, and set out on his journey. AOI.

monter.of: Li quens Rollant est muntet el destrer.
monter.tr: Count Roland has mounted his warhorse.

monter.of: Puis que il est sur un cheval muntet,
monter.tr: Once he is mounted upon a horse,

monter.of: Es destrers muntent, si chevalchent estreiz.
monter.tr: They mount their war-horses and ride in close ranks.

monter.of: Oliver est desur un pui haut muntez,
monter.tr: Oliver has climbed up onto a high hill,

monter.of: Oliver est desur un pui muntet;
monter.tr: Olivier has climbed up onto a hill;

monter.of: Sun cheval broche e muntet un lariz,
monter.tr: He spurs his horse and climbs a hillock,

monter.of: Puis sunt muntez sur lur curanz destrers.
monter.tr: Then they have mounted their swift war-horses.

monter.of: Es destrers muntent tuit li barun de l'ost,
monter.tr: Onto their war-horses mount all the barons of the host,

montrer.of: E il li ad cum chevaler mustree.
montrer.tr: And he has shown it to him in knightly fashion.

mordre.of: El destre braz li morst uns vers si mals.
mordre.tr: In his right arm a fierce boar bit him.

mourir.of: Ne li chalt, sire, de quel mort nus muriuns.
mourir.tr: Cares not, sire, by what death we may die.

mourir.of: Mielz est que sul moerge que tant bon chevaler.
mourir.tr: Better that I alone die than so many good knights.

mourir.of: La murrez vus a hunte e a viltet.»
mourir.tr: There you will die in shame and in disgrace.»

mourir.of: Que suls i moerge en l'estrange cuntree,
mourir.tr: That I died alone in the foreign land,

mourir.of: Meilz voelt murir que guerpir sun barnet.»
mourir.tr: He would rather die than abandon his noble company.»

mourir.of: Tanz riches reis morz e vencuz en champ:
mourir.tr: So many rich kings dead and vanquished in the field:

mourir.of: Mort sunt li cunte, se est ki mei en creit.
mourir.tr: The counts are dead, if there is anyone who believes me in this.

mourir.of: - «Chi purreit faire, que Rollant i fust mort,
mourir.tr: - «Whoever could bring it about that Roland be killed there,

mourir.of: E, se il poet, murrat i veirement.
mourir.tr: And, if he can, he will truly die there.

mourir.of: N'en unt poür ne de murir dutance.
mourir.tr: They have no fear nor dread of dying.

mourir.of: Franceis murrunt a doel e a viltiet.
mourir.tr: The French will die in grief and in shame.

mourir.of: Franceis murrunt, si a nus s'abandunent;
mourir.tr: The French will die, if they engage with us;

mourir.of: Franceis murrunt e France en ert deserte,
mourir.tr: The French shall die and France will be left desolate,

mourir.of: Franceis murrunt, Carles en ert dolent.
mourir.tr: The French will die, Charles will be grieved by it.

mourir.of: Franceis murrunt e France en ert hunie;
mourir.tr: The French will die and France will be shamed by it;

mourir.of: Franceis murrunt e France en ert deserte.»
mourir.tr: The French shall die and France shall be left desolate.»

mourir.of: Ja pur murir ne vus en faldrat uns.» AOI.
mourir.tr: Never, even to escape death, will a single one of us fail you.» AOI.

mourir.of: Melz voeill murir que huntage me venget.
mourir.tr: I would rather die than that shame befall me.

mourir.of: Ja pur murir n'eschiverunt bataille.
mourir.tr: Never, even on pain of death, will they shun battle.

mourir.of: Se jo i moert, dire poet ki l'avrat
mourir.tr: If I die here, whoever has it may say

mourir.of: Pur nostre rei devum nus ben murir.
mourir.tr: For our king we must surely die.

mourir.of: Se vos murez, esterez seinz martirs,
mourir.tr: If you die, you will be holy martyrs,

mourir.of: Or est le jur qu'els estuvrat murir.»
mourir.tr: Now is the day when they must die.»

mourir.of: Moerent paien e alquanz en i pasment.
mourir.tr: The pagans die and several of them faint there.

mourir.of: Ki de murir nen ourent esperance. AOI.
mourir.tr: Who had no hope of escaping death. AOI.

mourir.of: Moerent paien a miller(e)[s] e a cent;
mourir.tr: Pagans die there by thousands and by hundreds;

mourir.of: Paien sunt morz a millers e a fuls:
mourir.tr: The pagans are dead by the thousands and in droves:

mourir.of: Asez est mielz que moerium cumbatant.
mourir.tr: It is much better that we die fighting.

mourir.of: «Sire cumpainz, ja est morz Engeler;
mourir.tr: «Sir companion, Engelier is now dead;

mourir.of: Li quens Rollant, quant il veit Sansun mort,
mourir.tr: Count Roland, when he sees Samson dead,

mourir.of: Morz est li quens, de sun tens n'i ad plus.
mourir.tr: The count is dead, of his time there is no more.

mourir.of: Cels qu'il unt mort, ben les poet hom preiser;
mourir.tr: those whom they have slain, one can well count them;

mourir.of: Einz que il moergent, se vendrunt mult cher.
mourir.tr: Before they die, they will sell themselves very dear.

mourir.of: Mielz voeill murir que hunte nus seit retraite.» AOI.
mourir.tr: I would rather die than that shame be reported of us.» AOI.

mourir.of: Franceis sunt morz par vostre legerie.
mourir.tr: The French are dead through your rashness.

mourir.of: U pris ú mort i fust li reis Marsilie.
mourir.tr: Captured or killed would have been King Marsile.

mourir.of: Vos i murrez e France en ert (...) huníe.
mourir.tr: You will die here and France will be shamed by it.

mourir.of: «Se veïssum Rollant einz qu'il fust mortz,
mourir.tr: «If only we might see Roland before he is dead,

mourir.of: (Morz est Turpin le guerreier Charlun)
mourir.tr: (Turpin, the warrior of Charles, is dead)

mourir.of: Barons Franceis, pur mei vos vei murir:
mourir.tr: French barons, for my sake I see you die:

mourir.of: De doel murra, se altre ne m'i ocit.
mourir.tr: I shall die of grief, if nothing else slays me.

mourir.of: Que hoi murrum par le mien escient.
mourir.tr: That today we shall die, to my knowledge.

mourir.of: Morz est li quens, que plus ne se demuret.
mourir.tr: The count is dead, he lingers no longer.

mourir.of: Or veit Rollant que mort est sun ami,
mourir.tr: Now Roland sees that his friend is dead,

mourir.of: Quant tu es mor[t], dulur est que jo vif!»
mourir.tr: Since you are dead, it is grief that I live!»

mouvoir.of: Jo t'en muvra[i] un si grant contr[a]ire
mouvoir.tr: I will stir up against you such a great calamity

mouvoir.of: Apres li dist: «Culvert, mar i moüstes!
mouvoir.tr: Afterward he says to him: «Coward, in an evil hour you set forth!

muer.of: Set cenz cameilz e mil hosturs muez,
muer.tr: seven hundred camels and a thousand moulted hawks,

muer.of: Li reis Marsilies ad la culur muee;
Ne poet muer que des oilz ne plurt.
muer.tr: He cannot help but weep from his eyes.

muer.of: Pitet l'en prent, ne poet muer n'en plurt. AOI.
muer.tr: Pity takes him, he cannot but weep. AOI.

muer.of: Si grant doel ai ne puis muer nel pleigne.
muer.tr: Such great grief I have that I cannot keep from lamenting it.

muer.of: Carles li magnes ne poet muer n'en plurt.
muer.tr: Charlemagne cannot help but weep at it.

muer.of: Quant ele le veit, ne poet muer ne riet;
muer.tr: When she sees him, she cannot help but smile;

muer.of: Ne poet muer qu'il ne s'en espoent,
muer.tr: He cannot help but be terrified by it,

mêler.of: Jo me crendreie, que vos vos meslisez.
mêler.tr: I would fear that you would come to blows.

navrer.of: Oliver sent qu'il est a mort nasfret.
navrer.tr: Oliver feels that he is wounded to death.

navrer.of: E Oliver ki est a mort nasfret:
navrer.tr: And Oliver, who is wounded to death:

nombrer.tr: The king has counted twenty battalions.

nourrir.of: Li empereres tant mare vos nurrit!
nourrir.tr: The emperor raised you for such an ill fate!

noyer.of: La sunt neiez, jamais nes en verrez;
noyer.tr: There they were drowned, never will you see them again;

occire.of: Par nun d'ocire i enveierai le men.
occire.tr: Even at the risk of death I will send mine.

occire.of: Ne seit ocis, u devient chrestien.
occire.tr: Who is not slain, or becomes a Christian.

occire.of: Pa[r] num de ocire i metrai un mien filz,
occire.tr: At risk of being slain, I will put there a son of mine,

occire.of: Si vengez cels que li fels fist ocire!» AOI.
occire.tr: And avenge those whom the villain had killed!» AOI.

occire.of: Seit, ki l'ociet, tute pais puis avriúmes.» AOI.
occire.tr: Were someone to slay him, we should then have full peace.» AOI.

occire.of: Que il querreient, que Rollant fust ocis.
occire.tr: That they would seek a way for Roland to be killed.

occire.of: «Cum faitement purrai Rollant ocire?»
occire.tr: «How might I be able to kill Roland?»

occire.of: Jo l'ocirai a mun espiet trenchant.
occire.tr: I will kill him with my sharp spear.

occire.of: Il est juget que nus les ocirum.» AOI.
occire.tr: It is judged that we shall kill them.» AOI.

occire.of: En Rencesvals irai Rollant ocire,
occire.tr: To Roncevaux I will go to slay Roland,

occire.of: Des .XII. pers li .X. en sunt ocis;
occire.tr: Of the Twelve Peers, ten of them are slain;

occire.of: Pois ad ocis Turgis e Esturguz;
occire.tr: Then he has slain Turgis and Esturguz;

occire.of: E l'arcevesque lor ocist Siglorel,
occire.tr: And the archbishop slew Siglorel for them,

occire.of: Mielz est mult que jo l'alge ocire.
occire.tr: It is far better that I go and slay him.

occire.of: Ço ert uns reis qu'l ocist en Denemarche.
occire.tr: He was a king whom he slew in Denmark.

occire.of: Puis ad ocis le duc Alphaïen;
occire.tr: Then he has slain Duke Alphaïen;

occire.of: Le patriarche ocist devant les funz.
occire.tr: He slew the patriarch before the baptismal fonts.

occire.of: Ambure ocit, ki quel blasme ne quil lot.
occire.tr: He kills them both, let who will blame or praise it.

occire.of: Tel ad ocis dunt al coer me regrette.»
occire.tr: You have slain one for whom my heart grieves.»

occire.of: Sun cumpaignun Gerers ocit uncore
occire.tr: His companion Gerier he slays as well

occire.of: Tel as ocis que mult cher te quid vendre!»
occire.tr: You have killed one such that I think I will sell him to you very dear!»

occire.of: Ambure ocist seinz nul recoevrement,
occire.tr: He slew both without any recovery,

occire.of: Tuz sunt ocis cist Franceis chevalers,
occire.tr: All are slain, these French knights,

occire.of: De doel murra, se altre ne m'i ocit.
occire.tr: I shall die of grief, if nothing else slays me.

occire.of: Puis ad ocis Yvoeries e Ivon
occire.tr: then he has killed Yvoire and Yvon,

occire.of: A si grant tort m'ociz mes cumpaignuns!
occire.tr: So very wrongly you kill my companions!

octroyer.of: Li quens Rollant, ki ne l'otriet mie,
octroyer.tr: Count Roland, who did not agree to it at all,

octroyer.of: Se cest acorde ne vulez otrier,
octroyer.tr: If you will not grant this accord,

octroyer.of: Si ceste acorde ne volez otrier,
octroyer.tr: If you will not grant this accord,

octroyer.of: Respont Rollant: «E! Deus la nus otreit!
octroyer.tr: Roland answers: «Ah! God grant it to us!
«Lord companion, if you will grant it,

octroyer.of: Tutes voz anmes otreit il pareïs!
octroyer.tr: May he grant paradise to all your souls!

oser.of: Suz cel n'ad gent ki [l']osast (re)querre en champ.
oser.tr: Under heaven there is no man who would dare seek him out in the field.

oublier.tr: We must not forget Charles's war-cry here.»

oublier.of: Ferez, Franceis! Nul de vus ne s'ublit!
oublier.tr: Strike, Franks! Let none of you forget himself!

oublier.of: L'enseigne Carle n'i volt mie ublier:
oublier.tr: Charles's battle-cry he does not wish to forget:

ouïr.of: «Oëz, seignurs, quel pecchet nus encumbret:
ouïr.tr: «Hear, my lords, what calamity weighs upon us:

ouïr.of: N'orrat de nos paroles ne nuveles.
ouïr.tr: He will hear of us neither word nor news.

ouïr.of: Respunt Rollant: «Orgoill ói e folage.
ouïr.tr: Rollant answers: «I hear arrogance and folly.

ouïr.of: Quant l'ot Rollant, si cumençat a rire. AOI.
ouïr.tr: When Roland heard it, he began to laugh. AOI.

ouïr.of: Oït l'avez, sur vos le jugent Franc.»
ouïr.tr: You have heard it, the Franks judge it against you.»

ouïr.of: - «Seignurs» dist Guenes, «vos en orrez noveles!»
ouïr.tr: - «Lords,» said Ganelon, «you will hear news of it!»

ouïr.of: Pur les nuveles qu'il vuldreient oïr.
ouïr.tr: Because of the news they would wish to hear.

ouïr.of: Par lui orrez si avrez pais u nun.»
ouïr.tr: From him you will hear whether you will have peace or not.»

ouïr.of: Respunt Marsilie: «Or diet, nus l'orrum!» AOI.
ouïr.tr: Marsile replies: «Let him speak now, we shall hear him!» AOI.

ouïr.of: Vos le doüssez esculter e oïr.»
ouïr.tr: You ought to have listened to him and heard him out.»

ouïr.of: Quant l'oït Guenes, l'espee en ad branlie;
ouïr.tr: When Ganelon heard this, he brandished his sword;

ouïr.of: De Carlemagne vos voeill oïr parler.
ouïr.tr: I want to hear you speak of Charlemagne.

ouïr.of: Quan l'ot Marsilie, si l'ad baiset el col,
ouïr.tr: When Marsile heard this, he kissed him on the neck,

ouïr.of: Quant l'ot li reis, fierement le reguardet,
ouïr.tr: When the king heard this, fiercely he looks at him,

ouïr.of: Li quens Rollant quant il s'oït juger,
ouïr.tr: Count Roland, when he hears himself thus assigned,

ouïr.of: Quant ot Rollant, qu'il ert en la rereguarde,
ouïr.tr: When Roland heard that he would be in the rearguard,

ouïr.of: De .XV. lius en ot hom la rimur.
ouïr.tr: From fifteen leagues away one could hear their din.

ouïr.of: Asez orrez, laquele irat desure.
ouïr.tr: Soon you will hear which one will prevail.

ouïr.of: Granz est la noise, si l'oïrent Franceis.
ouïr.tr: Great is the din, and the French heard it.

ouïr.of: Si l'orrat Carles, si returnerat l'ost.»
ouïr.tr: Charles will hear it, and he will turn back the army.»

ouïr.of: Si l'orrat Carles, ferat l'ost returner,
ouïr.tr: Charles will hear it, will make the army turn back,

ouïr.of: Si l'orrat Carles, ki est as porz passant.
ouïr.tr: Charles will hear it, he who is passing through the mountain passes.

ouïr.of: Ki dunc oïst «Munjoie» demander,
ouïr.tr: Whoever then had heard them call «Munjoie»

ouïr.of: Quant l'ot Rollant, Deus! si grant doel en out!
ouïr.tr: When Roland heard it, God! such great grief he had!

ouïr.of: Ot le Oliver, sin ad mult grant irur;
ouïr.tr: Oliver hears him, and feels very great wrath at it;

ouïr.of: Ben l'entendit li arc[e]vesques Turpin.
ouïr.tr: Archbishop Turpin heard him well.

ouïr.of: Ne l'oï dire ne jo mie nel sai
ouïr.tr: I never heard it told, nor do I know at all

ouïr.of: Ben ad oït que Franceis se dementent;
ouïr.tr: He has clearly heard that the Franks are lamenting;

ouïr.of: Sun ceval brochet, ki oït del cuntence.
ouïr.tr: He spurs his horse, which heard the dispute.

ouïr.of: Si l'orrat Carles, ki est as porz passant.
ouïr.tr: So Charles, who is passing through the passes, will hear it.

ouïr.of: Jo cornerai, si l'orrat li reis Karles.»
ouïr.tr: I will sound the horn, and King Charles will hear it.»

ouïr.of: Li arceves[ques] les ót cuntrarier,
ouïr.tr: The archbishop heard them quarreling,

ouïr.of: Granz .XXX. liwes l'oïrent il respundre.
ouïr.tr: They heard it echo back a full thirty leagues.

ouïr.of: Karles l'oït e ses cumpaignes tutes.
ouïr.tr: Charles heard it, and all his companions.

ouïr.of: Karles l'entent, ki est as porz passant.
ouïr.tr: Charles hears it, who is passing through the gorges.

ouïr.of: Naimes li duc l'oïd, si l'escultent li Franc.
ouïr.tr: Naimes the duke heard it, and the Franks listen to it.

ouïr.of: Ce dist li reis: «Jo oi le corn Rollant!
ouïr.tr: The king says: «I hear Roland's horn!

ouïr.of: Karles l'oït e ses Franceis l'entendent,
ouïr.tr: Charles hears it and his Franks hear it,

ouïr.of: Asez oez que Rollant se dementet!»
ouïr.tr: You hear well enough that Roland laments!»

ouïr.of: Dist Oliver: «Or vos oi jo parler;
ouïr.tr: Oliver says: «Now I hear you speak;

ouïr.of: Jamais en tere n'orrez plus dolent hume!
ouïr.tr: Never on earth will you hear a more grieving man!

pardonner.of: Ferut vos ai, car le me pardunez!»
pardonner.tr: I have struck you, so forgive me for it!»

pardonner.of: Jol vos parduins ici e devant Deu.»
pardonner.tr: I forgive you for it here and before God.»

parler.of: Sa custume est qu'il parolet a leisír.
parler.tr: His custom is that he speaks at leisure.

parler.of: Dient Franceis: «Ben ad parlet li dux.» AOI.
parler.tr: The Franks say: «The duke has spoken well.» AOI.

parler.of: N'en parlez mais, se jo nel vos cumant!» AOI.
parler.tr: Speak of it no more, unless I command you to!» AOI.

parler.of: Par grant saveir parolet li uns a l'altre.
parler.tr: With great wisdom the one speaks to the other.

parler.of: Par grant saver cumencet a parler
parler.tr: With great wisdom he begins to speak,

parler.of: Apres parlat ses filz envers Marsilies,
parler.tr: After this his son spoke to Marsile,

parler.of: De Carlemagne vos voeill oïr parler.
parler.tr: I want to hear you speak of Charlemagne.

parler.of: Ço dist Marsilies: «Qu'en parlereient il plus?
parler.tr: Marsile said: «Why should they speak further about it?

parler.of: Par grant veisdie cumencet a parler,
parler.tr: With great cunning he begins to speak,

parler.of: Dunc ad parled a lei de chevaler:
parler.tr: Then spoke as befits a knight:
Angrily he spoke to his stepfather:

parler.of: Cil ad parlet a lei de bon vassal:
parler.tr: He has spoken in the manner of a good vassal:
Said Oliver: «I have no wish to speak.

parler.of: Ne leserat, ço dit, que n'i parolt:
parler.tr: He will not refrain, he says, from speaking there:

parler.of: Ne laisserat que n'i parolt, ço dit:
parler.tr: He will not refrain from speaking to him, he says this:

parler.of: N'i ad celoi (a celoi) a l'altre ne parolt:
parler.tr: There is not one of them but speaks to the other:

parler.of: Dist Oliver: «Or vos oi jo parler;
parler.tr: Oliver says: «Now I hear you speak;

passer.of: Vendrat li jurz, si passerat li termes,
passer.tr: The day will come, and so the term will pass,

passer.of: Ja einz ne verrat passer cest premer meis,
passer.tr: He will not see this first month pass

passer.of: Vers Engletere passat il la mer salse,
passer.tr: Toward England he crossed the salt sea,

passer.of: Men escient dous cenz anz ad passet.
passer.tr: To my knowledge he has passed two hundred years.

passer.of: Ja ne verrez cest premer meis passet
passer.tr: Never will you see this first month pass
Cross the passes in complete security:

passer.of: Le jur passerent Franceis a grant dulur;
passer.tr: That day the French passed through with great suffering;

passer.of: En Rencesvals irez as porz passant,
passer.tr: You will go to Roncevaux, passing through the gorges,

passer.of: Si l'orrat Carles, ki est as porz passant.
passer.tr: Charles will hear it, he who is passing through the mountain passes.

passer.of: As porz d'Espaigne en est passet Rollant
passer.tr: Through the passes of Spain Roland has gone by

passer.of: Empeint le bien, par mi le cors li passet,
passer.tr: Drives it home hard, runs it right through his body,

passer.of: Si l'orrat Carles, ki est as porz passant.
passer.tr: So Charles, who is passing through the passes, will hear it.

peindre.of: E cil escuz, ki ben sunt peinz a flurs,
peindre.tr: And those shields, which are finely painted with flowers,

peler.of: Icil li peilent la barbe e les gernuns;
peler.tr: These pluck out his beard and his mustaches;

penser.of: Baisset sun chef, si cumencet a penser. AOI.
penser.tr: he bows his head, and begins to think. AOI.

penser.of: Li quens Rollant nel se doüst penser,
penser.tr: Count Roland ought not to have thought of it,

perdre.of: Asez est melz qu'il i perdent le chefs,
perdre.tr: It is far better that they lose their heads,

perdre.of: Que nus perduns l'onur ne la deintet,
perdre.tr: Than that we lose our honor and our dignity,

perdre.of: Asez est mielz, qu'il i perdent les testes,
perdre.tr: It is far better that they lose their heads there,

perdre.of: Que nus perduns clere Espaigne, la bele,
perdre.tr: Than that we lose bright Spain, the fair,

perdre.of: A ben petit que il ne pert le sens;
perdre.tr: he very nearly loses his mind;

perdre.of: Par jugement iloec perdrez le chef.
perdre.tr: By judgment there you will lose your head.

perdre.of: Dunc perdreit Carles le destre braz del cors,
perdre.tr: Then Charles would lose the right arm of his body,

perdre.of: N'i perdrat Carles, li reis ki France tient,
perdre.tr: Charles, the king who holds France, shall lose nothing by it,

perdre.of: Ne n'i perdrat ne runcin ne sumer,
perdre.tr: Nor shall he lose pack-horse or sumpter,

perdre.of: Que l'emperere nis un des soens n'i perdet.»
perdre.tr: So that the emperor loses not one of his own there.»

perdre.of: Deus! se jol pert, ja n'en avrai escange!» AOI.
perdre.tr: God! if I lose him, I shall have no replacement for him!» AOI.

perdre.of: Li .XII. per tuit sunt jugez a perdre;
perdre.tr: The Twelve Peers are all condemned to perish;

perdre.of: Sin deit hom perdre e del quir e del peil.
perdre.tr: And a man should lose both hide and hair for it.

perdre.of: En dulce France en perdreie mun los.
perdre.tr: In sweet France I would lose my renown.

perdre.of: Que ja pur mei perdet sa valur France!
perdre.tr: That ever for my sake France should lose its worth!

perdre.of: Sin deit hom perdre del sanc e de la char.
perdre.tr: And a man must lose of his blood and of his flesh.

perdre.of: Enquoi perdrat France dulce sun los,
perdre.tr: This day sweet France shall lose its renown,

perdre.of: Oí n'en perdrat France dulce sun los.
perdre.tr: Today sweet France shall not lose its renown.

perdre.of: «Enquoi perdrat France dulce s'onur!»
perdre.tr: «This day shall sweet France lose her honor!»

perdre.of: Apres li dist: «Turnet estes a perdre!» AOI.
perdre.tr: Afterward he says to him: «You are doomed to perish!» AOI.

perdre.of: A .XV. cols l'ad fraite e [...] perdue,
perdre.tr: At fifteen blows he has broken and lost it,

perdre.of: Tant bon Franceis i perdent lor juvente!
perdre.tr: So many good Franks there lose their youth!

perdre.of: Puis en perdit e sa vie e ses membres;
perdre.tr: Afterward he lost for it both his life and his limbs;

perdre.of: Franceis i perdent lor meillors guarnemenz;
perdre.tr: The French lose there their best warriors;

perdre.of: Iço ne di que Karles n'i ait perdut;
perdre.tr: I do not say that Charles has lost nothing here;

perdre.of: L'oíe pert e la veüe tute;
perdre.tr: He loses his hearing and his sight entirely;

peser.of: Que l'abat [mort], qui qu'en peist u qui nun,
peser.tr: So that he strikes him down [dead], whoever may be grieved by it or not,

plaindre.of: Si grant doel ai ne puis muer nel pleigne.
plaindre.tr: Such great grief I have that I cannot keep from lamenting it.

plaindre.of: Jamais n'ert jor que Carles ne se pleignet.» AOI.
plaindre.tr: Never will there be a day that Charles does not lament it.» AOI.

plaindre.of: Pleindre poüms France dulce, la bele:
plaindre.tr: We may pity France the sweet, the fair:

plaire.tr: Ganelon answers this: «May it not please the Lord God!

plaire.of: Deus se lui plaist, a bien le vos mercie!» AOI.
plaire.tr: May God, if it please Him, reward you well for it!» AOI.

plaire.of: Ço respunt Guenes: «Issi seit cum vos plaist!»
plaire.tr: Ganelon answered: «So be it, as it pleases you!»
Roland replies: «May it not please the Lord God

plaire.of: - «Ne placet Deu,» ço li respunt Rollant,
plaire.tr: - «May it not please God,» Roland answers him,

plaire.of: Ne placet Damnedeu ne ses angles
plaire.tr: May it not please the Lord God nor his angels

pleurer.tr: There you would have seen so many knights weep,

pleurer.of: Ne poet muer que des oilz ne plurt.
pleurer.tr: He cannot help but weep from his eyes.

pleurer.of: Cel nen i ad ki de pitet ne plurt.
pleurer.tr: There is none there who does not weep for pity.

pleurer.of: Pitet l'en prent, ne poet muer n'en plurt. AOI.
pleurer.tr: Pity takes him, he cannot but weep. AOI.

pleurer.of: Carles li magnes ne poet muer n'en plurt.
pleurer.tr: Charlemagne cannot help but weep at it.

pleurer.of: Karles li magnes en pluret, si se demente.
pleurer.tr: Charles the Great weeps for it, and laments.

pleurer.of: Plurent des oilz de doel e de tendrur
pleurer.tr: They weep from their eyes out of grief and tenderness

pleurer.of: Si nus plurrunt de doel e de pitet,
pleurer.tr: And will weep for us with grief and pity,

pleurer.of: N'i ad celoi ki durement ne plurt,
pleurer.tr: There is none among them who does not weep bitterly,

pleurer.of: N'i ad celoi n'i plurt e se dement,
pleurer.tr: There is not one of them who does not weep and lament,

pleurer.of: E il les pluret cum chevaler gentill:
pleurer.tr: And he weeps for them as a noble knight:

pleurer.of: Rollant li ber le pluret, sil duluset;
pleurer.tr: Roland the brave weeps for him, laments him;

poindre.of: Puignent ad ait tuit li barun de France;
poindre.tr: The barons of France spur on eagerly;

porter.of: Branches d'olives en voz mains porterez,
porter.tr: Olive branches you shall carry in your hands,

porter.of: Branches d'olive en voz mains portereiz,
porter.tr: Olive branches you shall carry in your hands,

porter.of: Enz en lur mains portent branches d'olive.
porter.tr: In their hands they bear branches of olive.

porter.of: Cha(n)cuns portout une branche d'olive;
porter.tr: Each one carried an olive branch;

porter.of: Ne vos ne il n'i porterez les piez.
porter.tr: Neither you nor he shall set foot there.

porter.of: Qu'a Marsiliun me portast mun message.»
porter.tr: Who would carry my message to Marsile.»

porter.of: Tant vus avrai en curt a rei portee!
porter.tr: So long shall I have borne you at the king's court!

porter.of: Marsilies fait porter un livre avant:
porter.tr: Marsile has a book brought forward:

porter.of: Mult se fait fiers de ses armes porter;
porter.tr: He bears himself fiercely in carrying his arms;

porter.of: Jamais en tere ne portera curone.»
porter.tr: Never on earth will he wear a crown again.»

porter.of: Se trois Rollant, n'en porterat la teste,
porter.tr: If I find Roland, he will not carry away his head,

porter.of: Ne Oliver n'en porterat la vie;
porter.tr: Nor will Oliver carry off his life from it;

porter.of: Greignor fais portet par giu, quant il s'enveiset,
porter.tr: For sport, when he amuses himself, he carries a greater load

porter.of: Portet ses armes, mult li sunt avenanz,
porter.tr: He bears his arms, which become him well,

porter.of: L'anme de lui en portet Sathanas. AOI.
porter.tr: Satan carries off his soul. AOI.

porter.of: En tanz lius les avum nos portees!
porter.tr: In so many places have we borne them!

porter.of: Sun dragun portet a qui sa gent s'alient.
porter.tr: He carries his dragon-standard, around which his people rally.

porter.of: L'anme de lui en portent aversers.
porter.tr: Demons carry off his soul.

porter.of: L'escut vermeill li freint, de col li portet;
porter.tr: He shatters his vermilion shield, tears it from his neck;

porter.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Por quei me portez ire?»
porter.tr: Roland said this: «Why do you bear anger toward me?»

porter.of: Ki armes portet e en bon cheval set;
porter.tr: Who bears arms and sits upon a good horse;

pouvoir.of: Nes poet guarder que mals ne l'i ateignet. AOI.
pouvoir.tr: he cannot keep harm from reaching him there. AOI.

pouvoir.of: Ben en purrat luer ses soldeiers.
pouvoir.tr: Well will he be able to pay his soldiers with it.

pouvoir.of: Dient paien: «Issi poet il ben estre!»
pouvoir.tr: The pagans say: «It may well be so!»

pouvoir.of: Par voz saveirs sem puez acorder,
pouvoir.tr: By your wisdom, if you can bring me to an accord,

pouvoir.of: Nes poet guarder que alques ne l'engignent. AOI.
pouvoir.tr: He cannot guard against their deceiving him in some way. AOI.

pouvoir.of: Dunt bien purrez voz soldeiers luer.
pouvoir.tr: that you will well be able to pay your soldiers.

pouvoir.of: En quel mesure en purrai estre fiz?»
pouvoir.tr: In what measure can I be assured of it?»

pouvoir.of: Charles respunt: «Uncore purrat guarir.» AOI.
pouvoir.tr: Charles answers: «He may yet be saved.» AOI.

pouvoir.of: - «Seignurs baruns, qui i purruns enveier
pouvoir.tr: - «Lord barons, whom can we send

pouvoir.of: Respunt Rollant: «Jo i puis aler mult ben!»
pouvoir.tr: Roland answers: «I can go there very well!»

pouvoir.of: Se li reis voelt, jo i puis aler ben.»
pouvoir.tr: If the king wills it, I can well go there.»

pouvoir.of: Dient Franceis: «Car il le poet ben faire;
pouvoir.tr: The Franks say: «For he can do it well;

pouvoir.of: Hom ki la vait, repairer ne s'en poet.
pouvoir.tr: A man who goes there cannot return from it.

pouvoir.of: - «Jo i puis aler, mais n'i avrai guarant: AOI.
pouvoir.tr: - «I can go there, but I shall have no protection: AOI.

pouvoir.of: Dient Franceis: «Deus! que purrat ço estre?
pouvoir.tr: The Franks say: «God! what can this be?

pouvoir.of: De ses meillors que il pout recuvrer:
pouvoir.tr: With the best of it that he could obtain:

pouvoir.of: Ne mul ne mule que puissez chevalcher;
pouvoir.tr: Nor mule nor she-mule that you may ride;

pouvoir.of: Sa grant valor, kil purreit acunter?
pouvoir.tr: His great valor, who could recount it?

pouvoir.of: Dist li paiens: «Mult me puis merveiller
pouvoir.tr: The pagan said: «Greatly may I marvel

pouvoir.of: Quarte cenz milie chevalers puis aveir.
pouvoir.tr: Four hundred thousand knights I can have.

pouvoir.of: Puis m'en cumbatre a Carlles e a Franceis?»
pouvoir.tr: Can I then fight against Charles and the Franks?»

pouvoir.of: «Cum faitement purrai Rollant ocire?»
pouvoir.tr: «How might I be able to kill Roland?»

pouvoir.of: - «Chi purreit faire, que Rollant i fust mort,
pouvoir.tr: - «Whoever could bring it about that Roland be killed there,

pouvoir.of: E, se il poet, murrat i veirement.
pouvoir.tr: And, if he can, he will truly die there.

pouvoir.of: Qu'en rereguarde trover le poüsum.»
pouvoir.tr: That we may find him in the rearguard.»

pouvoir.of: Par quel mesure le poüssum hunir.»
pouvoir.tr: As to by what means we might dishonor him.»

pouvoir.of: Sel pois trover a port ne a passage,
pouvoir.tr: If I can find him at pass or defile,

pouvoir.of: Ne poet muer que des oilz ne plurt.
pouvoir.tr: He cannot help but weep from his eyes.

pouvoir.of: Pitet l'en prent, ne poet muer n'en plurt. AOI.
pouvoir.tr: Pity takes him, he cannot but weep. AOI.

pouvoir.of: Si grant doel ai ne puis muer nel pleigne.
pouvoir.tr: Such great grief I have that I cannot keep from lamenting it.

pouvoir.of: Carles li magnes ne poet muer n'en plurt.
pouvoir.tr: Charlemagne cannot help but weep at it.

pouvoir.of: Quant ele le veit, ne poet muer ne riet;
pouvoir.tr: When she sees him, she cannot help but smile;

pouvoir.of: Gesir porrum el burc de seint Denise.»
pouvoir.tr: We shall be able to lie in the town of Saint Denis.»

pouvoir.of: Soleill n'i luist, ne blet n'i poet pas creistre,
pouvoir.tr: The sun does not shine there, nor can grain grow there,

pouvoir.of: De Sarrazins purum bataille aveir.»
pouvoir.tr: We may have battle from the Saracens.»

pouvoir.of: Sul les escheles ne poet il acunter;
pouvoir.tr: He cannot even count the battalions;

pouvoir.of: Cum il einz pout, del pui est avalet,
pouvoir.tr: As fast as he could, he came down from the hill,

pouvoir.of: Veeir poez, dolente est la rereguarde;
pouvoir.tr: You can see, the rearguard is to be pitied;

pouvoir.of: Se jo i moert, dire poet ki l'avrat
pouvoir.tr: If I die here, whoever has it may say

pouvoir.of: Kar chevalchez a quanque vos puez!
pouvoir.tr: So ride on with all the speed you can!

pouvoir.of: De vasselage li poüst remembrer.
pouvoir.tr: would have been reminded of true valor.

pouvoir.of: Vait le ferir li quens quanque il pout.
pouvoir.tr: The count goes to strike him with all his might.

pouvoir.of: Grant demi pied mesurer i pout hom.
pouvoir.tr: A good half-foot one could measure there.

pouvoir.of: «Ceste bataille ben la puum tenir,
pouvoir.tr: «This battle we can well sustain,

pouvoir.of: - «Ne la poi traire,» Oliver li respunt,
pouvoir.tr: - «I could not draw it,» Oliver answers him,

pouvoir.of: De cent millers n'en poent guarir dous.
pouvoir.tr: Of a hundred thousand not two of them can escape.

pouvoir.of: La traïsun ne poet estre celee;
pouvoir.tr: The treason cannot be concealed;

pouvoir.of: Nel poet guarir sun escut ne sa bronie:
pouvoir.tr: Neither his shield nor his hauberk can protect him:

pouvoir.of: Poez saveir que mult grant doel en out.
pouvoir.tr: You may be sure that he felt very great grief at it.

pouvoir.of: Vait le ferir li bers, quanque il pout,
pouvoir.tr: The baron goes to strike him with all his strength,

pouvoir.of: Respont Rollant: «Ne pois amer les voz;
pouvoir.tr: Roland replies: «I cannot love your people;

pouvoir.of: Beste nen est ki poisset curre a lui.
pouvoir.tr: There is no beast that can run as fast as it.

pouvoir.of: Ne poet muer qu'il ne s'en espoent,
pouvoir.tr: He cannot help but be terrified by it,

pouvoir.of: Li Sarrazin nel poent susfrir tant:
pouvoir.tr: The Saracens cannot endure it so long:

pouvoir.of: Cels qu'il unt mort, ben les poet hom preiser;
pouvoir.tr: those whom they have slain, one can well count them;

pouvoir.of: Pleindre poüms France dulce, la bele:
pouvoir.tr: We may pity France the sweet, the fair:

pouvoir.of: Oliver, frere, cumment le purrum nus faire?
pouvoir.tr: Oliver, brother, how shall we manage it?

pouvoir.of: Se puis veeir ma gente sorur Alde,
pouvoir.tr: If I can see my fair sister Alde again,

pouvoir.of: Venget li reis, si nus purrat venger;
pouvoir.tr: Let the king come, for he will be able to avenge us;

pouvoir.of: Demurent trop, n'i poedent estre a tens. AOI.
pouvoir.tr: They tarry too long, they cannot be there in time. AOI.

pouvoir.of: Jo ne vos pois tenser ne guarantir.
pouvoir.tr: I cannot protect you nor save you.

pouvoir.of: De bon vassal li poüst remembrer.
pouvoir.tr: Might well remember a good vassal.

pouvoir.of: Ne loinz ne pres (es) ne poet vedeir si cler
pouvoir.tr: Neither far nor near can he see clearly enough

pouvoir.of: Que rec[on]oistre poisset nuls hom mortel.
pouvoir.tr: That any mortal man could be recognized.

pouvoir.of: Quel part qu'il alt, ne poet mie chaïr.
pouvoir.tr: Wherever he may go, he cannot fall.

prendre.of: Cordres ad prise e les murs peceiez,
prendre.tr: He has taken Cordres and shattered its walls,

prendre.of: Des ore cumencet le cunseill que mal prist. AOI.
prendre.tr: From now begins the counsel that came to ill. AOI.

prendre.of: Pris ai Valterne e la tere de Pine
prendre.tr: I have taken Valterne and the land of Pine

prendre.of: A vos Franceis un cunseill en presistes:
prendre.tr: With your Franks you took a counsel on it:

prendre.of: Les chef en prist es puis desuz Haltilie.
prendre.tr: He took their heads in the hills below Haltilie.

prendre.of: Quant le dut prendre, si li caït a tere.
prendre.tr: When he had to take it, it fell from him to the ground.

prendre.of: De guarnemenz se prent a cunreer,
prendre.tr: He sets about equipping himself with gear,

prendre.of: Pris e liez serez par poested;
prendre.tr: You will be seized and bound by force,

prendre.of: Par poestet serez pris e liez;
prendre.tr: By force you will be taken and bound;

prendre.of: Dunt pris les chefs as puis de Haltoíe;
prendre.tr: Whose heads I took at the heights of Haltoie;

prendre.of: E Guenes (l')ad pris par la main destre ad deiz,
prendre.tr: And Ganelon took him by the right hand by the fingers,

prendre.of: Tanz [colps] ad pris sur sun escut bucler,
prendre.tr: So many blows has he taken upon his buckler-shield,

prendre.of: Tanz colz ad pris de lances e d'espiet,
prendre.tr: so many blows he has taken from lances and spears,

prendre.of: Tanz colps ad pris de bons espiez trenchanz,
prendre.tr: So many blows has he taken from good sharp spears,

prendre.of: Il les ad prises, en sa hoese les butet, AOI.
prendre.tr: He took them, into his boot he stuffed them, AOI.

prendre.of: Li quens Rollant il l'ad e prise e fraite;
prendre.tr: Count Roland had taken and shattered it;

prendre.of: Les castels pris, (. . .) les citez violees.
prendre.tr: taken the castles, (. . .) violated the cities.

prendre.of: «Pernez mil Francs de France, nostre tere,
prendre.tr: «Take a thousand Franks from France, our land,

prendre.of: Pitet l'en prent, ne poet muer n'en plurt. AOI.
prendre.tr: Pity takes him, he cannot but weep. AOI.

prendre.of: Pris en ad or e aveir e deners.
prendre.tr: He has taken for it gold and wealth and money.

prendre.of: El plait ad Ais en fut juget a prendre,
prendre.tr: At the trial at Aix he was condemned to be seized,

prendre.of: Mult grant venjance en prendrat l'emperere.
prendre.tr: The emperor will take very great vengeance for it.

prendre.of: Pramis nus est, fin prendrum a itant,
prendre.tr: It is promised to us, we shall come to our end thereby,

prendre.of: Fiance prist de Guenelun le cunte,
prendre.tr: He took an oath from Ganelon the count,

prendre.of: Jerusalem prist ja par traïsun,
prendre.tr: He took Jerusalem once by treason,

prendre.of: Ceste bataille oüsum faite u prise;
prendre.tr: This battle we would have fought and won;

prendre.of: U pris ú mort i fust li reis Marsilie.
prendre.tr: Captured or killed would have been King Marsile.

prendre.of: Vint tresqu'a els, sis prist a castier:
prendre.tr: Came right up to them, and began to rebuke them:

prendre.of: Ja prist il Noples seinz le vostre comant;
prendre.tr: He once took Noples without your command;

prendre.of: Li reis fait prendre le cunte Guenelun,
prendre.tr: The king has Count Ganelon seized,

prendre.of: Puis prent la teste de Jurfaleu le Blund,
prendre.tr: Then he takes the head of Jurfaleu the Blond,

prendre.of: E dit apres: «Un col avez pris fort!
prendre.tr: And said after: «You have taken a hard blow!

prendre.of: Mult dulcement a regreter le prist:
prendre.tr: Very tenderly he began to lament him:

prier.of: Tuit li preierent li meillor Sarrazin,
prier.tr: All the noblest Saracens begged him,

prier.of: N'i ad paien nel prit e nel aort.
prier.tr: There is no pagan who does not pray to him and adore him.

prier.of: Clamez vos culpes, si preiez Deu mercit!
prier.tr: Confess your sins, and pray to God for mercy!

prier.of: Pur deu vos pri, ben seiez purpensez
prier.tr: For God's sake I pray you, be well resolved

prier.of: Pur Deu vos pri que ne seiez fuiant,
prier.tr: For God's sake I pray you not to flee,

prier.of: Pur Deu vos pri, ne vos cuntraliez!
prier.tr: For God's sake I pray you, do not quarrel!

prier.of: E p[ri]ent Deu qu'il guarisset Rollant
prier.tr: And they pray to God that He keep Roland safe

prier.of: Si prierat tuz jurz por noz peccez.»
prier.tr: Where he would pray every day for our sins.»

prier.of: Si priet Deu que pareïs li dunget
prier.tr: And he prays to God to grant him paradise

priser.of: Tant nel vos sai ne preiser ne loer
priser.tr: I cannot so praise him to you nor extol him

priser.of: «Car mult vos priset mi sire e tuit si hume.
priser.tr: «For my lord esteems you greatly, and all his men.

priser.of: Encuntre mei fait asez a preiser.
priser.tr: Beside me he does very well, worthy of praise.

priser.of: Cels qu'il unt mort, ben les poet hom preiser;
priser.tr: those whom they have slain, one can well count them;

promettre.of: Pramis nus est, fin prendrum a itant,
promettre.tr: It is promised to us, we shall come to our end thereby,

présenter.of: «De trestuz reis vos present les curunes.»
présenter.tr: «Of all kings I present to you the crowns.»

présenter.of: Le grant aveir en presentez al rei Carles,
présenter.tr: Present its great wealth to King Charles,

pâmer.of: Moerent paien e alquanz en i pasment.
pâmer.tr: The pagans die and several of them faint there.

pâmer.of: A icest mot sur sun cheval se pasmet. AOI.
pâmer.tr: At this word, upon his horse he swoons. AOI.

pâmer.of: As vus Rollant sur sun cheval pasmet,
pâmer.tr: There is Roland, fainting upon his horse,

pâmer.of: A icest mot se pasmet li marchis
pâmer.tr: At this word the marquis swoons

quérir.of: Que il querreient, que Rollant fust ocis.
quérir.tr: That they would seek a way for Roland to be killed.

quérir.of: Cist paien vont grant martirie querant.
quérir.tr: These pagans go seeking great martyrdom.

quérir.of: Trenchet l'eschine, hunc n'i out quis [joi]nture,
quérir.tr: He cuts through the spine, never seeking a joint there,

quérir.of: Dist Oliver: «Jo nel sai cument quere.
quérir.tr: Said Oliver: «I know not how to seek it.

quérir.of: Suz cel n'ad gent ki [l']osast (re)querre en champ.
quérir.tr: Under heaven there is no man who would dare seek him out in the field.

racheter.tr: And all of them answer back the call of the oliphant.

raconter.of: Sa grant valor, kil purreit acunter?
raconter.tr: His great valor, who could recount it?

raconter.of: Sul les escheles ne poet il acunter;
raconter.tr: He cannot even count the battalions;

raconter.of: Vint as Franceis, tut lur ad acuntet.
raconter.tr: Came to the French, told them everything.

rallier.of: Sunet sun gresle pur les soens ralier.
rallier.tr: He sounds his bugle to rally his men.

rappeler.of: Ki ques rapelt, ja n'en returnerunt. AOI.
rappeler.tr: whoever may recall them, they will never return. AOI.

rayer.of: Li sancs tuz clers par mi le cors li raiet:
rayer.tr: The bright blood streams down across his body:

recevoir.of: Si recevrez la lei de chrestiens,
recevoir.tr: Thus you will receive the law of the Christians,
And I will receive the Christian faith,

recevoir.of: Si recevrat la nostre lei plus salve;
recevoir.tr: And there will receive our most saving faith;

recevoir.of: Puis recevrat la lei que nus tenum,
recevoir.tr: Then will receive the faith that we hold,

recevoir.of: Si recevez le bastun e lu guant.
recevoir.tr: And receive the staff and the glove.

recevoir.of: Getet le a tere, sil receit Blancandrin;
recevoir.tr: He throws it to the ground, and Blancandrin catches it;

recevoir.of: Que recevez la lei de chrestiens;
recevoir.tr: That you receive the faith of Christians;

recevoir.of: Si recevrat la lei que vos tenez,
recevoir.tr: And he will receive the faith that you hold,

recevoir.of: De sa main destre, quant reçut le bastun.»
recevoir.tr: from his right hand, when he received the staff.»

recevoir.of: Li reis li dunet, e Rollant l'a reçut.
recevoir.tr: The king gives it to him, and Roland received it.

recevoir.of: De colps ferir, de receivre e (de) duner!
recevoir.tr: to strike blows, to receive and (to) give them!

recevoir.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Vos receif jo, frere!
Cil le receit, si met .C. cumpaignons
recevoir.tr: The cook receives him, and sets a hundred companions
Roland said: «Here we shall receive our martyrdom,

recommencer.of: A icest mot l'unt Francs recumencet.
recommencer.tr: At this word the Franks renewed the fight.

recommencer.of: A icest mot l'unt Francs recumencet.
recommencer.tr: At this word the Franks have begun again.

recommencer.of: Ferez Franceis, car jol vos recumenz!»
recommencer.tr: Strike, Franks, for I begin it again for you!»

reconnaître.of: Enceis nel vit, sil recunut veirement
reconnaître.tr: Never before had he seen him, yet he recognized him truly

reconnaître.of: Que rec[on]oistre poisset nuls hom mortel.
reconnaître.tr: That any mortal man could be recognized.

recouvrer.of: De ses meillors que il pout recuvrer:
recouvrer.tr: With the best of it that he could obtain:

redresser.of: Quant se redrecet, mult par out fier lu vis;
redresser.tr: When he straightens up, his face looked most proud;

regarder.of: Par mi cel host suvent e menu reguarded:.
regarder.tr: Throughout that host he looks about frequently and closely.

regarder.of: Quant l'ot li reis, fierement le reguardet,
regarder.tr: When the king heard this, fiercely he looks at him,
Toward the Saracens he looks fiercely

regarder.of: Rollant reguardet es munz e es lariz;
regarder.tr: Roland looks over the mountains and the slopes;
Roland looks at Oliver in the face:

regarder.of: A icel colp l'ad Rollant reguardet,
regarder.tr: At that blow Roland has looked at him,

regretter.tr: Often they lament Oliver and Roland,

regretter.of: Tel ad ocis dunt al coer me regrette.»
regretter.tr: You have slain one for whom my heart grieves.»

regretter.of: Mult dulcement a regreter le prist:
regretter.tr: Very tenderly he began to lament him:

reluire.of: Cuntre le soleil reluisent cil adub,
reluire.tr: Against the sun those pieces of armor gleam,

remembrer.of: Que me remembre de la dolur e (de) l'ire,
remembrer.tr: That I remember the grief and the wrath,

remembrer.of: Dunc le remembret des fius e des honurs,
remembrer.tr: Then he remembers his fiefs and his honors,
would have been reminded of true valor.

remembrer.of: De bon vassal li poüst remembrer.
remembrer.tr: Might well remember a good vassal.

remuer.of: N'avez baron ki jamais la remut.
remuer.tr: You have no baron who would ever change it.

rendre.of: Une bataille lur i rendent cil primes;
rendre.tr: Let these give them a first battle there;

rendre.of: Franc e paien merveilus colps i rendent.
rendre.tr: Frank and pagan exchange marvelous blows.

rendre.of: Malvais servis[e] le jur li rendit Guenes,
rendre.tr: An evil service Ganelon rendered him that day,

rendre.of: Li arcevesques plus de mil colps i rent,
rendre.tr: The archbishop deals more than a thousand blows,

rendre.of: Tant le guardent quel rendent a Charlun.
rendre.tr: They guard him until they hand him back to Charles.

repairer.of: En France, ad Ais, s'en deit ben repairer.
repairer.tr: To France, to Aix, he ought well to return.

repairer.of: En France, ad Ais, devez bien repairer;
repairer.tr: to France, to Aix, you ought rightly to return;

repairer.of: Se Deus ço dunet que jo de la repaire,
repairer.tr: If God grants that I return from there,

repairer.of: Hom ki la vait, repairer ne s'en poet.
repairer.tr: A man who goes there cannot return from it.

repairer.of: En dulce France s'en repairerat li reis;
repairer.tr: The king will return to sweet France;

repairer.of: Li emperere s'en repairet en France;
Li quens Rollant el champ est repairet:
repairer.tr: Count Roland has returned to the field:

reprocher.tr: To my knowledge they will not reproach me

requérir.of: Que nus requert ça en la nostre marche?»
requérir.tr: Why does he make demands on us here in our march?»

requérir.of: Vunt par le camp, si requerent les lor,
requérir.tr: They go through the field, and they seek their own,

ressembler.of: Par tels paroles vus resemblez enfant.
ressembler.tr: By such words you seem like a child.

retenir.of: Retenez les, ço est vostre salvement.»
retenir.tr: Keep them, this is your safeguard.»

retenir.of: XX. milie Francs retendrai ben vaillanz.
retenir.tr: Twenty thousand Franks I will keep, right valiant ones.

retenir.of: Seignors baruns, el camp vos retenez!
retenir.tr: Lord barons, hold your ground on the field!
- «Munjoie!» he cries out, to hold the field.

retentir.of: N'i ad celoi ki mot sunt ne mot tint,
retentir.tr: There is none among them who speaks a word nor utters a word,

retourner.of: Si l'orrat Carles, si returnerat l'ost.»
retourner.tr: Charles will hear it, and he will turn back the army.»

retourner.of: Si l'orrat Carles, ferat l'ost returner,
retourner.tr: Charles will hear it, will make the army turn back,

retourner.of: Je vos plevis, ja returnerunt Franc.»
retourner.tr: I guarantee you, the Franks will return at once.»

retourner.of: Jo vos plevis ja returnerunt Franc.»
retourner.tr: I pledge to you the Franks will return at once.»

retourner.of: Ki ques rapelt, ja n'en returnerunt. AOI.
retourner.tr: whoever may recall them, they will never return. AOI.

revoir.of: Ne reverrunt lor meres ne lor femmes,
revoir.tr: They will not see again their mothers nor their wives,

revoir.of: Ne reverrunt lor peres ne lor parenz
revoir.tr: They will not see again their fathers nor their kinsmen,

rire.of: Quant l'ot Rollant, si cumençat a rire. AOI.
rire.tr: When Roland heard it, he began to laugh. AOI.

rire.of: Quant ço veit Guenes que ore s'en rit Rollant,
rire.tr: When Ganelon sees that Roland now laughs about it,

rire.of: Cler en riant l'ad dit a Guenelun:
rire.tr: Brightly, laughing, he said to Ganelon:

rire.of: Cler en riant a Guenelun l'ad dit:
rire.tr: Brightly, laughing, he said to Ganelon:

rire.of: Dist a sun uncle belement en riant:
rire.tr: He said to his uncle, smiling pleasantly:

rire.of: Quant ele le veit, ne poet muer ne riet;
rire.tr: When she sees him, she cannot help but smile;

rire.of: Cors ad mult gent, le vis cler e riant.
rire.tr: His body is most noble, his face bright and smiling.

rire.of: Unches nuls hom nel vit juer ne rire.
rire.tr: Never did any man see him play or laugh.

rompre.of: L'osberc li rumpt entresque a la charn,
rompre.tr: He breaks the hauberk right through to the flesh,

rompre.of: De sun osberc li rumpit la ventaille,
rompre.tr: Tears open the ventail of his hauberk,

rompre.of: De sun osberc li ad rumput les pans,
rompre.tr: He has burst open the skirts of his hauberk,

rompre.of: Tant gunfanun rumpu e tant enseigne!
rompre.tr: So many a gonfalon torn and so many a standard!

rompre.of: De sun osberc li ad les pans rumput,
rompre.tr: From his hauberk he has torn away the skirts,

rompre.of: De sun cervel le temple en est rumpant.
rompre.tr: The temple of his brain is bursting.

rompre.of: De sun cervel rumput en est li temples.
rompre.tr: The temple of his brain is burst.

réclamer.tr: serves Mahomet and calls upon Apollin;

répondre.of: N'i ad paien ki un sul mot respundet,
répondre.tr: There is no pagan who answers a single word,

répondre.of: Charles respunt: «Uncore purrat guarir.» AOI.
répondre.tr: Charles answers: «He may yet be saved.» AOI.

répondre.of: Ne ben ne mal ne respunt sun nevuld.
répondre.tr: Neither good nor ill does he answer his nephew.

répondre.of: Guenes li quens ço vus ad respondud,
répondre.tr: Ganelon the count has answered you thus,

répondre.of: Respunt dux Neimes: «Jo irai par vostre dun!
répondre.tr: Duke Naimes replies: «I will go, by your leave!

répondre.of: Respunt li reis: «Vos estes saives hom;
répondre.tr: The king replies: «You are a wise man;

répondre.of: Respunt Rollant: «Jo i puis aler mult ben!»
répondre.tr: Roland answers: «I can go there very well!»

répondre.of: Respunt li reis: «Ambdui vos en taisez!
répondre.tr: The king answers: «Both of you, be silent about it!

répondre.of: Li empereres respunt par maltalant:
répondre.tr: The emperor answers with displeasure:

répondre.of: Respunt Rollant: «Orgoill ói e folage.
répondre.tr: Rollant answers: «I hear arrogance and folly.

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Pur mei n'iras tu mie!
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «You will not go at all on my behalf!

répondre.of: Carles respunt: «trop avez tendre coer.
répondre.tr: Charles answers: «you have too tender a heart.

répondre.of: Ço respunt Guenes: «Ne placet Damnedeu!
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers this: «May it not please the Lord God!

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Itels est sis curages,
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «Such is his temper,

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Jo ne sai veirs nul hume,
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «I know of no man truly,

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Par la franceise gent.
répondre.tr: Ganelon replies: «With the French people.

répondre.of: Respunt Marsilie: «Or diet, nus l'orrum!» AOI.
répondre.tr: Marsile replies: «Let him speak now, we shall hear him!» AOI.

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Jo nel desotrei mie.
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «I do not refuse it at all.

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Carles n'est mie tels.
répondre.tr: Ganelon replies: «Charles is not at all such a man.

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Ne vus a ceste feiz!
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «Not this time!

répondre.of: Guenes respont: «Ço vos sai jo ben dire.
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «That I can well tell you.

répondre.of: Ço respunt Guenes: «Issi seit cum vos plaist!»
répondre.tr: Ganelon answered: «So be it, as it pleases you!»

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Ben seit vostre comant!» AOI.
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «May your command be a good one!» AOI.

répondre.of: - «Ben serat fait,» li quens Guenes respunt.
répondre.tr: - «It will be well done,» answers Count Ganelon.

répondre.of: - «Ben serat fait,» Guenes respundit.
répondre.tr: - «It will be well done,» Ganelon answered.

répondre.of: E cil respunt: «Oïl, sire, asez bien:
répondre.tr: And he replies: «Yes, lord, very amply:

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Mei est vis que trop targe!»
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «It seems to me that I tarry too long!»

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Rollant cist miens fillastre:
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «Roland, this stepson of mine:

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Oger de Denemarche:
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «Ogier of Denmark:

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Veir dites, jol sai bien.» AOI.
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «You speak truly, I know it well.» AOI.

répondre.of: Respunt gualter: «Pur vos le dei ben faire.»
répondre.tr: Gautier replies: «For you I must do it well.»

répondre.of: Carles respunt: «Tort fait kil me demandet!
répondre.tr: Charles replies: «He who asks me does wrong!

répondre.of: Tut premerein l'en respunt Falsaron,
répondre.tr: Falsaron answers him first of all,

répondre.of: E cil respundent: «(Sire,) a vostre comandement!
répondre.tr: And they answer: «(Sire,) at your command!

répondre.of: Respont Rollant: «E! Deus la nus otreit!
répondre.tr: Roland answers: «Ah! God grant it to us!

répondre.of: - «Tais Oliver,» li quens Rollant respunt,
répondre.tr: - «Be silent, Oliver,» Count Roland replies,

répondre.of: Respunt Rollant: «Jo fereie que fols!
répondre.tr: Roland answers: «I would act like a fool!

répondre.of: Respont Rollant: «Ne placet Damnedeu
répondre.tr: Roland replies: «May it not please the Lord God

répondre.of: - «Ne placet Deu,» ço li respunt Rollant,
répondre.tr: - «May it not please God,» Roland answers him,

répondre.of: Respunt Rollant: «Mis talenz en est graigne.
répondre.tr: Roland answers: «My desire is the greater for it.

répondre.of: Respunt Rollant: «Ne dites tel ultrage!
répondre.tr: Roland answers: «Do not speak such outrage!

répondre.of: - «Ne la poi traire,» Oliver li respunt,
répondre.tr: - «I could not draw it,» Oliver answers him,

répondre.of: Respunt Rollant: «Vencut est le culvert.
répondre.tr: Roland answers: «The villain is vanquished.

répondre.of: Respont li quens: «Deus le me doinst venger!»
répondre.tr: The count replies: «May God grant me to avenge him!»

répondre.of: Respont Rollant: «Ne pois amer les voz;
répondre.tr: Roland replies: «I cannot love your people;

répondre.of: Respunt li quens: «Kar li aluns aider!»
répondre.tr: The count replies: «Then let us go help him!»

répondre.of: Respont li quens: «Colps i ai fait mult genz!» AOI.
répondre.tr: The count replied: «I have struck blows most fine!» AOI.

répondre.of: (E cil) E il respont: «Cumpainz, vos le feïstes,
répondre.tr: And he answers: «Companion, you did it,

répondre.of: Respunt Rollant: «Sire, mult dites bien.» AOI.
répondre.tr: Roland answers: «Lord, you speak very well.» AOI.

répondre.of: Granz .XXX. liwes l'oïrent il respundre.
répondre.tr: They heard it echo back a full thirty leagues.

répondre.of: E Guenelun li respundit encuntre:
répondre.tr: And Ganelon answered him in return:

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «De bataille est il nient!
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «There is no battle at all!

répondre.of: Respont dux Neimes: «Baron i fait la p[e]ine!
répondre.tr: Duke Naimes answers: «A baron takes the trouble of it!

répondre.of: Respunt Rollant: «Ferez, nes esparignez!»
répondre.tr: Roland answers: «Strike, do not spare them!»

répondre.of: Rollant respunt: «Jo n'ai nient de mal.
répondre.tr: Roland answers: «I have no hurt at all.

saigner.of: Tant ad seinet (ki) li oil li sunt trublet;
saigner.tr: He has bled so much that his eyes are clouded;

saillir.of: Par mi la buche en salt fors li cler sancs.
saillir.tr: Out through his mouth the bright blood leaps forth.

saisir.of: Guenes li quens l'ad sur lui saisie;
saisir.tr: Ganelon the count has seized it from him;

saisir.of: Jusqu'a un an avrum France saisie;
saisir.tr: Within a year we shall have seized France;

saluer.of: Sil saluerent par amur e par bien.
saluer.tr: And greeted him with love and good will.

saluer.of: De meie part ma muiller saluez,
saluer.tr: On my behalf greet my wife,

savoir.of: Par voz saveirs sem puez acorder,
savoir.tr: By your wisdom, if you can bring me to an accord,

savoir.of: Mais jo ne sai quels en est sis curages.»
savoir.tr: But I do not know what his intention is.»

savoir.of: Saveir i ad, mais qu'il seit entendud.
savoir.tr: There is wisdom in it, provided it be heeded.

savoir.of: Ço set hom ben que jo sui tis parastres;
savoir.tr: One knows well that I am your stepfather;

savoir.of: Ço set hom ben, n'ai cure de manace;
savoir.tr: One knows well, I have no care for a threat;

savoir.of: «En Sarraguce sai ben, [qu']aler m'estoet.
savoir.tr: «I know well that I must go to Saragossa.

savoir.of: E Baldewin, mun filz que vos savez,
savoir.tr: And Baldwin, my son whom you know,

savoir.of: Guenes respunt: «Jo ne sai veirs nul hume,
savoir.tr: Ganelon answers: «I know of no man truly,

savoir.of: Par grant saver cumencet a parler
savoir.tr: With great wisdom he begins to speak,

savoir.of: Cume celui ki ben faire le set,
savoir.tr: Like one who well knows how to do it,

savoir.of: Ço dist Marsilies: «Guenes par veir sacez,
savoir.tr: Marsile said this: «Ganelon, know in truth,

savoir.of: N'est hom kil veit e conuistre le set
savoir.tr: There is no man who sees him and comes to know him

savoir.of: Tant nel vos sai ne preiser ne loer
savoir.tr: I cannot so praise him to you nor extol him

savoir.of: Lessez (la) folie, tenez vos al saveir.
savoir.tr: Leave aside folly, hold to wisdom.

savoir.of: Guenes respont: «Ço vos sai jo ben dire.
savoir.tr: Ganelon answers: «That I can well tell you.

savoir.of: Cunseill n'est proz dunt hume n'est sevus.
savoir.tr: Counsel is not worthwhile of which no man can be sure.

savoir.of: Deus! quel dulur que li Franceis nel sevent! AOI.
savoir.tr: God! what grief that the French do not know it! AOI.

savoir.of: Il ne sevent, liquels d'els la veintrat.
savoir.tr: They do not know which of the two will win it.

savoir.of: Guenes respunt: «Veir dites, jol sai bien.» AOI.
savoir.tr: Ganelon answers: «You speak truly, I know it well.» AOI.

savoir.of: «Bel sire nies, or savez veirement,
savoir.tr: «Fair lord nephew, now you know truly,

savoir.of: Guenes le sout, li fel, li traïtur,
savoir.tr: Ganelon knew it, the traitor, the betrayer,

savoir.of: Tant en i ad que mesure n'en set.
savoir.tr: There are so many of them that he does not know their number.

savoir.of: Dist Oliver: «D'iço ne sai jo blasme?
savoir.tr: Said Oliver: «Of this I do not know how to make complaint.

savoir.of: «Sire cumpainz, mult ben le saviez
savoir.tr: «Sir companion, you knew it well

savoir.of: Il n'en set mot, n'i ad culpes li bers.
savoir.tr: He knows nothing of it; the noble lord is not to blame.

savoir.of: Ne l'oï dire ne jo mie nel sai
savoir.tr: I never heard it told, nor do I know at all

savoir.of: Il nel sevent, ne dient veir nient:
savoir.tr: They do not know it, they speak no truth at all:

savoir.of: Poez saveir que mult grant doel en out.
savoir.tr: You may be sure that he felt very great grief at it.

savoir.of: Ben set ferir e de lance e d'espiet.»
savoir.tr: He well knows how to strike with both lance and spear.»

savoir.of: Dist Oliver: «Jo nel sai cument quere.
savoir.tr: Said Oliver: «I know not how to seek it.

savoir.of: Asez savez le grant orgoill Rollant;
savoir.tr: You know well Roland's great pride;

savoir.of: Home ki ço set, que ja n'avrat prisun
savoir.tr: A man who knows this, that he will never be taken prisoner,

savoir.of: E de m'espee enquoi savras le nom.»
savoir.tr: and you will know today the name of my sword.»

savoir.of: E or sai ben n'avons guaires a vivre;
savoir.tr: And now I know well we have not much longer to live;

savoir.of: Ço dist li quens: «Or sai jo veirement
savoir.tr: The count said this: «Now I know truly

savoir.of: «Deus!» dist li quens, «or ne sai jo que face.
savoir.tr: «God!» said the count, «now I know not what to do.

secourir.of: Succurrat nos li reis od tut sun barnet.»
secourir.tr: The king will come to our aid with all his host of barons.»

sembler.of: De noz Franceis m'i semblet aveir mult poi!
sembler.tr: of our Franks it seems to me there are very few!
«This Saracen seems to me very much a heretic:

sembler.of: «S'altre le desist, ja semblast grant mençunge!» AOI.
sembler.tr: «Had another said it, it would seem a great lie!» AOI.

sentir.of: Oliver sent que a mort est ferut.
sentir.tr: Oliver feels that he is wounded to death.

sentir.of: Oliver sent qu'il est a mort nasfret.
sentir.tr: Oliver feels that he is wounded to death.

sentir.of: Oliver sent que la mort mult l'angoisset.
sentir.tr: Oliver feels that death greatly anguishes him.

seoir.of: Sur palies blancs siedent cil cevaler,
seoir.tr: Upon white silks those knights are seated,

seoir.of: La siet li reis, ki dulce france tient.
seoir.tr: There sits the king who holds sweet France.

seoir.of: Alez sedeir, quant nuls ne vos sumunt.»
seoir.tr: Go sit down, since no one summons you.»
«Go sit down upon that white silk cloth!

seoir.of: Er matin sedeit li emperere suz l'umbre;
seoir.tr: Yesterday morning the emperor sat in the shade;

seoir.of: Li quens Gerins set el ceval Sorel
seoir.tr: Count Gerin sits on his horse Sorel

seoir.of: Siet el cheval qu'il tolit a Grossaille,
seoir.tr: He sits on the horse that he took from Grossaille,

seoir.of: As Innocenz vos en serez seant.»
seoir.tr: Among the Innocents you shall be seated.»

seoir.of: Siet el ceval qu'il cleimet Barbamusche,
seoir.tr: He sits on the horse he calls Barbamusche,

seoir.of: Siet el cheval qu'il cleimet Gramimund,
seoir.tr: He sits on the horse he calls Gramimund,

seoir.of: Siet el ceval qu'il cleimet Salt Perdut:
seoir.tr: He sits on the horse that he calls Salt Perdut:

seoir.of: Siet el cheval que il cleimet Marmorie,
seoir.tr: He sits on the horse that he calls Marmorie,

seoir.of: Ki armes portet e en bon cheval set;
seoir.tr: Who bears arms and sits upon a good horse;

seoir.of: Siet el cheval qu'il apelet Gaignun,
seoir.tr: He sits on the horse that he calls Gaignun,

seoir.of: Li Marganices sist sur un ceval sor,
seoir.tr: The Marganice sat upon a sorrel horse,

servir.tr: serves Mahomet and calls upon Apollin;

servir.of: «Bel sire reis, jo vos ai servit tant,
servir.tr: «Fair lord king, I have served you so long,

servir.of: Se lui servez, l'onur del camp ert nostre.
servir.tr: If you serve him, the honor of the field will be ours.

servir.of: Si lungement tuz tens m'avez servit,
servir.tr: So long, through all time, you have served me,

sevrer.of: Tute la teste li ad par mi sevree,
sevrer.tr: He has cut his whole head clean in two,

signer.of: De sa main destre l'ad asols e seignet,
signer.tr: With his right hand he absolved and blessed him,

signer.of: E l'arcevesque de Deu les ad seignez;
signer.tr: And the archbishop has blessed them in God's name;

songer.of: Sunjat qu'il eret al greignurs porz de Sizer,
songer.tr: He dreamt that he was at the great passes of Cize,
After this one he dreamed another vision:

sonner.of: N'i ad celoi ki mot sunt ne mot tint,
sonner.tr: There is none among them who speaks a word nor utters a word,

sonner.of: Par mi cel ost funt mil grailles suner;
sonner.tr: Throughout that host they have a thousand trumpets sounded;

sonner.of: En Sarraguce fait suner ses taburs;
sonner.tr: In Saragossa he has his drums sounded;

sonner.of: Sunent mil grailles por ço que plus bel seit:
sonner.tr: A thousand trumpets sound so that it may be more splendid:

sonner.of: «Mis parrastre est, ne voeill que mot en suns.»
sonner.tr: «He is my stepfather, I do not wish that you say a word of it.»

sonner.of: Cumpaign Rollant, kar sunez vostre corn:
sonner.tr: Companion Roland, do sound your horn:

sonner.of: Vostre olifan, suner vos nel deignastes;
sonner.tr: Your olifant, you did not deign to sound it;
You did not deign to sound your oliphant,

sonner.of: Sunet sun gresle pur les soens ralier.
sonner.tr: He sounds his bugle to rally his men.

sonner.of: .VII. milie graisles i sunent la menee:
sonner.tr: Seven thousand trumpets sound the charge there:

sonner.of: Si fait suner ses cors e ses buisines,
sonner.tr: So he has his horns and his trumpets sounded,

sonner.of: Empeint le ben, par grant vertut le sunet.
sonner.tr: He grips it well, sounds it with great force.

sonner.of: Par grant dulor sunet sun olifan.
sonner.tr: With great anguish sounds his oliphant.

sonner.of: Unc nel sunast se ne fust (cu)cumbatant.»
sonner.tr: He would never sound it unless he were fighting.»

sonner.of: L'olifan sunet a dulor e a peine.
sonner.tr: He sounds the oliphant in sorrow and in pain.

sonner.of: Li empereres ad fait suner ses corns.
sonner.tr: The emperor has had his horns sounded.

sonner.of: Sunent cil graisle e derere e devant,
sonner.tr: Those trumpets sound, both in the rear and in the van,

souffrir.tr: - «Sire,» said Ganelon, «it falls to me to endure it;

souffrir.of: Pur sun seignor deit hom susfrir destreiz
souffrir.tr: For his lord a man should suffer hardships

souffrir.of: Pur sun seignur deit hom susfrir granz mals
souffrir.tr: For his lord a man must suffer great hardships

souffrir.of: Nuveles vos di: mort vos estoet susfrir.
souffrir.tr: News I tell you: you must suffer death.

souffrir.of: Li Sarrazin nel poent susfrir tant:
souffrir.tr: The Saracens cannot endure it so long:

souffrir.of: Ço est merveille que Deus le soefret tant.
souffrir.tr: It is a marvel that God suffers him so long.

sourdre.of: Li reis Marsilie od sa grant ost lor surt. AOI.
sourdre.tr: King Marsile with his great host surges upon them. AOI.

suivre.of: Vos le sivrez a la feste seint Michel:
suivre.tr: You will follow him there at the feast of Saint Michael:

suivre.of: Que jel sivrai od mil de mes fedeilz,
suivre.tr: Before I follow him with a thousand of my faithful,

suivre.of: La vos sivrat, ço dit mis avoez.»
suivre.tr: there he will follow you, so says my lord.»

suivre.of: Mis avoez la vos sivrat, ço dit;
suivre.tr: My lord will follow you there, so he says;

suivre.of: Il me sivrat ad Aís, a mun estage,
suivre.tr: He will follow me to Aix, to my residence,

suivre.of: Qu'il vos sivrat en France le regnet,
suivre.tr: Before he will follow you into France the realm,

suivre.of: Sun cumpaignun apres le vait sivant,
suivre.tr: His companion comes following after him,

tailler.of: Tient Durendal, ki ben trenchet e taillet,
tailler.tr: Holds Durendal, which cuts and slashes well,

taire.of: Franceis se taisent ne mais que Guenelun,
taire.tr: The French keep silent, all save Ganelon,

taire.of: Respunt li reis: «Ambdui vos en taisez!
taire.tr: The king answers: «Both of you, be silent about it!

taire.of: Franceis se taisent: as les vus aquisez.
taire.tr: The Franks fall silent: behold them quieted.

teindre.of: En Rencesvals jo la teindrai vermeille.
teindre.tr: At Roncevaux I shall stain it crimson.

tendre.of: Li empereres tent (...) ses mains vers Deu,
tendre.tr: The emperor stretches out his hands toward God,

tendre.of: El grant verger fait li reis tendre un tref,
tendre.tr: In the great orchard the king has a tent pitched,

tendre.of: Li empereres li tent sun guant le destre;
tendre.tr: The emperor holds out to him his right glove;

tendre.of: Dunez li l'arc que vos avez tendut,
tendre.tr: Give him the bow that you have drawn,

tenir.of: Li reis Marsilie la tient, ki Deu nen amet,
tenir.tr: King Marsile holds it — he who does not love God,

tenir.of: A seint Michel tendrat mult halte feste.
tenir.tr: At Saint Michael's he will hold a very high feast.

tenir.of: La siet li reis, ki dulce france tient.
tenir.tr: There sits the king who holds sweet France.

tenir.of: Li empereres en tint sun chef enclin;
tenir.tr: The emperor kept his head bowed;

tenir.of: Chrestiens ert, de mei tendrat ses marches;
tenir.tr: He will be a Christian, will hold his marches from me;

tenir.of: Li empe[re]re en tint sun chef enbrunc,
tenir.tr: The emperor held his head bowed down,

tenir.of: E tute Espaigne tendrat par vostre dun,
tenir.tr: And will hold all Spain by your gift,

tenir.of: Puis recevrat la lei que nus tenum,
tenir.tr: Then will receive the faith that we hold,

tenir.of: Laissun les fols, as sages nus tenuns.» AOI.
tenir.tr: Let us leave the fools, let us hold to the wise.» AOI.

tenir.of: Al Sarrazin ki Sarraguce tient?»
tenir.tr: To the Saracen who holds Saragossa?»

tenir.of: L'estreu li tint sun uncle Guinemer.
tenir.tr: His uncle Guinemer held the stirrup for him.

tenir.of: E lui aidez e pur seignur le tenez.»
tenir.tr: And help him and hold him as your lord.»
