tenir.of: En sa main tint une vermeille pume:
tenir.tr: In his hand he held a crimson apple:

tenir.of: «Tenez bel sire,» dist Rollant a sun uncle,
tenir.tr: «Take it, fair lord,» said Roland to his uncle,

tenir.of: La fut li reis ki tute Espaigne tint;
tenir.tr: There was the king who held all Spain;

tenir.of: Par le puig[n] tint le cunte Guenelun,
tenir.tr: By the fist he held the count Ganelon,

tenir.of: E d'Apollin, qui seintes leis tenuns!
tenir.tr: And by Apollo, whose holy laws we hold!

tenir.of: Un algier tint, ki d'or fut enpenet,
tenir.tr: He held a javelin, which was feathered with gold,

tenir.of: En sun puign destre par l'orie punt la tint.
tenir.tr: In his right fist he held it by the golden pommel.

tenir.of: Quar ço vos mandet Carles, ki France tient,
tenir.tr: For thus Charles, who rules France, commands you:

tenir.of: Lessez (la) folie, tenez vos al saveir.
tenir.tr: Leave aside folly, hold to wisdom.

tenir.of: «Tenez m'espee, meillur n'en at nuls hom;
tenir.tr: «Take my sword, no man has a better one;

tenir.of: «Tenez mun helme, unches meillor ne vi.
tenir.tr: «Take my helmet, never did I see a better one.

tenir.of: Marsilies tint Guen[elun] par l'espalle;
tenir.tr: Marsile took Ganelon by the shoulder;

tenir.of: Par cele lei que vos tenez plus salve,
tenir.tr: By the law that you hold most sacred,

tenir.of: Tenez les clefs de ceste citet large,
tenir.tr: Take the keys of this great city,

tenir.of: Que il ne voelent ne tenir ne guarder.
tenir.tr: Which they wish neither to hold nor to keep.

tenir.of: Si recevrat la lei que vos tenez,
tenir.tr: And he will receive the faith that you hold,

tenir.of: De vos tendrat Espaigne le regnet.»
tenir.tr: From you he will hold Spain the realm.»

tenir.of: Entre ses poinz teneit sa hanste fraisnine.
tenir.tr: Between his hands he held his ashen lance.

tenir.of: N'i perdrat Carles, li reis ki France tient,
tenir.tr: Charles, the king who holds France, shall lose nothing by it,

tenir.of: «Dunez mei l'arc, que vos tenez el poign.
tenir.tr: «Give me the bow that you hold in your fist.

tenir.of: Li empereres en tint sun chef enbrunc,
tenir.tr: The emperor held his head bowed,

tenir.of: Li nies Marsilies tient le guant en sun poign,
tenir.tr: Marsile's nephew holds the gauntlet in his fist,

tenir.of: Cil tient la tere entre[s]qu'as Cazmarine.
tenir.tr: He holds the land as far as Cazmarine.

tenir.of: «Seignurs barons, suef pas alez tenant!
tenir.tr: «Lord barons, go forward at a gentle pace!

tenir.of: Il tint la tere Datliun e Balbiun.
tenir.tr: He held the land of Dathan and Abiram.

tenir.of: «Ceste bataille ben la puum tenir,
tenir.tr: «This battle we can well sustain,

tenir.of: Tient Durendal, ki ben trenchet e taillet,
tenir.tr: Holds Durendal, which cuts and slashes well,

tenir.of: Tient Halteclere, sanglent en est l'acer,
tenir.tr: Grasps Halteclere, its steel is bloody,

tenir.of: Tient Durendal, qui plus valt que fin ór.
tenir.tr: He grasps Durendal, which is worth more than fine gold.

tenir.of: Ki tint Valeri e envers sur le Rosne.
tenir.tr: Who held Valence and the lands along the Rhône.

tenir.of: [L]i quens Rollant tint s'espee sanglente.
tenir.tr: Count Roland held his bloodied sword.

tenir.of: Del corn qu'il tient l'oiïe en est mult grant:
tenir.tr: Of the horn he holds the sound is very great:

tenir.of: Tient Durendal, cume vassal i fiert.
tenir.tr: He holds Durendal, and strikes like a brave warrior.

tenir.of: Ki tint Kartagene, Alfrere, Garmalie
tenir.tr: Who held Carthage, Alfrere, Garmalie

tenir.of: Tient Halteclere, dunt li acer fut bruns,
tenir.tr: He grips Halteclere, whose steel was burnished,

toucher.of: Sur un mulet od un bastun tuchant.
toucher.tr: On a mule, prodding it with a stick.

toucher.of: Deus le guarit, qu'el(l) cors ne l'ad tuchet.
toucher.tr: God protects him, for it did not touch his body.

tourner.of: Sur mei avez turnet fals jugement.
tourner.tr: you have turned a false judgment upon me.

tourner.of: Guardez de nos ne turnez le curage.
tourner.tr: Take care that you do not turn your heart away from us.

tourner.of: Cuntre le ciel vait la mure turnant,
tourner.tr: Toward the sky he goes turning the point,
One half of it he turns downward;

tourner.of: Apres li dist: «Turnet estes a perdre!» AOI.
tourner.tr: Afterward he says to him: «You are doomed to perish!» AOI.

tourner.of: Ja cil d'Espaigne ne s'en deivent turner liez.
tourner.tr: Those of Spain shall not return from here rejoicing.

tourner.of: Ansdous les oilz en la teste li turnent,
tourner.tr: Both his eyes turn back in his head,

trahir.of: Traït vos ad ki a guarder vos out.
trahir.tr: He who should have guarded you has betrayed you.

trahir.of: Cil l'at traït ki vos en roevet feindre.
trahir.tr: He who asks you to feign indifference has betrayed him.

traire.of: Enceis qu'en seient. VII.C. espees traites.
traire.tr: Before seven hundred swords be drawn.

traire.of: Trait Durendal, sa bone espee nue,
traire.tr: He draws Durendal, his good naked sword,

traire.of: - «Ne la poi traire,» Oliver li respunt,
traire.tr: - «I could not draw it,» Oliver answers him,

traire.of: Danz Oliver trait ad sa bone espee,
traire.tr: Lord Oliver has drawn his good sword,

trancher.of: De noz ostages ferat tre[n]cher les testes;
trancher.tr: He will have the heads of our hostages cut off;

trancher.of: Tanz colps ad pris de bons espiez trenchanz,
trancher.tr: So many blows has he taken from good sharp spears,

trancher.of: La destre oreille al premer uer trenchat,
trancher.tr: It tore off the right ear of the first beast,

trancher.of: Jo l'ocirai a mun espiet trenchant.
trancher.tr: I will kill him with my sharp spear.

trancher.of: Trenchet le piz, si li briset les os,
trancher.tr: Slashes his chest, and breaks his bones,

trancher.of: Trenchet li le coer, le firie e le pulmun,
trancher.tr: He cuts through his heart, his liver, and his lung,

trancher.of: Que tut li trenchet le vermeill e le blanc;
trancher.tr: So that he cuts clean through the red and the white;

trancher.of: Trenchet la cors e la cheveleüre,
trancher.tr: He cuts through the scalp and the hair,

trancher.of: Si li trenchat les oilz e la faiture,
trancher.tr: And cut through the eyes and the face,

trancher.of: Trenchet l'eschine, hunc n'i out quis [joi]nture,
trancher.tr: He cuts through the spine, never seeking a joint there,

trancher.of: Tient Durendal, ki ben trenchet e taillet,
trancher.tr: Holds Durendal, which cuts and slashes well,

trancher.of: Trenchet le cors e [la] bronie safree,
trancher.tr: Cleaves the body and the gilded mail-coat,

trancher.of: E al ceval a l'eschine trenchee;
trancher.tr: And cut through the horse's spine;

trancher.of: Le cors li trenchet tres l'un costet qu'a l'altre,
trancher.tr: He cleaves his body from one side to the other,

trancher.of: Escababi i ad le chef trenchet;
trancher.tr: He has cut off the head of Escababi;

trancher.of: Trenchet la teste e la bronie e le cors,
trancher.tr: He slices through the head and the hauberk and the body,

trancher.of: Tut li trenchat le vermeill e l'azur;
trancher.tr: He cuts clean through the vermilion and the azure;

trancher.of: Trenchet le nes e la buche e les denz,
trancher.tr: Cuts through the nose and the mouth and the teeth,

trancher.of: Tren[chen]t cez poinz, cez costez, cez eschines,
trancher.tr: They cut through these fists, these flanks, these spines,

trancher.of: Faldrun de Pui i ad par mi trenchet,
trancher.tr: He has cut Faldrun of Pui clean through the middle,

trancher.of: Trenchet li ad li quens le destre poign.
trancher.tr: the count has cut off his right fist.

trancher.of: Trenchet la teste d'ici qu'as denz menuz,
trancher.tr: He cleaves the head down to the fine teeth,

trancher.of: Trenchet cez hanstes e cez escuz buclers,
trancher.tr: He slices through those lances and those bossed shields,

transmettre.of: Que li tramist li reis de Suatilie;
transmettre.tr: Which the king of Suatilie had sent to him;

transmettre.of: «Li reis Marsilie m'ad tramis ses messages;
transmettre.tr: «King Marsile has sent me his messengers;

transmettre.of: Dous de voz cuntes al paien tramesistes,
transmettre.tr: Two of your counts you sent to the pagan,

transmettre.of: Se lui lessez, n'i trametrez plus saive.»
transmettre.tr: If you leave him aside, you will not send a wiser one.»

transmettre.of: Si la tramist li amiralz de Primes.
transmettre.tr: The emir of Primes sent it to me thus.

transmettre.of: Si li tramist li amiralz Galafes.
transmettre.tr: And the emir Galafe sent it on to him.

transmettre.of: Dist al paien: «Deus tut mal te tramette!
transmettre.tr: He said to the pagan: «May God send you every evil!

travailler.of: Lui e altrui travaillent, e cunfundent.»
travailler.tr: They torment him and others, and bring ruin.»

travailler.of: Par tantes teres ad sun cors traveillet,
travailler.tr: Through so many lands he has worn out his body,

troubler.of: Tant ad seinet (ki) li oil li sunt trublet;
troubler.tr: He has bled so much that his eyes are clouded;

trousser.of: D'or e d'argent .IIII. cenz muls trussez,
trousser.tr: four hundred mules loaded with gold and silver,

trousser.of: Franc desherbergent, funt lur sumers trosser:
trousser.tr: The Franks break camp, have their pack-horses loaded:

trousser.of: Par tel air l'at estrussee e brandie,
trousser.tr: With such force he wrenched and brandished it,

trouver.of: Se en rereguarde troevet le cors Rollant,
trouver.tr: If in the rearguard he finds Roland's person,
That we may find him in the rearguard.»

trouver.of: Sel pois trover a port ne a passage,
trouver.tr: If I can find him at pass or defile,

trouver.of: Si li truvez ki tres bien li aiut!»
trouver.tr: And find him someone who may aid him well!»

trouver.of: Se truis Rollant, ne lerrai que nel mat!»
trouver.tr: If I find Roland, I will not fail to bring him down!»

trouver.of: Se truis Rollant, de mort serat finet,
trouver.tr: If I find Roland, he will be finished by death,

trouver.of: Se trois Rollant, de mort li duins fiance.
trouver.tr: If I find Roland, I pledge him to death.

trouver.of: Se trois Rollant, n'en porterat la teste,
trouver.tr: If I find Roland, he will not carry away his head,

trouver.of: Se trois Rollant li proz enmi ma veie,
trouver.tr: If I find Roland the brave in my path,

trouver.of: Truverunt nos e morz e detrenchez,
trouver.tr: They will find us both dead and cut to pieces,

trouver.of: Cuntre un des noz en truverat morz .XV.,
trouver.tr: For one of ours he will find fifteen of them dead,

user.of: Il est mult vielz, si ad sun tens uset;
user.tr: He is very old, and has worn out his time;

vaincre.of: Li reis Marsilie est de guere vencud:
vaincre.tr: King Marsile is defeated in war:

vaincre.of: Ses citez arses e ses humes vencuz;
vaincre.tr: Burned his cities and vanquished his men;

vaincre.of: Tanz riches reis morz e vencuz en champ:
vaincre.tr: So many rich kings dead and vanquished in the field:

vaincre.of: Il ne sevent, liquels d'els la veintrat.
vaincre.tr: They do not know which of the two will win it.

vaincre.of: Faites batailles e vencues en champ!
vaincre.tr: Fought battles and won them in the field!

vaincre.of: El camp estez, que ne seium vencuz!
vaincre.tr: Hold the field, that we be not vanquished!»

vaincre.of: Ferez i, Francs, kar tres ben les veincrum!»
vaincre.tr: Strike, Franks, for we shall surely defeat them!»

vaincre.of: Par tel glutun n'ert bataille oi vencue.»
vaincre.tr: By such a villain a battle will not be won today.»

vaincre.of: «Ferez paien, car tres ben les veintrum!»
vaincre.tr: «Strike, pagans, for we shall vanquish them right well!»

valoir.of: Jamais n'ert hume ki encuntre lui vaille.» AOI.
valoir.tr: Never will there be a man who can match himself against him.» AOI.

valoir.of: Melz en valt l'or que ne funt cinc cenz livres:
valoir.tr: their gold is worth more than five hundred pounds:

valoir.of: Eles valent mielz que tut l'aveir de Rume,
valoir.tr: They are worth more than all the wealth of Rome,

valoir.of: Plus valt Mahum que seint Perre de Rume!
valoir.tr: Muhammad is worth more than Saint Peter of Rome!

valoir.of: Sis bons escuz un dener ne li valt:
valoir.tr: His good shield is not worth a denier to him:

valoir.of: Fers e acers i deit aveir valor.
valoir.tr: Iron and steel ought to be of worth here.

valoir.of: Enpres sun colp ne quid que un dener vaillet,
valoir.tr: After his blow I think it is not worth a denier,

valoir.of: Tient Durendal, qui plus valt que fin ór.
valoir.tr: He grasps Durendal, which is worth more than fine gold.

valoir.of: Fuïr s'en voel, mais ne li valt nient:
valoir.tr: He wishes to flee from it, but it avails him nothing:

valoir.of: Mielz valt mesure que ne fait estultie.
valoir.tr: Moderation is worth more than recklessness.

valoir.of: De ço qui calt? car ne lur valt nient.
valoir.tr: But what use is it? It avails them nothing.
Or otherwise he is not worth four deniers;

vanter.of: N'en vanteras el regne dunt tu fus
vanter.tr: Will you boast, in the realm from which you came,

vendre.of: Qu'en Sarraguce sa maisnee alat vendre;
vendre.tr: When he went to Saragossa to sell his household;

vendre.of: Tel as ocis que mult cher te quid vendre!»
vendre.tr: You have killed one such that I think I will sell him to you very dear!»

vendre.of: Einz que il moergent, se vendrunt mult cher.
vendre.tr: Before they die, they will sell themselves very dear.

vendre.of: Mais tut seit fel cher ne se vende primes!
vendre.tr: But cursed be whoever does not sell himself dearly first!

venger.of: Si vengez cels que li fels fist ocire!» AOI.
venger.tr: And avenge those whom the villain had killed!» AOI.

venger.of: Li emperere nos devreit ben venger.
venger.tr: The emperor ought well to avenge us.

venger.of: Respont li quens: «Deus le me doinst venger!»
venger.tr: The count replies: «May God grant me to avenge him!»

venger.of: Venget li reis, si nus purrat venger;
venger.tr: Let the king come, for he will be able to avenge us;

venger.of: Jamais n'iert home plus se voeillet venger.
venger.tr: Never will a man be more eager to take revenge.

venger.of: Li nostre deu, vengez nos de Carlun.
venger.tr: O our gods, avenge us on Charles!

venger.of: Kar de vos sul ai ben venget les noz.»
venger.tr: For through you alone I have well avenged our men.»

venger.of: De lui venger ja mais ne li ert lez.
venger.tr: Never will it be pleasant for him to avenge himself.

venir.of: En cest païs nos est venuz cunfundre.
venir.tr: Has come into this land to destroy us.

venir.of: Vendrat li jurz, si passerat li termes,
venir.tr: The day will come, and so the term will pass,

venir.of: Vindrent a Charles ki France ad en baillie:
venir.tr: They came to Charles, who has France in his keeping:

venir.of: La noit demurent tresque vint al jur cler.
venir.tr: The night they remain until the bright day came.

venir.of: Ensembl' od els li quens Rollant i vint,
venir.tr: Together with them Count Roland came there,

venir.of: Guenes i vint, ki la traïsun fist.
venir.tr: Guenes came there, who committed the treason.

venir.of: En piez se drecet, si li vint cuntredire.
venir.tr: Rises to his feet, and came to contradict him.

venir.of: Set anz [ad] pleins, que en Espaigne venimes;
venir.tr: Seven full years it is, since we came into Spain;

venir.of: En piez se drecet, si vint devant Carlun,
venir.tr: He rises to his feet, and came before Charles,

venir.of: Apres iço i est Neimes venud;
venir.tr: After this, Naimes came forward;

venir.of: Ço dist li reis: «Guenes venez avant. AOI.
venir.tr: The king said: «Ganelon, come forward. AOI.

venir.of: Vint i ses nies, out vestue sa brunie,
venir.tr: His nephew came, had donned his hauberk,

venir.of: Blancandrins vint devant l'empereür;
venir.tr: Blancandrin came before the emperor;

venir.of: En Sarraguce vus vendrat aseger;
venir.tr: He will come to besiege you in Saragossa;

venir.of: E Blancandrins i vint, al canud peil,
venir.tr: And Blancandrin came there, with the hoary hair,

venir.of: Puis si cumencet a venir ses tresors. AOI.
venir.tr: Then he begins to bring forth his treasures. AOI.

venir.of: A tant i vint uns paiens, Valdabruns:
venir.tr: Thereupon came a pagan, Valdabrun:

venir.of: Apres i vint un paien, Climorins.
venir.tr: After him came a pagan, Climorins.

venir.of: A tant i vint la reine Bramimunde.
venir.tr: Then came the queen Bramimonde.

venir.of: Venuz en est a la citet de Galne.
venir.tr: He has come to the city of Galne.

venir.of: Guenes li quens est venuz as herberges. AOI.
venir.tr: Ganelon the count has come to the encampment. AOI.

venir.of: Guenes i vint, li fels, li parjurez.
venir.tr: Ganelon came there, the felon, the perjurer.

venir.of: Devers Ardene vit venir uns leuparz,
venir.tr: From toward the Ardennes he saw a leopard coming,

venir.of: Que vint a Carles le galops e les salz,
venir.tr: Which came to Charles at a gallop and by leaps,

venir.of: Anpres iço i est Neimes venud:
venir.tr: After this Naimes came forward:

venir.of: Cuntre lui vient sis cumpainz Oliver;
venir.tr: Toward him comes his companion Oliver;

venir.of: Vint i Gerins e li proz quens Gerers,
venir.tr: There came Gerin and the valiant count Gerier,

venir.of: E vint i Otes, si i vint Berengers,
venir.tr: And there came Oton, and there came Berengier,

venir.of: E vint i Astors e Anseïs li veillz;
venir.tr: And there came Astor and Anseïs the old;

venir.of: Vint i Gerart de Rossillon li fiers;
venir.tr: There came Gerart of Roussillon the proud;

venir.of: Venuz i est li riches dux Gaifiers.
venir.tr: The mighty duke Gaifier has come there too.

venir.of: Puis que il venent a la Tere Majur,
venir.tr: When they come to the Great Land,

venir.of: Li nies Marsilie, il est venuz avant,
venir.tr: The nephew of Marsile, he has come forward,

venir.of: Ço dist Marsilie: «Seignurs, venez avant!
venir.tr: Marsile said this: «Lords, come forward!

venir.of: Venez i, reis, sil verrez veirement:
venir.tr: Come there, king, and you will truly see it:

venir.of: Curant i vint Margariz de Sibilie;
venir.tr: Margariz of Seville came running there;

venir.of: Vint en la presse, sur les altres s'escriet
venir.tr: He came into the throng, above the others he cries out

venir.of: Si veit venir cele gent paienur,
venir.tr: And sees the pagan host coming,

venir.of: «Devers Espaigne vei venir tel bruur,
venir.tr: «From the direction of Spain I see such a tumult coming,

venir.of: Vint as Franceis, tut lur ad acuntet.
venir.tr: Came to the French, told them everything.

venir.of: Felun paien mar i vindrent as porz:
venir.tr: Ill-fated pagans, in an evil hour they came to the passes:

venir.of: Melz voeill murir que huntage me venget.
venir.tr: I would rather die than that shame befall me.

venir.of: Marsilie vient par mi une valee
venir.tr: Marsile comes through the middle of a valley

venir.of: D'Affrike i ad un Affrican venut,
venir.tr: From Africa there has come an African,

venir.of: Ki quel cumpert, venuz en sunt ensemble.
venir.tr: Whoever pays for it, they have come together.

venir.of: Sem(e) creïsez, venuz i fust mi sire;
venir.tr: Had you trusted me, my lord would have come here;

venir.of: Vint tresqu'a els, sis prist a castier:
venir.tr: Came right up to them, and began to rebuke them:

venir.of: Venget li reis, si nus purrat venger;
venir.tr: Let the king come, for he will be able to avenge us;

venir.of: Josque il vengent el camp cumunement:
venir.tr: Until they reach the field all together:

venir.of: Quant en cest camp vendrat Carles, mi sire,
venir.tr: When Charles, my lord, comes onto this field,

violer.of: Les castels pris, (. . .) les citez violees.
violer.tr: taken the castles, (. . .) violated the cities.
And violated the temple of Solomon,

vivre.of: Ne l'amerai a trestut mun vivant,
vivre.tr: I shall not love him all the days of my life,

vivre.of: Tant ad erret nen est dreiz que plus vivet.
vivre.tr: He has gone so far that it is not right that he live longer.

vivre.of: - «Ço n'iert,» dist Guenes: «tant cum vivet sis niés:
vivre.tr: - «That will not be,» said Ganelon, «as long as his nephew lives:

vivre.of: - «Ço n'iert,» dist Guenes, «tant cum vivet Rollant:
vivre.tr: - «That will not be,» said Ganelon, «so long as Roland lives:

vivre.of: Soürs est Carlles, ne (cre) crent hume vivant.» AOI.
vivre.tr: Charles is secure, he fears no living man.» AOI.

vivre.of: «Que ço seit dit de nul hume vivant,
vivre.tr: «That it be said by any living man,

vivre.of: Ultre cest jurn ne serum plus vivant;
vivre.tr: Beyond this day we shall be alive no longer;

vivre.of: E or sai ben n'avons guaires a vivre;
vivre.tr: And now I know well we have not much longer to live;

vivre.of: Quant tu es mor[t], dulur est que jo vif!»
vivre.tr: Since you are dead, it is grief that I live!»

voir.of: L'ost des Franceis verrez sempres desfere.
voir.tr: You will soon see the host of the Franks disband.

voir.of: Ja einz ne verrat passer cest premer meis,
voir.tr: He will not see this first month pass

voir.of: Par ceste barbe que veez [blancheier],
voir.tr: By this beard that you see growing white,

voir.of: Sin vois vedeir alques de sun semblant.»
voir.tr: And go see something of his disposition.»

voir.of: Quant ço veit Guenes que ore s'en rit Rollant,
voir.tr: When Ganelon sees that Roland now laughs about it,

voir.of: Dreiz emperere, veiz me ci en present,
voir.tr: Rightful emperor, here you see me present,

voir.of: Gua[r]dez le ben, ja nel verrai des oilz.»
voir.tr: Guard him well, for I shall never see him again with my eyes.»

voir.of: La veïsez tant chevaler plorer,
voir.tr: There you would have seen so many knights weep,

voir.of: Quant le vit Guenes, mist la main a l'espee,
voir.tr: When Ganelon saw it, he put his hand to his sword,

voir.of: Guardet al bref, vit la raisun escrite:
voir.tr: He looks at the letter, saw the writing set down there:

voir.of: N'est hom kil veit e conuistre le set
voir.tr: There is no man who sees him and comes to know him

voir.of: «Jo ai tel gent, plus bele ne verreiz;
voir.tr: «I have such troops, you will never see finer;

voir.of: Carlles verrat sun grant orguill cadeir;
voir.tr: Charles will see his great pride fall;

voir.of: «Tenez mun helme, unches meillor ne vi.
voir.tr: «Take my helmet, never did I see a better one.

voir.of: Kar a mes oilz vi .IIII.C. milie armez,
voir.tr: For with my own eyes I saw four hundred thousand armed men,

voir.of: La sunt neiez, jamais nes en verrez;
voir.tr: There they were drowned, never will you see them again;

voir.of: Ja ne verrez cest premer meis passet
voir.tr: Never will you see this first month pass

voir.of: Devers Ardene vit venir uns leuparz,
voir.tr: From toward the Ardennes he saw a leopard coming,

voir.of: «Veez les porz e les destreiz passages:
voir.tr: «You see the passes and the narrow defiles:

voir.of: Virent Guascuigne, la tere lur seignur.
voir.tr: They saw Gascony, the land of their lord.

voir.of: De cels de France virent les gunfanuns.
voir.tr: Of those of France they saw the banners.

voir.of: Veez m'espee, ki est e bone e lunge:
voir.tr: See my sword, which is both good and long:

voir.of: Venez i, reis, sil verrez veirement:
voir.tr: Come there, king, and you will truly see it:

voir.of: Cele nel veit vers lui ne s'esclargisset;
voir.tr: None sees him but she brightens toward him;

voir.of: Quant ele le veit, ne poet muer ne riet;
voir.tr: When she sees him, she cannot help but smile;

voir.of: Veez m'espee, ki d'or est enheldie:
voir.tr: Behold my sword, which is hilted with gold:

voir.of: Si veit venir cele gent paienur,
voir.tr: And sees the pagan host coming,

voir.of: «Devers Espaigne vei venir tel bruur,
voir.tr: «From the direction of Spain I see such a tumult coming,

voir.of: Or veit il ben d'Espaigne le regnet
voir.tr: Now he sees clearly the realm of Spain

voir.of: Dist Oliver: «Jo ai paiens veüz:
voir.tr: Oliver said: «I have seen the pagans:

voir.of: Unc mais nuls hom en tere n'en vit plus.
voir.tr: never before did any man on earth see more of them.

voir.of: Tut en verrez le brant ensanglentet.
voir.tr: You will see its blade all bloodied because of it.

voir.of: De Durendal verrez l'acer sanglent.
voir.tr: You will see the bloody steel of Durendal.

voir.of: Jo ai veüt les Sarrazins d'Espaigne,
voir.tr: I have seen the Saracens of Spain,

voir.of: Dist Oliver: «Rollant, veez en alques!
voir.tr: Said Oliver: «Roland, look over there a bit!

voir.of: Veeir poez, dolente est la rereguarde;
voir.tr: You can see, the rearguard is to be pitied;

voir.of: Quant Rollant veit que la bataille serat,
voir.tr: When Roland sees that the battle will take place,

voir.of: Kar a voz oilz veez les Sarrazins.
voir.tr: For with your own eyes you see the Saracens.

voir.of: Asez ad doel quant vit mort sun nevold,
voir.tr: He has great grief when he sees his nephew dead,

voir.of: Guardet a tere, veit gesir le glutun,
voir.tr: He looks to the ground, sees the wretch lying there,

voir.of: Guardet arere, veit le glutun gesir,
voir.tr: He looks behind, sees the wretch lying there,

voir.of: Ki lui veïst l'un geter mort su[r] l'altre,
voir.tr: Whoever had seen him throw one dead upon another,

voir.of: Hume nel veit,ki mult ne s espaent.
voir.tr: No man sees it who is not greatly terrified.

voir.of: Unches mais hom tel ne vit ajustee.
voir.tr: Never did any man see such a one joined.

voir.of: Marsilies veit de sa gent le martirie,
voir.tr: Marsile sees the slaughter of his people,

voir.of: Unches nuls hom nel vit juer ne rire.
voir.tr: Never did any man see him play or laugh.

voir.of: Cum il le vit, a ferir le desiret.
voir.tr: As soon as he saw him, he longed to strike him.

voir.of: Franceis veient que paiens i ad tant,
voir.tr: The French see that there are so many pagans there,

voir.of: Li quens Rollant, quant il veit Sansun mort,
voir.tr: Count Roland, when he sees Samson dead,

voir.of: Enceis nel vit, sil recunut veirement
voir.tr: Never before had he seen him, yet he recognized him truly

voir.of: La veïssez si grant dulor de gent,
voir.tr: There you would have seen such great suffering of people,

voir.of: Ki puis veïst Rollant e Oliver
voir.tr: Whoever then might have seen Roland and Oliver

voir.of: Li quens Rollant des soens i veit grant perte; AOI.
voir.tr: Count Roland sees great loss among his own men; AOI.

voir.of: Tanz bons vassals veez gesir par tere!
voir.tr: So many good vassals you see lying upon the ground!

voir.of: Se puis veeir ma gente sorur Alde,
voir.tr: If I can see my fair sister Alde again,

voir.of: Vostre proecce, Rollant, mar la ve[ï]mes!
voir.tr: Your prowess, Roland, in an evil hour did we see it!

voir.of: «Se veïssum Rollant einz qu'il fust mortz,
voir.tr: «If only we might see Roland before he is dead,

voir.of: De cels de France i veit tanz morz gesir!
voir.tr: of those of France he sees so many lying dead!

voir.of: Meillors vassals de vos unkes ne vi.
voir.tr: Better vassals than you I never saw.

voir.of: Barons Franceis, pur mei vos vei murir:
voir.tr: French barons, for my sake I see you die:

voir.of: De Sarrazins verrat tel discipline,
voir.tr: He will see such slaughter of Saracens,

voir.of: Quan Rollant veit la contredite gent
voir.tr: When Roland sees the accursed people,

voir.of: Quant paien virent que Franceis i out poi,
voir.tr: When the pagans saw that the French were few there,

voir.of: Ne a muiler ne a dame qu'aies veüd,
voir.tr: But neither to wife nor to lady whom you have seen

voir.of: Ki lui veïst Sarrazins desmembrer,
voir.tr: Whoever had seen him dismember the Saracens,

voir.of: Ne loinz ne pres (es) ne poet vedeir si cler
voir.tr: Neither far nor near can he see clearly enough

voir.of: Jo ne vos vei, veied vus Damnedeu!
voir.tr: I do not see you, may the Lord God see you!

voir.of: Or veit Rollant que mort est sun ami,
voir.tr: Now Roland sees that his friend is dead,

voler.of: Qu'envers le cel en volent les escicles.
voler.tr: That toward the sky the splinters fly.

voler.of: Plus est isnels que n'est oisel ki volet;
voler.tr: Swifter is it than a bird that flies;

vouloir.of: S'en volt ostages, e vos l'en enveiez,
vouloir.tr: If he wants hostages, then send them to him,

vouloir.of: Teres e fiéz tant cum vos en vuldrez.»
vouloir.tr: Lands and fiefs as much as you will want of them.»

vouloir.of: S'il voelt ostages, il en avrat par veir.»
vouloir.tr: If he wants hostages, he shall have them indeed.»

vouloir.of: De sun aveir vos voelt asez duner,
vouloir.tr: of his wealth he wishes to give you plenty,

vouloir.of: - «Voet par hostages,» ço dist li Sarrazins,
vouloir.tr: - «By hostages,» said the Saracen,

vouloir.of: La vuldrat il chrestiens devenir.»
vouloir.tr: There he will wish to become a Christian.»

vouloir.of: Par cels de France voelt il del tut errer. AOI.
vouloir.tr: Through those of France he wishes to act in everything. AOI.

vouloir.of: De sun aveir me voelt duner grant masse,
vouloir.tr: From his wealth he wishes to give me a great quantity,

vouloir.of: U par ostage vos (en) voelt faire soürs;
vouloir.tr: Or by hostages he wishes to give you assurance;

vouloir.of: Se li reis voelt, jo i puis aler ben.»
vouloir.tr: If the king wills it, I can well go there.»

vouloir.of: Si li reis voelt, prez sui por vus le face.» AOI.
vouloir.tr: If the king wishes, I am ready to do it for you.» AOI.

vouloir.of: Ademplir voeill vostre comandement.» AOI.
vouloir.tr: I will fulfill your command.» AOI.

vouloir.of: Mais li quens Guenes iloec ne volsist estre:
vouloir.tr: But Count Ganelon would not wish to be there:

vouloir.of: Ki tute gent voelt faire recreant,
vouloir.tr: He who wants to make every people cry for mercy,

vouloir.of: Pur les nuveles qu'il vuldreient oïr.
vouloir.tr: Because of the news they would wish to hear.

vouloir.of: Demi Espaigne vos voelt en fiu duner.
vouloir.tr: He wishes to give you half of Spain in fief.

vouloir.of: Se cest acorde ne vulez otrier,
vouloir.tr: If you will not grant this accord,

vouloir.of: Ferir l'en volt, se n'en fust desturnet. AOI.
vouloir.tr: He wished to strike him with it, had he not been prevented. AOI.

vouloir.of: Mais de s'espee ne volt mie guerpir;
vouloir.tr: But he would by no means give up his sword;

vouloir.of: Si ceste acorde ne volez otrier,
vouloir.tr: If you will not grant this accord,

vouloir.of: Se de mun cors voeil aquiter la vie,
vouloir.tr: If I wish to ransom the life of my body,

vouloir.of: En talant ai que mult vos voeill amer,
vouloir.tr: I have the desire that I much want to love you,

vouloir.of: De Carlemagne vos voeill oïr parler.
vouloir.tr: I want to hear you speak of Charlemagne.

vouloir.of: Meilz voelt murir que guerpir sun barnet.»
vouloir.tr: He would rather die than abandon his noble company.»

vouloir.of: De mun aveir vos voeill dunner grant masse:
vouloir.tr: Of my wealth I will give you a great quantity:

vouloir.of: Que il ne voelent ne tenir ne guarder.
vouloir.tr: Which they wish neither to hold nor to keep.

vouloir.of: Se Mahumet me voelt estre guarant,
vouloir.tr: If Muhammad is willing to be my protector,

vouloir.of: Pur tut l'or Deu ne volt estre cuard [...]
vouloir.tr: For all God's gold he would not be a coward [...]

vouloir.of: De chrestiens voelt faire male vode.
vouloir.tr: Of Christians he wishes to make an evil massacre.

vouloir.of: «Mis parrastre est, ne voeill que mot en suns.»
vouloir.tr: «He is my stepfather, I do not wish that you say a word of it.»

vouloir.of: Melz voeill murir que huntage me venget.
vouloir.tr: I would rather die than that shame befall me.

vouloir.of: Ne traïsun unkes amer ne volt.
vouloir.tr: Nor did he ever wish to love treason.

vouloir.of: Suz ciel n'at hume que [tant] voeillet haïr;
vouloir.tr: Under heaven there is no man he would so wish to hate;

vouloir.of: Voillet o nun, tut i laisset sun tens.
vouloir.tr: Willing or not, each leaves his life there.

vouloir.of: Fuïr s'en voel, mais ne li valt nient:
vouloir.tr: He wishes to flee from it, but it avails him nothing:

vouloir.of: Voelent u nun, si guerpissent le camp.
vouloir.tr: Willing or not, they abandon the field.

vouloir.of: «Sire cumpaign, sel volez otrier,
vouloir.tr: «Lord companion, if you will grant it,

vouloir.of: Mielz voeill murir que hunte nus seit retraite.» AOI.
vouloir.tr: I would rather die than that shame be reported of us.» AOI.

vouloir.of: Jamais n'iert home plus se voeillet venger.
vouloir.tr: Never will a man be more eager to take revenge.

vouloir.of: L'enseigne Carle n'i volt mie ublier:
vouloir.tr: Charles's battle-cry he does not wish to forget:

vêtir.of: Vint i ses nies, out vestue sa brunie,
vêtir.tr: His nephew came, had donned his hauberk,

vêtir.of: Halbercs vestuz e tres bien fermeez
vêtir.tr: hauberks donned and very well fastened,

vêtir.of: Helmes laciez e blancs osbercs vestuz
vêtir.tr: helmets laced on and white hauberks donned.

ébaudir.of: A icest mot si s'esbaldissent Franc,
ébaudir.tr: At this word the Franks take heart,

éclaircir.of: Cele nel veit vers lui ne s'esclargisset;
éclaircir.tr: None sees him but she brightens toward him;

éclaircir.of: Esclargiz est li vespres e li jurz.
éclaircir.tr: The evening and the day grow bright.

éclairer.of: Que jo n'esclair ceste meie grant ire.»
éclairer.tr: To relieve this great wrath of mine.»

éclairer.of: Par main en l'albe, si cum li jurz esclairet,
éclairer.tr: At morning, in the dawn, as the day grows bright,

éclisser.of: La hanste briset e esclicet josqu'as poinz.
éclisser.tr: His lance breaks and splinters all the way to his fists.

écouter.of: Messe e matines ad li reis escultet.
écouter.tr: Mass and matins the king has heard.

écouter.of: Vos le doüssez esculter e oïr.»
écouter.tr: You ought to have listened to him and heard him out.»

écouter.of: Messe e matines ad li reis escultet.
écouter.tr: Mass and matins has the king heard.

écouter.of: Naimes li duc l'oïd, si l'escultent li Franc.
écouter.tr: Naimes the duke heard it, and the Franks listen to it.

écrier.of: Devant Marsilie cil s'escriet mult halt:
écrier.tr: Before Marsile he cries out very loudly:

écrier.of: Devant Marsilie cil en est escriet:
écrier.tr: Before Marsile this man cries out:

écrier.of: Devant Marsilie s'escriet en la presse,
écrier.tr: Before Marsile he cries out in the throng,

écrier.of: Vint en la presse, sur les altres s'escriet
écrier.tr: He came into the throng, above the others he cries out
He calls out to the French, he addressed Oliver:

écrier.of: A icest mot sunt Franceis escriet.
écrier.tr: At this word the Franks cried out.
And cries out the pagan war-cry;

écrier.of: - «Munjoie!» escriet, ço est l'enseigne Carlun. AOI.
écrier.tr: - «Monjoie!» he cries, that is Charles's war-cry. AOI.
- «Munjoie!» he cries out, to hold the field.

écrier.of: - «Munjoie!» escriet, ço est l'enseigne Carle. AOI.
écrier.tr: - «Munjoie!» he cries, that is Charles's battle-cry. AOI.

écrier.of: De tutes parz est «Munjo[i]e» escriee. AOI.
écrier.tr: On all sides «Munjoie» is cried. AOI.
Then he cries out: «These are good to destroy!

écrier.of: A voiz escriet: «Ferez i, chevaler!» AOI.
écrier.tr: In a loud voice he cries out: «Strike here, knights!» AOI.
The pagans cry out: «Help us, Mahomet!
Then they shout the pagan battle cry.

écrier.of: Apres escriet Rollant qu'il li aiut. AOI.
écrier.tr: Afterward he cries out to Roland to help him. AOI.
«Munjoie!» he cries out both loudly and clearly.

écrire.of: Guardet al bref, vit la raisun escrite:
écrire.tr: He looks at the letter, saw the writing set down there:

écrire.of: Il est escrit en la Geste Francor
écrire.tr: It is written in the Geste of the Franks

écrire.of: Il est escrit es cartres e es brefs,
écrire.tr: It is written in the charters and in the records,

égarer.of: E lui meïsme en est mult esguaret.
égarer.tr: And he himself is much troubled by it.

élire.of: «Car m'eslisez un barun de ma marche,
élire.tr: «Now choose me a baron of my march,
Among themselves they choose twenty thousand knights. AOI.
Choose me twelve of your barons,

émerveiller.of: Dist li paiens: «Mult me puis merveiller
émerveiller.tr: The pagan said: «Greatly may I marvel

émerveiller.of: N'i ait Franceis ki tot ne s'en merveilt.
émerveiller.tr: That there will be no Frank who does not marvel at it.

épargner.of: Turpins i fiert, ki nient ne l'esparignet,
épargner.tr: Turpin strikes him there, who spares him not at all,

épargner.of: Ne mes seisante, que Deus i ad esparniez:
épargner.tr: all but sixty, whom God has spared:
Roland answers: «Strike, do not spare them!»

épier.of: Que Guenelun nos ad tuz espiez;
épier.tr: That Ganelon has betrayed us all;

épouvanter.of: Hume nel veit,ki mult ne s espaent.
épouvanter.tr: No man sees it who is not greatly terrified.

épouvanter.of: Ne poet muer qu'il ne s'en espoent,
épouvanter.tr: He cannot help but be terrified by it,

éveiller.of: Carles se dort, qu'il ne s'esveillet mie.
éveiller.tr: Charles sleeps, for he does not wake at all.

éveiller.of: Carles se dort, mie ne s'esveillat. AOI.
éveiller.tr: Charles sleeps on, he did not wake at all. AOI.

être.of: set anz tuz pleins ad estet en Espaigne:
être.tr: has been in Spain seven full years:

être.of: Mur ne citet n'i est remés a fraindre
être.tr: no wall or city is left to be breached

être.of: Fors Saraguce, ki est en une muntaigne.
être.tr: except Saragossa, which sits upon a mountain.

être.of: Li reis Marsilie esteit en Sarraguce.
être.tr: King Marsile was in Saragossa.

être.of: Alez en est en un verger suz l'umbre;
être.tr: He has gone off into an orchard beneath the shade;

être.of: En cest païs nos est venuz cunfundre.
être.tr: Has come into this land to destroy us.

être.of: Blancandrins fut des plus saives paiens:
être.tr: Blancandrin was among the wisest pagans:

être.of: De vasselage fut asez chevaler,
être.tr: In valor he was very much a knight,

être.of: Serez ses hom par honur e par ben.
être.tr: You will be his man in honor and in good faith.

être.of: Asez est melz qu'il i perdent le chefs,
être.tr: It is far better that they lose their heads,

être.of: Ne nus seiuns cunduiz a mendeier.» AOI.
être.tr: And that we be reduced to begging.» AOI.

être.of: Quant cascuns ert a sun meillor repaire,
être.tr: When each is back at his best dwelling,

être.of: Carles serat ad Ais, a sa capele;
être.tr: Charles will be at Aix, in his chapel;

être.of: Li reis est fiers e sis curages pesmes:
être.tr: The king is fierce and his heart is cruel:

être.of: Asez est mielz, qu'il i perdent les testes,
être.tr: It is far better that they lose their heads there,

être.of: Dient paien: «Issi poet il ben estre!»
être.tr: The pagans say: «It may well be so!»

être.of: Il est al siege a Cordres la citet.
être.tr: He is at the siege of the city of Cordres.

être.of: [S]erai ses hom par amur e par feid;
être.tr: I will be his man through love and through faith;

être.of: Li frein sunt d'or, les seles d'argent mises.
être.tr: The bridles are of gold, the saddles set with silver.

être.of: Cil sunt muntez ki le message firent,
être.tr: Those who carried the message have mounted,

être.of: Ne seit ocis, u devient chrestien.
être.tr: Who is not slain, or becomes a Christian.

être.of: Li empereres est en un grant verger,
être.tr: The emperor is in a great orchard,

être.of: E si i furent e gerin et gerers,
être.tr: And there too were Gerin and Gerier,

être.of: La u cist furent, des altres i out bien:
être.tr: Where these were, there were indeed many others:

être.of: S'est, kil demandet, ne l estoet enseigner.
être.tr: He is so, whoever seeks him, that there is no need to point him out.

être.of: E dist al rei: «Salvez seiez de Deu
être.tr: and said to the king: «Be saved by God

être.of: En cest païs avez estet asez;
être.tr: In this land you have stayed long enough;

être.of: De sa parole ne fut mie hastifs:
être.tr: He was in no way hasty with his speech:

être.of: Sa custume est qu'il parolet a leisír.
être.tr: His custom is that he speaks at leisure.

être.of: Li reis Marsilies est mult mis enemis.
être.tr: King Marsile is a great enemy of mine.

être.of: En quel mesure en purrai estre fiz?»
être.tr: In what measure can I be assured of it?»

être.of: Quant vus serez el palais seignurill,
être.tr: When you are in the lordly palace,

être.of: Bels fut li vespres e li soleilz fut cler.
être.tr: Beautiful was the evening and the sun was bright.

être.of: Li empereres est par matin levet;
être.tr: The emperor is risen at morning;

être.of: Desuz un pin en est li reis alez,
être.tr: Beneath a pine the king has gone,

être.of: E si i furent e Gerers e Gerin;
être.tr: And there too were Gerers and Gerin;

être.of: Chrestiens ert, de mei tendrat ses marches;
être.tr: He will be a Christian, will hold his marches from me;

être.of: Mais jo ne sai quels en est sis curages.»
être.tr: But I do not know what his intention is.»

être.of: L'un fut Basan e li altres Basilies;
être.tr: The one was Basan and the other Basile;

être.of: Apres iço i est Neimes venud;
être.tr: After this, Naimes came forward;

être.of: Saveir i ad, mais qu'il seit entendud.
être.tr: There is wisdom in it, provided it be heeded.

être.of: Li reis Marsilie est de guere vencud:
être.tr: King Marsile is defeated in war:

être.of: Respunt li reis: «Vos estes saives hom;
être.tr: The king replies: «You are a wise man;

être.of: «Vostre curages est mult pesmes e fiers;
être.tr: «Your temper is very harsh and fierce;

être.of: Li duze per mar i serunt jugez!»
être.tr: The twelve peers would be ill-judged [for it]!»

être.of: Turpins de Reins en est levet del renc,
être.tr: Turpin of Reims has risen from the ranks,

être.of: En cest païs avez estet set anz;
être.tr: In this land you have been seven years;

être.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Ço ert Guenes, mis parastre.»
être.tr: Rollant said this: «That will be Guenes, my stepfather.»

être.of: E li quens Guenes en fut mult anguisables;
être.tr: And the count Guenes was very distressed by it;

être.of: E est remes en sun blialt de palie.
être.tr: And he remained in his tunic of silk.

être.of: Tant par fut bels tuit si per l'en esguardent.
être.tr: He was so very handsome that all his peers gaze at him.

être.of: Ço set hom ben que jo sui tis parastres;
être.tr: One knows well that I am your stepfather;

être.of: Si li reis voelt, prez sui por vus le face.» AOI.
être.tr: If the king wishes, I am ready to do it for you.» AOI.

être.of: Tu n'ies mes hom ne jo ne sui tis sire.
être.tr: You are not my vassal, nor am I your lord.

être.of: Ço est Baldewin,» ço dit, «ki ert prozdoem.
être.tr: it is Baldwin,» he says, «who will be a worthy man.

être.of: Ne Oliver, por ço qu'il est si cumpainz;
être.tr: Nor Oliver, because he is his companion;

être.of: Mais li quens Guenes iloec ne volsist estre:
être.tr: But Count Ganelon would not wish to be there:

être.of: Dient Franceis: «Deus! que purrat ço estre?
être.tr: The Franks say: «God! what can this be?

être.of: En Tachebrun, sun destrer est munted;
être.tr: He has mounted Tachebrun, his warhorse;

être.of: Ki tuit li dient «Tant mare fustes, ber!
être.tr: Who all say to him: «What an evil hour was yours, baron!

être.of: En (la) cort al rei mult i avez ested,
être.tr: In the king's court you have long been,

être.of: Par Charlemagne n'er(cs) guariz ne tensez.
être.tr: Will not be protected nor defended by Charlemagne.

être.of: Que estrait estes de mult grant parented.»
être.tr: Since you are descended from a very great lineage.»

être.of: Mielz est que sul moerge que tant bon chevaler.
être.tr: Better that I alone die than so many good knights.

être.of: Asemblet s'est as sarrazins messag[es].
être.tr: He has joined the Saracen messengers;

être.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Merveilus hom est Charles,
être.tr: Blancandrin said: «Charles is a marvelous man,

être.of: Guenes respunt: «Itels est sis curages,
être.tr: Ganelon answers: «Such is his temper,

être.of: Jamais n'ert hume ki encuntre lui vaille.» AOI.
être.tr: Never will there be a man who can match himself against him.» AOI.

être.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Francs sunt mult gentilz home;
être.tr: Blancandrin said: «The Franks are very noble men;

être.of: Seit, ki l'ociet, tute pais puis avriúmes.» AOI.
être.tr: Were someone to slay him, we should then have full peace.» AOI.

être.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Mult est pesmes Rollant,
être.tr: Blancandrin said: «Roland is most fearsome,

être.of: Que il querreient, que Rollant fust ocis.
être.tr: That they would seek a way for Roland to be killed.

être.of: Esvolupet fut d'un palie alexandrin:
être.tr: It was wrapped in a silk cloth of Alexandria:

être.of: La fut li reis ki tute Espaigne tint;
être.tr: There was the king who held all Spain;

être.of: E dist al rei: «Salvez seiez de Mahun
être.tr: And said to the king: «Be saved by Mahomet

être.of: Ki est de France, si est mult riches hom:
être.tr: Who is from France, and he is a very mighty man:

être.of: Mais li quens Guenes se fut ben purpenset.
être.tr: But Count Ganelon had thought it over well.

être.of: E dist al rei: «Salvez seiez de Deu
être.tr: And said to the king: «May you be saved by God,

être.of: Pris e liez serez par poested;
être.tr: You will be seized and bound by force,

être.of: Al siege ad Ais en serez amenet,
être.tr: To the seat at Aix you will be brought,

être.of: Par jugement serez iloec finet;
être.tr: By judgment you will there be put to death;

être.of: Li reis Marsilies en fut mult esfreed.
être.tr: King Marsile was much frightened by this.

être.of: Un algier tint, ki d'or fut enpenet,
être.tr: He held a javelin, which was feathered with gold,

être.of: Ferir l'en volt, se n'en fust desturnet. AOI.
être.tr: He wished to strike him with it, had he not been prevented. AOI.

être.of: Si li ad dit: «Mult estes bele e clere!
être.tr: And said to it: «You are very fair and bright!

être.of: Ne por tut l'aveir, ki seit en cest païs,
être.tr: Nor for all the wealth that may be in this land,

être.of: Afublez est d'un mantel sabelin,
être.tr: He is wrapped in a sable mantle,

être.of: Ki fut cuvert d'une palie alexandrin.
être.tr: Which was covered with an Alexandrian silk.

être.of: Par poestet serez pris e liez;
être.tr: By force you will be taken and bound;

être.of: Menet serez . . . [tut] dreit ad Ais le siet:
être.tr: You will be led straight to the seat of Aix:

être.of: Getet serez sur un malvais sumer.
être.tr: You will be thrown upon a wretched pack-horse.

être.of: Marsilies fut esculurez de l'ire;
être.tr: Marsile was pale with rage;

être.of: Ço est de Basan e de sun frere Basilie,
être.tr: That is, of Basan and of his brother Basile,

être.of: Tant ad erret nen est dreiz que plus vivet.
être.tr: He has gone so far that it is not right that he live longer.

être.of: E Jurfaret, ki est ses filz e ses heirs,
être.tr: And Jurfaret, who is his son and his heir,

être.of: Einz demain noit en iert bele l'amendise.»
être.tr: before tomorrow night the reparation will be fair.»

être.of: Il est mult vielz, si ad sun tens uset;
être.tr: He is very old, and has worn out his time;

être.of: Quant ert il mais recreanz d'osteier?»
être.tr: When will he ever be weary of waging war?»

être.of: Guenes respunt: «Carles n'est mie tels.
être.tr: Ganelon replies: «Charles is not at all such a man.

être.of: N'est hom kil veit e conuistre le set
être.tr: There is no man who sees him and comes to know him

être.of: Que ço ne diet que l'emperere est ber.
être.tr: Who would not say that the emperor is noble.

être.of: De Carlemagne, ki est canuz e vielz!
être.tr: at Charlemagne, who is white-haired and old!

être.of: Quant ert il mais recreanz d'osteier?»
être.tr: when will he ever be weary of waging war?»

être.of: - «Ço n'iert,» dist Guenes: «tant cum vivet sis niés:
être.tr: - «That will not be,» said Ganelon, «as long as his nephew lives:

être.of: Mult par est proz sis cumpainz Oliver.
être.tr: His companion Oliver is very valiant indeed.

être.of: Soürs est Carles, que nuls home ne crent.» AOI.
être.tr: Charles is secure, for he fears no man.» AOI.

être.of: De Carlemagne, ki est canuz e blancs!
être.tr: About Charlemagne, who is hoary and white!

être.of: Par tantes teres est alet cunquerant,
être.tr: Through so many lands has he gone conquering,

être.of: Quant ier il mais d'osteier recreant?»
être.tr: When will he ever weary of waging war?»

être.of: - «Ço n'iert,» dist Guenes, «tant cum vivet Rollant:
être.tr: - «That will not be,» said Ganelon, «so long as Roland lives:

être.of: Mult par est proz Oliver, sis cumpainz;
être.tr: Very valiant is Oliver, his companion;

être.of: Soürs est Carlles, ne (cre) crent hume vivant.» AOI.
être.tr: Charles is secure, he fears no living man.» AOI.

être.of: Iert i sis nies, li quens Rollant, (...) ço crei,
être.tr: There will be his nephew, Count Roland, (...) so I believe,

être.of: Mort sunt li cunte, se est ki mei en creit.
être.tr: The counts are dead, if there is anyone who believes me in this.

être.of: Li reis serat as meillors porz de Sizer;
être.tr: The king will be at the great passes of Cize;

être.of: Iert i sis nies, li quens Rollant, li riches,
être.tr: There will be his nephew, Count Roland, the mighty one,

être.of: La gent de France iert blecee e blesmie;
être.tr: The host of France will be wounded and bruised;

être.of: Nel di por ço, des voz iert la martirie.
être.tr: I do not say it for that, the slaughter will be of yours.

être.of: De quel que seit Rollant n'estuertrat mie.
être.tr: From one or the other Roland will not escape.

être.of: - «Chi purreit faire, que Rollant i fust mort,
être.tr: - «Whoever could bring it about that Roland be killed there,

être.of: Cunseill n'est proz dunt hume n'est sevus.
être.tr: Counsel is not worthwhile of which no man can be sure.

être.of: La traïsun me jurrez de Rollant si illi est.»
être.tr: You will swear to me the betrayal of Roland, if it is so.»

être.of: Ço respunt Guenes: «Issi seit cum vos plaist!»
être.tr: Ganelon answered: «So be it, as it pleases you!»

être.of: La traïsun jurat, e si s'en est forsfait. AOI.
être.tr: He swore the betrayal, and thus made himself guilty of it. AOI.

être.of: La lei i fut Mahum e Tervagan.
être.tr: In it was the law of Mahomet and Tervagant.

être.of: Guenes respunt: «Ben seit vostre comant!» AOI.
être.tr: Ganelon answers: «May your command be a good one!» AOI.

être.of: - «Ben serat fait,» li quens Guenes respunt.
être.tr: - «It will be well done,» answers Count Ganelon.

être.of: - «Ben serat fait,» Guenes respundit.
être.tr: - «It will be well done,» Ganelon answered.

être.of: «L'aveir Carlun est il apareillez?»
être.tr: «Is Charles's tribute made ready?»

être.of: Si li ad dit: «Mult par ies ber e sage.
être.tr: And said to him: «You are most valiant and wise.

être.of: Jamais n'iert an, altretel ne vos face.
être.tr: Never will a year pass that I do not give you the same.

être.of: Guenes respunt: «Mei est vis que trop targe!»
être.tr: Ganelon answers: «It seems to me that I tarry too long!»

être.of: Pois est munted, entret en sun veiage. AOI.
être.tr: Then he mounted, and set out on his journey. AOI.

être.of: Venuz en est a la citet de Galne.
être.tr: He has come to the city of Galne.

être.of: Puis icel jur en fut cent anz deserte.
être.tr: After that day it lay deserted a hundred years.

être.of: Guenes li quens est venuz as herberges. AOI.
être.tr: Ganelon the count has come to the encampment. AOI.

être.of: Li empereres est par matin levet;
être.tr: The emperor has risen at morning;

être.of: Rollant i fut e Oliver li ber,
être.tr: Roland was there and Oliver the brave,

être.of: E dist al rei: «Salvez seiez de Deu!
être.tr: And said to the king: «May you be saved by God!

être.of: Se il fust vif, jo l'oüsse amenet.
être.tr: Had he been alive, I would have brought him.

être.of: Jointes ses mains iert vostre comandet;
être.tr: With joined hands he will be at your command;

être.of: Ço dist li reis: «Graciet en seit Deus!
être.tr: Thus said the king: «May God be thanked for it!

être.of: Tresvait le jur, la noit est aserie.
être.tr: The day passes, the night falls dark.

être.of: Sunjat qu'il eret al greignurs porz de Sizer,
être.tr: He dreamt that he was at the great passes of Cize,

être.of: Qu'il en France ert, a sa capele, ad Ais,
être.tr: That he was in France, at his chapel, at Aix,

être.of: Kar me jugez, ki ert en la rereguarde.»
être.tr: Now judge for me, who shall be in the rearguard.»

être.of: Si li ad dit: «Vos estes vifs diables.
être.tr: And said to him: «You are a living devil.

être.of: El cors vos est entree mortel rage.
être.tr: Into your body has entered a deadly rage.

être.of: E ki serat devant mei en l'ansguarde?»
être.tr: And who shall be before me in the vanguard?»

être.of: Que as espees ne seit einz eslegiet.»
être.tr: That shall not first be paid for at the point of swords.»

être.of: Quant ot Rollant, qu'il ert en la rereguarde,
être.tr: When Roland heard that he would be in the rearguard,

être.of: Anpres iço i est Neimes venud:
être.tr: After this Naimes came forward:

être.of: Li quens Rollant, il est mult irascut.
être.tr: Count Roland, he is very angry.

être.of: La rereguarde est jugee (...) sur lui:
être.tr: The rearguard has been assigned (...) to him:

être.of: Retenez les, ço est vostre salvement.»
être.tr: Keep them, this is your safeguard.»

être.of: Li quens Rollant est muntet el destrer.
être.tr: Count Roland has mounted his warhorse.

être.of: Venuz i est li riches dux Gaifiers.
être.tr: The mighty duke Gaifier has come there too.

être.of: «Hom sui Rollant, jo ne li dei faillir.»
être.tr: «I am Roland's man, I must not fail him.»

être.of: Enceis qu'en seient. VII.C. espees traites.
être.tr: Before seven hundred swords be drawn.

être.of: Halt sunt li pui e li val tenebrus,
être.tr: High are the peaks and shadowy the valleys,

être.of: Sur tuz les altres est Carles anguissus:
être.tr: Above all the others Charles is anguished:

être.of: Li .XII. per sunt remes en Espaigne.
être.tr: The Twelve Peers have remained in Spain.

être.of: Par Guenelun serat destruite France,
être.tr: Through Ganelon France will be destroyed,

être.of: Li nies Marsilie, il est venuz avant,
être.tr: The nephew of Marsile, he has come forward,

être.of: Dunez m'un feu, ço est le colp de Rollant;
être.tr: Grant me a fief: it is the blow against Roland;

être.of: Se Mahumet me voelt estre guarant,
être.tr: If Muhammad is willing to be my protector,

être.of: Las serat Carles, si recrerrunt si Franc;
être.tr: Charles will be weary, his Franks will give up;

être.of: Icil ert frere al rei Marsiliun:
être.tr: He was brother to King Marsile:

être.of: Il est juget que nus les ocirum.» AOI.
être.tr: It is judged that we shall kill them.» AOI.

être.of: Reis Corsalis, il est de l'altre part:
être.tr: King Corsalis, he is on the other side:

être.of: Barbarins est e mult de males arz.
être.tr: He is a Berber and full of evil arts.

être.of: Pur tut l'or Deu ne volt estre cuard [...]
être.tr: For all God's gold he would not be a coward [...]

être.of: Puis que il est sur un cheval muntet,
être.tr: Once he is mounted upon a horse,

être.of: De vasselage est il ben alosez;
être.tr: For valor he is well renowned;

être.of: Fust chrestiens, asez oüst barnet.
être.tr: Were he a Christian, he would have had ample baronage.

être.of: Devant Marsilie cil en est escriet:
être.tr: Before Marsile this man cries out:

être.of: Se truis Rollant, de mort serat finet,
être.tr: If I find Roland, he will be finished by death,

être.of: Carles li magnes velz est e redotez:
être.tr: Charles the Great is old and worn out:

être.of: Recreanz ert de sa guerre mener,
être.tr: He will be weary of waging his war,

être.of: Jamais n'ert jor que Carles ne se pleignet.» AOI.
être.tr: Never will there be a day that Charles does not lament it.» AOI.

être.of: D'altre part est Turgis de Turteluse:
être.tr: On the other side is Turgis of Tortelose:

être.of: Cil est uns quens, si est la citet sue.
être.tr: He is a count, and the city is his.

être.of: Se lui servez, l'onur del camp ert nostre.
être.tr: If you serve him, the honor of the field will be ours.

être.of: Veez m'espee, ki est e bone e lunge:
être.tr: See my sword, which is both good and long:

être.of: De l'altre part est Escremiz de Valterne:
être.tr: On the other side is Escremiz of Valterne:

être.of: Sarrazins est, si est sue la tere.
être.tr: He is a Saracen, and the land is his.

être.of: Li .XII. per tuit sunt jugez a perdre;
être.tr: The Twelve Peers are all condemned to perish;

être.of: Franceis murrunt e France en ert deserte,
être.tr: The French shall die and France will be left desolate,

être.of: D'altre part est uns paiens, Esturganz;
être.tr: On the other side is a pagan, Esturganz;

être.of: Estramariz i est, un soens cumpainz:
être.tr: Estramariz is there too, a companion of his:

être.of: Cil sunt felun, traïtur suduiant.
être.tr: These are felons, treacherous traitors.

être.of: Noz espees sunt bones e trenchant;
être.tr: Our swords are good and sharp;

être.of: Franceis murrunt, Carles en ert dolent.
être.tr: The French will die, Charles will be grieved by it.

être.of: Pur sa beltet dames li sunt amies:
être.tr: For his beauty the ladies are his friends:

être.of: Li .XII. per sunt remes en martirie.
être.tr: The Twelve Peers are left there in martyrdom.

être.of: Veez m'espee, ki d'or est enheldie:
être.tr: Behold my sword, which is hilted with gold:

être.of: Jo vos plevis qu'en vermeill sanc ert mise.
être.tr: I pledge to you that it will be set in crimson blood.

être.of: Franceis murrunt e France en ert hunie;
être.tr: The French will die and France will be shamed by it;

être.of: Jamais n'ert jurn qu'il n'en ait doel e ire.
être.tr: Never will there be a day that he does not have grief and wrath from it.

être.of: De l'altre part est Chernubles de Munigre;
être.tr: On the other side is Chernuble of Munigre;

être.of: Icele tere, ço dit, dun il esteit,
être.tr: That land, he says, from which he came,

être.of: Piere n'i ad que tute ne seit neire.
être.tr: There is no stone there that is not entirely black.

être.of: Franceis murrunt e France en ert deserte.»
être.tr: The French shall die and France shall be left desolate.»

être.of: Tuit li plusur en sunt (saraguzeis) dublez en treis,
être.tr: Most of them are triple-meshed,

être.of: Clers fut li jurz e bels fut li soleilz:
être.tr: Clear was the day and fair was the sun:

être.of: Sunent mil grailles por ço que plus bel seit:
être.tr: A thousand trumpets sound so that it may be more splendid:

être.of: Granz est la noise, si l'oïrent Franceis.
être.tr: Great is the din, and the French heard it.

être.of: Ben devuns ci estre pur nostre rei:
être.tr: We must indeed stand here for our king:

être.of: Que malvaise cançun de nus chantet ne seit!
être.tr: So that no evil song be sung about us!

être.of: Malvaise essample n'en serat, ja de mei.» AOI.
être.tr: An evil example will never come from me.» AOI.

être.of: Oliver est desur un pui haut muntez,
être.tr: Oliver has climbed up onto a high hill,

être.of: «Mis parrastre est, ne voeill que mot en suns.»
être.tr: «He is my stepfather, I do not wish that you say a word of it.»

être.of: Oliver est desur un pui muntet;
être.tr: Olivier has climbed up onto a hill;

être.of: E Sarrazins, ki tant sunt asemblez.
être.tr: And the Saracens, who are gathered in such numbers.

être.of: Luisent cil elme, ki ad or sunt gemmez,
être.tr: The helmets gleam, which are gemmed with gold,

être.of: E lui meïsme en est mult esguaret.
être.tr: And he himself is much troubled by it.

être.of: Cum il einz pout, del pui est avalet,
être.tr: As fast as he could, he came down from the hill,

être.of: Cil devant sunt .C. milie ad escuz
être.tr: Those in front number a hundred thousand with shields,

être.of: Bataille avrez, unches mais tel ne fut.
être.tr: You will have a battle, never was there such a one.

être.of: El camp estez, que ne seium vencuz!
être.tr: Hold the field, that we be not vanquished!»

être.of: Sanglant en ert li branz entresqu'a l'or.
être.tr: bloody will be the blade right up to the hilt of gold.

être.of: Jo vos plevis, tuz sunt jugez a mort.» AOI.
être.tr: I pledge you, all are condemned to death.» AOI.

être.of: Que mi parent pur mei seient blasmet
être.tr: That my kinsmen be blamed because of me

être.of: Felun paien mar i sunt asemblez:
être.tr: The felon pagans have gathered here to their misfortune:

être.of: Jo vos plevis, tuz sunt a mort livrez.» AOI.
être.tr: I pledge you, they are all delivered up to death.» AOI.

être.of: Si l'orrat Carles, ki est as porz passant.
être.tr: Charles will hear it, he who is passing through the mountain passes.

être.of: «Que ço seit dit de nul hume vivant,
être.tr: «That it be said by any living man,

être.of: Ne pur paien, que ja seie cornant!
être.tr: Nor for any pagan, that I should ever sound the horn!

être.of: Quant jo serai en la bataille grant
être.tr: When I am in the great battle

être.of: Franceis sunt bon, si ferrunt vassalment,
être.tr: The French are good, they will strike valiantly,

être.of: Cuverz en sunt li val e les muntaignes
être.tr: The valleys and the mountains are covered with them,

être.of: Granz sunt les oz de cele gent estrange;
être.tr: Great are the hosts of that foreign people;

être.of: Respunt Rollant: «Mis talenz en est graigne.
être.tr: Roland answers: «My desire is the greater for it.

être.of: Rollant est proz e Oliver est sage;
être.tr: Roland is brave and Oliver is wise;

être.of: Puis que il sunt as chevals e as armes,
être.tr: Once they are on their horses and at their arms,

être.of: Bon sunt li cunte e lur paroles haltes.
être.tr: Good are the counts, and their words are lofty.

être.of: Cist nus sunt pres, mais trop nus est loinz Carles.
être.tr: These are close to us, but Charles is too far from us.

être.of: Fust i li reis, n'i oüssum damage.
être.tr: Were the king here, we would have no harm.

être.of: Veeir poez, dolente est la rereguarde;
être.tr: You can see, the rearguard is to be pitied;

être.of: Mal seit del coer ki el piz se cuardet!
être.tr: Cursed be the heart that turns coward in the breast!

être.of: Par nos í ert e li colps e li caples.» AOI.
être.tr: By us will be both the blows and the fighting.» AOI.

être.of: Quant Rollant veit que la bataille serat,
être.tr: When Roland sees that the battle will take place,

être.of: (E purrunt dire) que ele fut a noble vassal.»
être.tr: (And they will be able to say) that it belonged to a noble vassal.»

être.of: D'altre part est li arcevesques Turpin,
être.tr: On the other side is Archbishop Turpin,

être.of: Bataille avrez, vos en estes tuz fiz,
être.tr: You will have battle, of this you are all certain,

être.of: Ben sunt asols e quites de lur pecchez,
être.tr: They are well absolved and released from their sins,

être.of: Puis sunt muntez sur lur curanz destrers.
être.tr: Then they have mounted their swift war-horses.

être.of: Adobez sunt a lei de chevalers
être.tr: They are equipped in the manner of knights

être.of: E de bataille sunt tuit apareillez.
être.tr: And are all made ready for battle.

être.of: As porz d'Espaigne en est passet Rollant
être.tr: Through the passes of Spain Roland has gone by

être.of: Portet ses armes, mult li sunt avenanz,
être.tr: He bears his arms, which become him well,

être.of: Cil ki la sunt ne funt mie a blasmer.
être.tr: Those who are there are not at all to be blamed.

être.of: Pur deu vos pri, ben seiez purpensez
être.tr: For God's sake I pray you, be well resolved

être.of: A icest mot sunt Franceis escriet.
être.tr: At this word the Franks cried out.

être.of: Fols est li reis ki vos laissat as porz.
être.tr: Foolish is the king who left you at the passes.

être.of: «Ultre culvert! Carles n'est mie fol,
être.tr: «Wretched coward! Charles is not at all foolish,

être.of: Ferez i, Francs, nostre est li premers colps!
être.tr: Strike, Franks, the first blows are ours!

être.of: Un duc i est, si ad num Falsaron:
être.tr: There is a duke there, named Falsaron:

être.of: Icil er[t] frere al rei Marsiliun;
être.tr: He was the brother of King Marsile;

être.of: Envers Franceis est mult cuntrarius:
être.tr: Toward the Franks he is most hostile:

être.of: - «Munjoie!» escriet, ço est l'enseigne Carlun. AOI.
être.tr: - «Monjoie!» he cries, that is Charles's war-cry. AOI.

être.of: Uns reis i est, si ad num Corsablix:
être.tr: A king is there, and his name is Corsablix:

être.of: Barbarins est, d'un estra[n]ge païs.
être.tr: He is a Barbary man, from a strange land.

être.of: Cels ki ci sunt devum aveir mult vil;
être.tr: Those who are here we should hold very cheap;

être.of: Ja pur Charles n'i ert un sul guarit:
être.tr: Never, for all of Charles, shall a single one be saved:

être.of: Or est le jur qu'els estuvrat murir.»
être.tr: Now is the day when they must die.»

être.of: Par grant vertut si l 'est alet ferir.
être.tr: With great force he has gone to strike him.

être.of: Carles, mi sire, nus est guarant tuz dis;
être.tr: Charles, my lord, is our protector always;

être.of: Cist premer colp est nostre, Deu mercit!»
être.tr: This first blow is ours, thanks be to God!»

être.of: Dist Oliver: «Gente est nostre bataille!»
être.tr: Oliver said: «Noble is our battle!»

être.of: L'escut li freinst, ki est a flurs e ad ór,
être.tr: He shattered his shield, which is wrought with flowers and gold,

être.of: Li bons osbercs ne li est guarant prod,
être.tr: His good hauberk is no real protection to him,

être.of: Dist l'arcevesque: «Cist colp est de baron!»
être.tr: Said the archbishop: «This blow is a baron's!»

être.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Cist colp est de produme!»
être.tr: Roland says this: «This blow is a worthy man's!»

être.of: Apres li dist: «Turnet estes a perdre!» AOI.
être.tr: Afterward he says to him: «You are doomed to perish!» AOI.

être.of: Des .XII. pers li .X. en sunt ocis;
être.tr: Of the Twelve Peers, ten of them are slain;

être.of: Ço est Chernubles e li quens Margariz.
être.tr: These are Chernuble and the count Margariz.

être.of: Margariz est mult vaillant chevalers,
être.tr: Margariz is a very valiant knight,

être.of: La bataille est merveilluse e cumune.
être.tr: The battle is marvelous and general.

être.of: Le blanc osberc, dunt la maile est menue,
être.tr: The white hauberk, whose mail is fine,

être.of: Enz en la sele, ki est a or batue;
être.tr: Right into the saddle, which is beaten with gold;

être.of: Par tel glutun n'ert bataille oi vencue.»
être.tr: By such a villain a battle will not be won today.»

être.of: - «Munjoie!» escriet, ço est l'enseigne Carle. AOI.
être.tr: - «Munjoie!» he cries, that is Charles's battle-cry. AOI.

être.of: Sa hanste est frait, n'en ad que un trunçun,
être.tr: His lance is broken, he has but one stump of it,

être.of: L'escut li freint, ki est ad or e a flur,
être.tr: He shatters his shield, which is adorned with gold and flowers,

être.of: U est vostre espee, ki Halteclere ad num?
être.tr: Where is your sword, which is named Halteclere?

être.of: D'or est li helz e de cristal li punz.»
être.tr: The hilt is of gold and the pommel of crystal.»

être.of: La bone sele, ki a ór est gemmee,
être.tr: The good saddle, which is jeweled with gold,

être.of: De tutes parz est «Munjo[i]e» escriee. AOI.
être.tr: On all sides «Munjoie» is cried. AOI.

être.of: Liquels d'els dous en fut li plus isnels.
être.tr: Which of the two was the swifter.

être.of: Esprieres icil fut filz Burdel,
être.tr: Esprieris, this one was the son of Burdel,

être.of: L'encanteür ki ja fut en enfer:
être.tr: The enchanter who had already been in hell:

être.of: Respunt Rollant: «Vencut est le culvert.
être.tr: Roland answers: «The villain is vanquished.

être.of: Oliver, frere, itels colps me sunt bel!»
être.tr: Oliver, brother, such blows please me!»

être.of: La bataille est aduree endementres.
être.tr: The battle is hard-fought meanwhile.

être.of: El plait ad Ais en fut juget a prendre,
être.tr: At the trial at Aix he was condemned to be seized,

être.of: La bataille est merveilluse e pesant;
être.tr: The battle is marvelous and hard;

être.of: Dient plusor: «Ço est li definement,
être.tr: Many say: «This is the ending,

être.of: La fin del secle ki nus est en present.»
être.tr: The end of the world that is upon us.»

être.of: Ço est li granz dulors por la mort de Rollant.
être.tr: This is the great mourning for the death of Roland.

être.of: Paien sunt morz a millers e a fuls:
être.tr: The pagans are dead by the thousands and in droves:

être.of: Rollant dist: «Nostre hume sunt mult proz:
être.tr: Roland said: «Our men are very valiant:

être.of: Il est escrit en la Geste Francor
être.tr: It is written in the Geste of the Franks

être.of: Grant est la noise par tute la contree.
être.tr: Great is the din throughout the whole country.

être.of: La traïsun ne poet estre celee;
être.tr: The treason cannot be concealed;

être.of: Male chançun n'en deit estre cantee.» AOI.
être.tr: Let no evil song be sung about it.» AOI.

être.of: Issi est neirs cume peiz ki est demise;
être.tr: He is as black as melted pitch;

être.of: Por ço est drud al felun rei Marsilie;
être.tr: For this he is a favorite of the felon king Marsile;

être.of: Mielz est mult que jo l'alge ocire.
être.tr: It is far better that I go and slay him.

être.of: Ço ert uns reis qu'l ocist en Denemarche.
être.tr: He was a king whom he slew in Denmark.

être.of: Li destrers est e curanz e aates,
être.tr: The warhorse is both swift and nimble,

être.of: Beste nen est nule ki encontre lui alge.
être.tr: There is no beast that can match it.

être.of: En l'arcevesque est ben la croce salve.»
être.tr: In the archbishop the crozier is well safe.»

être.of: De tutes parz en sunt cuvert li camp;
être.tr: On all sides the fields are covered with them;

être.of: Les .XII. pers, qu'il lor seient guarant.
être.tr: The Twelve Peers, that they might be their protectors.

être.of: Pur Deu vos pri que ne seiez fuiant,
être.tr: For God's sake I pray you not to flee,

être.of: Asez est mielz que moerium cumbatant.
être.tr: It is much better that we die fighting.

être.of: Ultre cest jurn ne serum plus vivant;
être.tr: Beyond this day we shall be alive no longer;

être.of: Mais d'une chose vos soi jo ben guarant:
être.tr: But of one thing I am your good warrant:

être.of: De la citet l'une meitet est sue:
être.tr: Of the city one half is his:

être.of: Ço est Climborins, ki pas ne fut produme.
être.tr: He is Climborin, who was no worthy man at all.

être.of: Plus est isnels que esprever ne arunde.
être.tr: Swifter is it than sparrowhawk or swallow.

être.of: Apres escriet: «Cist sunt bon a (o)[c]unfundre!
être.tr: Then he cries out: «These are good to destroy!

être.of: «Sire cumpainz, ja est morz Engeler;
être.tr: «Sir companion, Engelier is now dead;

être.of: Tient Halteclere, sanglent en est l'acer,
être.tr: Grasps Halteclere, its steel is bloody,

être.of: Cil ne sunt proz ja mais pur guerreier.
être.tr: They will never again be of use for waging war.

être.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Mis cumpainz est irez!
être.tr: Roland says: «My companion is enraged!

être.of: D'altre part est un paien, Valdabrun:
être.tr: On the other side is a pagan, Valdabrun:

être.of: Sire est par mer de .IIII.C. drodmunz;
être.tr: He is lord by sea of four hundred dromons;

être.of: Plus est isnels que nen est uns falcuns.
être.tr: Swifter is it than a falcon is.

être.of: Dient paien(t) [...]: «Cist colp nus est mult fort!»
être.tr: The pagans say: «This blow is very hard on us!»

être.of: Devers vos est li orguilz e li torz.» AOI.
être.tr: On your side is the arrogance and the wrong.» AOI.

être.of: Ço est Malquiant, le filz al rei Malcud.
être.tr: This is Malquiant, the son of King Malcud.

être.of: Si guarnement sunt tut a or batud;
être.tr: His trappings are all beaten with gold;

être.of: Beste nen est ki poisset curre a lui.
être.tr: There is no beast that can run as fast as it.

être.of: Morz est li quens, de sun tens n'i ad plus.
être.tr: The count is dead, of his time there is no more.

être.of: Dient Franceis: «Barun, tant mare fus!»
être.tr: The Franks say: «Baron, how ill-fated you were!»

être.of: De l'altre part est un paien, Grandonies,
être.tr: On the other side is a pagan, Grandonie,

être.of: Plus est isnels que n'est oisel ki volet;
être.tr: Swifter is it than a bird that flies;

être.of: Ki quel cumpert, venuz en sunt ensemble.
être.tr: Whoever pays for it, they have come together.

être.of: Grandonie fut e prozdom e vaillant
être.tr: Grandonie was a worthy man and valiant

être.of: La bataille est e merveillose e grant.
être.tr: The battle is both marvelous and great.

être.of: La (la) b[at]aille est m[erv]eilluse e hastive.
être.tr: The battle is marvelous and swift.

être.of: Sur tute gent est la tue hardie.»
être.tr: Above every people your boldness stands.»

être.of: Li arcevesque est mult bon chevaler,
être.tr: The archbishop is a very good knight,

être.of: Dur sunt li colps e li caples est grefs;
être.tr: Hard are the blows and the fighting is grievous;

être.of: Il est escrit es cartres e es brefs,
être.tr: It is written in the charters and in the records,

être.of: As quatre [es]turs lor est avenut ben;
être.tr: In the four assaults it went well for them;

être.of: Li quint apres lor est pesant e gref.
être.tr: The fifth, after, is heavy and grievous for them.

être.of: Tuz sunt ocis cist Franceis chevalers,
être.tr: All are slain, these French knights,

être.of: E! reis, amis, que vos ici nen estes?
être.tr: Ah! king, friend, why are you not here?

être.of: Mielz voeill murir que hunte nus seit retraite.» AOI.
être.tr: I would rather die than that shame be reported of us.» AOI.

être.of: Si l'orrat Carles, ki est as porz passant.
être.tr: So Charles, who is passing through the passes, will hear it.

être.of: Dist Oliver: «Vergoigne sereit grant
être.tr: Oliver said: «It would be a great shame

être.of: Se vos cornez, n'ert mie hardement.
être.tr: If you sound the horn, it will be no act of valor.

être.of: Ço dit Rollant: «Forz est nostre bataille;
être.tr: Roland says: «Our battle is fierce;

être.of: Dist Oliver: «Ne sereit vasselage!
être.tr: Oliver said: «That would be no act of valor!

être.of: Si fust li reis, n'i oüsum damage.
être.tr: Had the king been here, we would have suffered no harm.

être.of: Cil ki la sunt n'en deivent aveir blasme.»
être.tr: Those who are over there ought not bear blame for it.»

être.of: Kar vasselage par sens nen est folie;
être.tr: For bravery with good sense is not folly;

être.of: Franceis sunt morz par vostre legerie.
être.tr: The French are dead through your rashness.

être.of: Sem(e) creïsez, venuz i fust mi sire;
être.tr: Had you trusted me, my lord would have come here;

être.of: U pris ú mort i fust li reis Marsilie.
être.tr: Captured or killed would have been King Marsile.

être.of: N'ert mais tel home des qu'a Deu juïse.
être.tr: There will be no such man until God's Judgment.

être.of: Vos i murrez e France en ert (...) huníe.
être.tr: You will die here and France will be shamed by it.

être.of: Einz le vesp(e)re mult ert gref la departie.» AOI.
être.tr: Before evening the parting will be most grievous.» AOI.

être.of: Mais nepurquant si est il asez melz:
être.tr: But nevertheless it is much better so:

être.of: Halt sunt li pui e la voiz est mult lunge,
être.tr: High are the peaks and the sound carries very far,

être.of: Karles l'entent, ki est as porz passant.
être.tr: Charles hears it, who is passing through the gorges.

être.of: Unc nel sunast se ne fust (cu)cumbatant.»
être.tr: He would never sound it unless he were fighting.»

être.of: Guenes respunt: «De bataille est il nient!
être.tr: Ganelon answers: «There is no battle at all!

être.of: Ja estes veilz e fluriz e blancs;
être.tr: You are already old and white-haired and hoary;

être.of: Ço est merveille que Deus le soefret tant.
être.tr: It is a marvel that God suffers him so long.

être.of: Pur cel le fist ne fust a[pa]rissant.
être.tr: So that it should not be apparent.

être.of: Tere Major mult est loinz ça devant.» AOI.
être.tr: The Great Land is very far on ahead.» AOI.

être.of: De sun cervel rumput en est li temples.
être.tr: The temple of his brain is burst.

être.of: «Se veïssum Rollant einz qu'il fust mortz,
être.tr: «If only we might see Roland before he is dead,

être.of: Esclargiz est li vespres e li jurz.
être.tr: The evening and the day grow bright.

être.of: E cil escuz, ki ben sunt peinz a flurs,
être.tr: And those shields, which are finely painted with flowers,

être.of: E de Rollant sunt en grant poür.
être.tr: And for Roland they are in great fear.

être.of: (Morz est Turpin le guerreier Charlun)
être.tr: (Turpin, the warrior of Charles, is dead)

être.of: Halt sunt li pui e tenebrus e grant, AOI.
être.tr: High are the peaks and shadowy and great,

être.of: Demurent trop, n'i poedent estre a tens. AOI.
être.tr: They tarry too long, they cannot be there in time. AOI.

être.of: Que il ne sunt a Rollant le cataigne,
être.tr: That they are not beside Roland the captain,

être.of: Si est blecet, ne quit que anme i remaigne.
être.tr: He is so wounded, I do not think a soul remains in him.

être.of: Tere de France mult estes dulz païs
être.tr: Land of France, you are a most sweet country,

être.of: Li quens Rollant el champ est repairet:
être.tr: Count Roland has returned to the field:

être.of: Jamais n'iert home plus se voeillet venger.
être.tr: Never will a man be more eager to take revenge.

être.of: En bataille deit estre forz e fiers,
être.tr: In battle he ought to be strong and fierce,

être.of: Einz deit monie estre en un de cez mustiers,
être.tr: Rather he ought to be a monk in one of these minsters,

être.of: Pur ço sunt Francs si fiers cume leuns.
être.tr: for this reason the Franks are as fierce as lions.

être.of: Icil ert sire de Belne e de Digun,
être.tr: who was lord of Beaune and of Dijon.

être.of: Li quens Rollant ne li est guaires loign;
être.tr: Count Roland is not very far from him;

être.of: Icil ert filz al rei Marsiliun.
être.tr: who was son of King Marsile.

être.of: De ço qui calt? Se fuit s'en est Marsilies,
être.tr: What does it matter? Marsile has fled,

être.of: Remes i est sis uncles, Marganices,
être.tr: His uncle Marganice has remained there,

être.of: E sunt ensemble plus de cinquante milie.
être.tr: And together they number more than fifty thousand.

être.of: Mais tut seit fel cher ne se vende primes!
être.tr: But cursed be whoever does not sell himself dearly first!

être.of: Que dulce France par nus ne seit hunie!
être.tr: Let sweet France not be dishonored through us!

être.of: Ki plus sunt neirs que nen est arrement,
être.tr: Who are blacker than ink is,

être.of: Tort nos ad fait: nen est dreiz qu'il s'en lot,
être.tr: He has wronged us: it is not right that he should boast of it,

être.of: Oliver sent que a mort est ferut.
être.tr: Oliver feels that he is wounded to death.

être.of: Tient Halteclere, dunt li acer fut bruns,
être.tr: He grips Halteclere, whose steel was burnished,

être.of: N'en vanteras el regne dunt tu fus
être.tr: Will you boast, in the realm from which you came,

être.of: Oliver sent qu'il est a mort nasfret.
être.tr: Oliver feels that he is wounded to death.

être.of: De lui venger ja mais ne li ert lez.
être.tr: Never will it be pleasant for him to avenge himself.

être.of: A grant dulor ermes hoi desevrez.» AOI.
être.tr: With great sorrow shall we today be parted.» AOI.

être.of: Teint fut e pers, desculuret e pale.
être.tr: It was wan and livid, discolored and pale.

être.of: Sire cumpainz, mar fut vostre barnage!
être.tr: Lord companion, ill-fated was your valor!

être.of: Jamais n'iert hume ki tun cors cuntrevaillet.
être.tr: Never will there be a man who equals your worth.

être.of: E Oliver ki est a mort nasfret:
être.tr: And Oliver, who is wounded to death:

être.of: Tant ad seinet (ki) li oil li sunt trublet;
être.tr: He has bled so much that his eyes are clouded;

être.of: Ja est ço Rollant, ki tant vos soelt amer!
être.tr: This is indeed Roland, who is so wont to love you!

être.of: Morz est li quens, que plus ne se demuret.
être.tr: The count is dead, he lingers no longer.

être.of: Or veit Rollant que mort est sun ami,
être.tr: Now Roland sees that his friend is dead,

être.of: «Sire cumpaign, tant mar fustes hardiz!
être.tr: «Lord companion, what an evil hour your boldness brought you!

être.of: Ensemble avum estet e anz e dis;
être.tr: Together we have been for years and days;

être.of: Quant tu es mor[t], dulur est que jo vif!»
être.tr: Since you are dead, it is grief that I live!»

être.of: Afermet est a ses estreus d'or fin:
être.tr: He is held fast in his stirrups of fine gold:
